**Tariff Wars. The Working Class Tax Disguised as Trade Policy.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Trade & Tariffs/`. Liberation Day tariffs ($1.2T imports, 27% effective rate), Supreme Court February 2026 ruling striking IEEPA tariffs, $28B farmer bailout (top 10% got more than bottom 80%), USMCA as NAFTA rebrand, consumer cost $1,000 to $1,500 per household, 98,000 manufacturing jobs lost, Nucor benefiting from 50% tariff floor. Skinny repeal failure 49 to 51 (July 28, 2017, 1:30 AM McCain thumbs down), individual mandate zeroed via tax bill, enrollment drop 9.2M, Operation Warp Speed $18B public/$90B private profit, RFK Jr. HHS ($3.2M wellness industry ties, 17 ACIP members fired, $500M mRNA contracts terminated), One Big Beautiful Bill Medicaid $326B cuts, 10M projected uninsured by 2034. Miriam Adelson $215M+ (2024), Sheldon $273M lifetime, Jerusalem embassy May 14 2018 (Adelson front row), Golan Heights March 25 2019, Abraham Accords (UAE/Bahrain/Sudan/Morocco with side deals documented), JCPOA withdrawal, Soleimani strike, Iran war 2026 ($12B spent, $200B requested), Kushner Affinity Partners $2B Saudi PIF, $110B Saudi arms deal, Khashoggi "maybe he did maybe he didn't", AIPAC $126.9M 2024 cycle. ADM $3.65M lobbying, ~$4.9M total political spending. Farm Bureau $1.31M lobbying. NCBA $1.23M PAC. Ethanol vs oil refinery 50 to 1 lobbying asymmetry ($72M oil vs ~$1.37M ethanol). SNAP subsidy contradiction. $186B SNAP cuts while farm subsidies preserved. Top 10% farms get 65% of subsidies ($108K average) while bottom 80% get $1,180.

**Judicial. Donors and Backers.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Judicial/`. Leo network $2.2B total. $600M+ raised 2014 to 2020. Barre Seid $1.6B single donation (largest political advocacy donation in history, avoided ~$400M capital gains). 7 entity financial empire table (Federalist Society $45M+/year from Marble, 85 Fund $60M+/year, JCN/Concord Fund $48.1M 2023 to 2024, CRC Advisors $33M/year to Leo personally). Wellspring Committee $23.5M to JCN 2016, $14.8M 2017. Anonymous $17M checks. Harlan Crow $4.75M+ to Thomas over 30 years. Paul Singer $100K Alaska trip to Alito, 7 to 1 ruling, $2.4B settlement (24,000 to 1 ROI). Koch network overlap. Chevron deference elimination as highest ROI in entire donor class portfolio.

**Crypto and Tech. Donors and Backers.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Crypto/`. Fairshake PAC $195M+ 2024 (44% of all corporate election money, 48 for 48 win rate). Coinbase $75M+ to Fairshake. Ripple $40M+. Crypto inaugural $18M+ (Ripple $5M, Robinhood $2M, plus 5 companies at $1M each). Musk $277M+ (America PAC $239M, Senate Leadership $10M, MAHA $3M). Thiel $32.6M 2022 plus $1.7M+ 2024, Palantir contracts doubled $541M to $970.5M. Andreessen/Horowitz $5M combined. Winklevoss twins $10.1M combined. Sacks crypto czar conflict (449 AI companies, $200M divestments but still exposed). Trump family $1.4B from WLFI ($460M+ H1 2025, 75% entitlement through DT Marks DEFI LLC), UAE Royal Family $500M for 49% WLFI stake 4 days before inauguration. $TRUMP meme coin. 813,294 wallets lost $2B in 19 days. $100M fees to Trump entities. SEC reversals. Coinbase dismissed (10 days after EO), Binance dismissed with prejudice, Ripple penalty reduced 60% ($125M to $50M). Blockchain Association $1.97M lobbying. Stand With Crypto 2.6M members.

### Part 1. Domain Expansion (10 New Sub-Notes)
### DOGE expanded from 1 to 4 notes.
**The Ramaswamy 32 Days. DOGE Co-Lead to Ohio Governor Candidate.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/DOGE/`. November 12 2024 to January 20 2025 (32 days). H-1B controversy. $2.4B net worth. Strive Asset Management. Ohio governor campaign February 24 2025. Zero implemented policies. Musk ran everything. Pilot Program analytical pattern on resume line politics.

**The Federal Workforce Gutting. 320,000 Jobs and the Services That Disappeared.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/DOGE/`. 280K to 320K positions eliminated. Fork in the road email to ~2M workers. Schedule F reclassification. Agency by agency cuts (USAID 83%, EPA 4,000+, Education half of 4,100, CFPB gutted, FDA ~3,500, VA/SSA/IRS). AFGE, NTEU, NFFE union responses. Villain Framing on "bloated government" vs agencies that constrain capital.

**The Contractor Beneficiaries. Who Replaced the Civil Servants.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/DOGE/`. SpaceX $19.8B+, Starlink $1.8B+, Tesla $9.2B+, Palantir $541M to $970.5M. 7 agencies Musk cut that regulate his companies (SEC, EPA, FAA, FTC, CFPB, NLRB, OSHA). Koch template (50 year project). Federal spending UP 5% despite 320K fewer employees. Two Audience Problem on privatization.

### Immigration expanded from 1 to 4 notes.
**The Wall Contractor Money. Who Got Paid to Build What.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Immigration/`. $14B total first term. $9.9B diverted from military construction. 52 miles new vs 458 claimed. Fisher Sand and Gravel (Tommy Fisher personally lobbied Trump, $2B+ first term, $8B+ second term, 2.5ft foundations vs 7 to 8ft standard). 4 contractors 88% of contracts. 109 eminent domain lawsuits, 285 acres seized.

**Family Separation. Zero Tolerance and Who Profited.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Immigration/`. Sessions April 6 2018. 5,300 to 5,500 children separated. Southwest Key $458M FY2018, $3B lifetime, $775 per child per day. ~1,400 children never reunited. MVM Inc $72M (CIA contractor). Stephen Miller architect returned as Deputy CoS. November 2025 ORR halt reunifications.

**Visa Programs. Anti-Immigration Rhetoric Meets Tech Donor Needs.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Immigration/`. H-1B $100K fee September 2025 (contradicts Musk H-1B dependence). H-2A wage cuts up to $7 per hour ($2B worker losses). EB-5 Trump/Kushner usage (Bay Street $50M). Gold Card $5M replacement. Two Audience Problem on enforcement vs visa programs.

### Surveillance expanded from 1 to 3 notes.
**Section 702. The Warrantless Surveillance Expansion.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Surveillance/`. RISAA signed April 20 2024. House 273 to 147, Senate 60 to 34. Warrant amendment failed 212 to 212 (one vote). Trump "Kill FISA" then signed reauthorization. Google FISA requests +594%, Meta +2,171%, Apple +274%. Palantir donor connection. Expires April 2026.

**Signalgate. The Yemen Strike Chat and the Security Theater.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Surveillance/`. March 11 to 13 2025. "Houthi PC small group" 19 members. Goldberg accidentally added. F-18, MQ-9, Tomahawk timing discussed on commercial app. Pentagon IG found "SECRET" labeled info. Hegseth second personal chat with wife and brother. Waltz fired May 1 (101 days, second shortest NSA tenure). Full member table. Two Audience Problem on four audiences, zero consequences.

### Economic Policy expanded from 2 to 4 notes.
**The Billionaire Cabinet. Self-Dealing as Governance.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Economic Policy/`. Combined net worth $15B+. Musk ($40B federal entanglements). Burgum (Continental leases, $250K Hamm donation, 5,742 permits). Lutnick (5% Tether equity, $132B reserves custody). Bessent ($521M, missed divestiture deadline). McMahon (AFPI 86% of first 100 days EOs, 73 alumni placed). Vought (Project 2025 author to executor). Sacks (449 AI companies, 50/50 government/private).

**The One Big Beautiful Bill. Who Won and Who Paid.** `topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Economic Policy/`. Signed July 4 2025. TCJA permanent (corporate 21%, pass-through, estate tax). Working class provisions expire 2028 (tips, overtime, car loan). $911B Medicaid cuts. $536B Medicare automatic cuts through 2034. $186B SNAP cuts (largest in history). $18B fossil fuel incentives. Chamber $81M lobbying. Koch $500M+ per cycle. Genuine Win + Structural Limit on temporary vs permanent architecture.

### Session 33 — March 22, 2026

**Context.** Standalone build task: Alexander Acosta master profile as critical node in Trump-Epstein-Cabinet network. Acosta bridges Trump administration to Jeffrey Epstein Network vault. Task: create developed-level profile with full Epstein plea deal documentation, Labor Secretary deregulation analysis, and institutional protection framework.

### What was built:
**[[_Alexander Acosta Master Profile|_Alexander Acosta Master Profile]]** (Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/_Alexander Acosta Master Profile.md) — 1 file:
- Master profile: Trump's Secretary of Labor (April 2017-July 2019). US Attorney Southern District of Florida (2005-2009) who negotiated the 2008 non-prosecution agreement that freed Jeffrey Epstein. 30+ identified victims. Epstein served 13 months in county jail with work release. All co-conspirators shielded. When Epstein re-arrested July 2019, Acosta resigned to prevent scandal. Case study in institutional protection and career advancement through complicity.
- Core sections: Who He Is (biography, Harvard Law, Kirkland and Ellis, FIU dean); The Central Thesis (institutional protection framework); The Core Contradiction (civil rights prosecutor who enabled trafficking); The Epstein Plea Deal (2008 NPA structure, what Epstein got vs. victims got, DOJ review finding "poor judgment" but no misconduct); The Labor Secretary Appointment (Trump nomination, 60-38 confirmation, deregulation agenda); Labor Department policies (joint employment rollback, overtime opposition, gig economy deregulation, apprenticeship expansion outside union model); The Resignation (July 2019, Epstein re-arrest catalyst, bipartisan pressure, final statement framing resignation as distraction minimization); Donor connections and FIU career (institutional advancement pattern, Florida political network, Trump connection); Analytical Patterns (institutional survivor, villain framing, two-audience problem, genuine win + structural limit); Donation-to-Policy Timeline table; Sources (Tier 1-3).
- Research methodology: 5 parallel web searches (Secretary of Labor tenure, Epstein plea deal sweetheart framework, US Attorney role and NPA, resignation catalyst and timeline, FIU career background). Epstein vault connection established via [[Jeffrey Epstein Network]] wikilink. Trump Cabinet context via [[_Donald Trump Master Profile]] wikilinks. Prison industry connection via [[GEO Group]] and [[CoreCivic]] (private detention benefiting from Trump enforcement expansion).

### Editorial decisions:
- Framed Acosta as "institutional survivor" rather than individual villain. The sweetheart deal was a prosecutorial decision within his authority; complicity is structural, not uniquely personal.
- Documented DOJ review finding "poor judgment" but no prosecutable misconduct. This is load-bearing to the class analysis — the system is designed to allow this.
- Positioned Labor policies as genuine beneficiaries to capital (franchise companies, gig economy), not minor tweaks. "Unleashing jobs" rhetoric masks worker deprotection.
- Connected Epstein deal (2008) to resignation (2019) across 11 years of continued trafficking to show what impunity enables.
- Noted Acosta's return to institutional positions post-Labor Secretary, fulfilling the pattern: institutional loyalty preserves career even after scandal.

### Session 34 — March 22, 2026

**Context.** Standalone build task: RFK Jr and the HHS Demolition policy sub-note under Trump master profile Healthcare domain. Primary deliverable tracking pharmaceutical contradiction, MAHA branding vs deregulation reality, CDC/FDA workforce demolition, vaccine schedule changes, and measles outbreak cascade.

### What was built:
**[[RFK Jr and the HHS Demolition - Make America Healthy Again Meets Pharma Deregulation|RFK Jr and the HHS Demolition - Make America Healthy Again Meets Pharma Deregulation]]** (Topics/Politicians/Republicans/Presidential/Donald Trump/Healthcare/) — 1 file:
- Policy sub-note analyzing RFK Jr as HHS Secretary appointed through August 2024 Trump endorsement deal. 220 lines.
- Core sections: Who Is RFK Jr. (Children's Health Defense chair 2015-2023, $20K/week compensation, 30 lawsuits vs vaccine programs, campaign suspension-for-HHS-Cabinet deal); HHS Confirmation and Machinery (52-48 February 13 2025, sworn in March 19 2025, MAHA executive order); The MAHA Brand vs Reality (nutrition/natural therapy rhetoric vs. supplement deregulation + pharma enforcement weakening); FDA Restructuring and Staff Cuts (10,000 HHS layoffs May 27 2025, 3,500 FDA cuts shrinking agency 20%, Peter Marks resignation March 28 2025 quote: "truth and transparency not desired"); CDC Budget Cuts (500M mRNA contracts canceled, 11.4B state/local funding rolled back, 579 vaccination program positions eliminated, vaccine schedule changes for six vaccines); The Measles Crisis (2,281 cases 2025 = 34-year high, lost measles elimination status early 2026, 1,100+ 2026 cases, 17-member CDC vaccine panel ousted and replaced); The Class Analysis Two Masters (working class gains validation of pharma distrust but loses public health infrastructure, supplement industry gets deregulation, Big Pharma gets weakened enforcement while maintaining monopoly, working class families lose food safety/disease prevention when poor); Food Safety and Nutrition Policy (industrial sanitary requirement elimination, weakened meat inspection/pesticide oversight); HHS Workforce and DOGE Connection (280-320K positions eliminated, spending UP 5% despite cuts, Davis-Bacon enforcement targeted); The Pharma Contradiction Deepened (Mar-a-Lago pharma dinners while attacking pharma corruption, PhRMA welcomed Kennedy's FDA weakening); Current Status March 2026 (Kennedy remains HHS Secretary, February 2026 leadership shakeup, 1,000+ employees signed resignation demand letter, Kennedy has not resigned).
- Three custom callouts (money: FDA restructuring targets regulatory staff while protecting drug approvals; contradiction: MAHA ideology vs practice; quote: Peter Marks resignation statement).
- Research methodology: Four parallel web searches (confirmation date, FDA restructuring, MAHA pharmaceutical deregulation, measles outbreak), three additional searches (CHD background, RFK Jr status March 2026, pharma contradiction). All sources verified working URLs and Tier ratings (Tier 1: government, HHS, Congress.gov; Tier 2: major journalism WaPo, STAT, CNN, NPR, Axios, Bloomberg, PBS, Al Jazeera, CBS; Tier 3: Democrats.org). 26 total sources.

### Editorial decisions:
- Framed RFK Jr as transactional appointment negotiated through endorsement deal, not ideological conversion. Deal structure: August 2024 campaign suspension + endorsement in Phoenix in exchange for Cabinet post (HHS one of several discussed options).
- Documented MAHA as functional contradiction serving three distinct classes simultaneously: working class gets validation of legitimate pharma distrust, supplement industry gets deregulation, Big Pharma gets weakened enforcement. Only working class patients lose.
- Centered measles outbreak as material outcome of vaccine schedule changes: 2,281 cases in 2025 (first high in 34 years), loss of US measles elimination status, active outbreak clusters in 2026.
- Positioned pharmaceutical industry dinners at Mar-a-Lago as evidence of accommodation: industry wasn't lobbying against Kennedy's appointment because his deregulatory agenda served their interests even while he attacked their "corruption" rhetorically.
- Treated Peter Marks resignation (March 28, 2025) as direct indicator of institutional pressure: top FDA vaccine official departed citing disagreement with Kennedy, statement "truth and transparency not desired" quoted verbatim.
- No ambiguity on current status: Kennedy remains HHS Secretary as of March 22, 2026. Over 1,000 HHS employees signed resignation demand letter. Leadership shakeup February 2026 (O'Neill departed, Stuart departing) indicates dysfunction but not Cabinet change.

**Key finding: URL verification methodology.** Browser navigation is the only reliable method for checking links in this environment. WebFetch blocked by network proxy. JavaScript fetch blocked by CORS. Direct browser tab navigation is the only method that reliably detects 404s vs working pages. This is documented in the updated Research Methodology.

### Session 36 — March 22, 2026

**Context:** Standalone build task: Veterans & Military domain under Trump master profile. Two-file build covering VA privatization pipeline, defense contractor donations, Pete Hegseth pipeline, DOGE military readiness impacts, and class analysis of veteran healthcare privatization.

### What was built:
**Veterans & Military domain** (2 files, new subdomain):

1. **[[Veterans and Military - Donors and Backers]]** (158 lines):
 - D&B node mapping funding flows to Trump's military and veterans policy
 - Defense contractor donations: Lockheed Martin ($1M to Sedition Caucus), RTX, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics. Defense industry gave $6M+ to election-deniers since January 6th. Iran war escalation (Trump requesting $200B supplemental) flows directly to contractors
 - VA privatization companies: Optum/UnitedHealth (TPA regions 1-3, $544M COVID contract, $3.14M PAC 2023-24), TriWest (TPA regions 4-5, $86B contract + $103B increase requested), CVS/Aetna (dual-enrollment profit extraction)
 - VA MISSION Act (2018): expanded community care, created permanent funding stream to private networks. Spending rose from $14.8B (2018) to $28.5B (2023)
 - Pete Hegseth: Fox News to Defense Secretary. Pentagon spending $93B single month (September 2025). "It takes money to kill bad guys" justifying Iran war escalation
 - DOGE cuts to military readiness: 5,400+ civilian workforce reductions, base closures, military family housing funds repurposed for bonus announcements, IT system failures during Iran war
 - Koch network and Concerned Veterans for America: CVA $14M+ from Freedom Partners (Koch ATM). VA Secretary Doug Collins strongly supported by CVA. Inside Pentagon policy now implementing Koch privatization ideology
 - Wait time argument: circular logic of engineered crisis. Underfund VA, wait times increase, cite as proof of system failure, outsource to profitable private networks. VA continues underfunded. Cycle repeats
 - Wounded Warrior and veteran service organizations: funded by contractors and healthcare corporations with direct profit interest in privatization
 - Sources: 21 working URLs, Tier 1-2, all verified

2. **[[The VA Privatization Pipeline - Who Profits From Veteran Healthcare]]** (179 lines):
 - Deep analysis of 15-year Koch network project moving from think tank advocacy to Pentagon policy implementation
 - Timeline: 2010 CVA founding → 2018 MISSION Act → 2023 spending spike → 2025 Collins appointment → 2026 community care dominance
 - MISSION Act mechanics: "choice" framing disguises funding transfer mechanism. TriWest/Optum designated as TPAs. Community Care Network created. Referral rates increase 15-20% annually
 - Three-layer profit extraction: (1) TPAs extract 8-12% administrative overhead ($2.28-3.42B annually), (2) private providers receive referral volume (15-25% margin higher than VA), (3) dual-enrollment capture (VA + Medicare Advantage pays twice for same service)
 - Dual-enrollment profit detail: CMS overpaid Aetna $1.32B (2020 alone) for VA enrollees using zero Medicare services. 2018-2021 $90B total duplicate spending (VA spending $46B, CMS paying $44B for same population)
 - Koch connection: CVA formed 2011-2012. 2015 received $16M, $14M from Freedom Partners. After 2016 restructuring, AFP became umbrella. VA Secretary Collins appointment shows Koch network inside Pentagon. Outside advocacy (CVA) → inside government (Collins) → budget implementation (community care expansion)
 - Budget dynamics: FY2026 proposes $14.4B increase in community care (67% jump). 75% of all new VA medical funding goes to private providers. VA direct care funding flat/reduced. VA advisory panel warned expansion "threatens to materially erode VA's direct care system"
 - DOGE impact on VA: workforce cuts accelerate. Staffing reductions create appointment delays, referral processing delays, IT failures. Veterans choose community care (well-funded) over degraded VA direct care
 - Disability benefits processing crisis: VA claims processing takes 6 months to 3 years. DOGE cuts to adjudicators/processors worsen backlog. Privatization advocates cite processing failures as justification for private administration (which would add cost and reduce benefits)
 - Class analysis: working class veterans lose integrated care coordination, specialist access, higher out-of-pocket costs. Healthcare shareholders win at three extraction tiers. Military families lose BAH housing funds. Defense contractors win production acceleration without readiness improvement
 - Current 2026 status: 40% of VA patients in community care. Referral rates increasing 15-20% annually. TriWest/Optum requesting contract extensions. Federal budget locks in privatized spending. Return to integrated VA now structurally impossible. By 2030 trajectory suggests 60-70% in community care networks, VA becomes skeletal residual system
 - Sources: 13 working URLs, Tier 1-2, all verified

### Editorial decisions:
- Framed VA privatization as deliberate policy sequence, not market failure. Underfunding is engineered to create crisis justifying outsourcing
- Centered wait time argument as circular logic with design intent: create delays through underfunding, cite delays as proof of system failure, outsource to profitable networks while VA remains underfunded
- Treated dual-enrollment profit as structural extraction: government pays twice for same service. CVS/Aetna and UnitedHealth gain reimbursement for services not delivered
- Positioned Pete Hegseth as Fox-to-Pentagon pipeline, not defense expertise. His lack of traditional Pentagon background means direct industry access to decision-making
- Framed DOGE cuts as contradiction: weapons production exempt from cuts (contractors benefit), military readiness suffers (workforce reduced, housing defunded, IT fails). Capital extracts while military capability declines
- Documented Koch network pipeline completion: CVA outside advocacy → Collins Pentagon appointment → budget implementation. Privatization ideology now inside government

### Session 37 — March 22, 2026

**Context:** Master profile build for Pete Hegseth, Trump's Secretary of Defense. Single-file deliverable extracted from Session 36 priorities queue. Focus: biography, confirmation battle, Fox-to-Pentagon pipeline, Koch network VA privatization connection, Iran war management, DOGE contradictions, sexual assault settlement, and class analysis.

### What was built:
**[[_Pete Hegseth Master Profile]]** (186 lines, master politician profile):

- Biography: Fox News co-host (Fox & Friends Weekend 2017-2024), Army National Guard major (Iraq, Afghanistan, Guantanamo), Princeton BA politics (2003), Harvard Kennedy School MPP (2013). Born June 6, 1980, Minneapolis. Chose Princeton over West Point to play basketball. No visible Pentagon or strategic planning expertise before appointment.
- Confirmation: 51-50 Senate vote January 24, 2025, with VP Vance tie-breaking. Three Republicans opposed (Collins, Murkowski, McConnell). Second Cabinet member in US history confirmed on vice presidential tie-break. Narrowest Cabinet confirmation in modern era. One of most contested nominees ever.
- Central thesis: Pentagon transformed from independent military institution into ideological arm of Trump political project. Hegseth selected for media presence and political loyalty, not expertise. Defense Secretary role shifted from managing readiness to performing readiness for television audience.
- Core contradiction: Presents as warrior champion of troops while implementing DOGE cuts that systematically gut military readiness (50,000+ civilian workforce cuts), decimate personnel support, redirect logistics funds to weapons production. Requested $200B supplemental for Iran war while cutting base Pentagon budget $11.1B. Cut military family education/housing benefits while defending Pentagon luxury spending.
- Fox-to-Pentagon pipeline: News Corp/Fox News cultivated Hegseth as ideological asset. Groomed him through television platform to become politically viable for Cabinet position. Selected specifically for ability to communicate Pentagon decisions to Trump base rather than Pentagon management capability. Pentagon now functions as extension of Trump media apparatus.
- Koch network completion: Hegseth was CVA (Concerned Veterans for America) executive director 2011-2016. CVA is Koch-funded front group advocating VA privatization for 15 years. Now inside Pentagon with power to implement policies he previously advocated externally. VA Secretary Doug Collins has similar CVA alignment. Koch network moved from external advocacy (CVA) to Pentagon implementation (Hegseth/Collins) to budget reality (community care funding increases, VA direct care cuts flat/reduced). Privatization now locked into Pentagon policy and budget. Working class veteran loses integrated care, specialist access, coordination. Contractors (Optum, TriWest, CVS/Aetna) gain permanent funding streams. By 2030 trajectory suggests 60-70% of VA patients in community care networks. Return to integrated VA structurally impossible.
- Sexual assault settlement: Accused of assault October 2017 at Republican women's conference in Monterey, California. Paid $50,000 confidential settlement. During Senate testimony claimed "falsely accused" and "completely cleared" while simultaneously confirming settlement payment. Contradiction never resolved. Attorney claimed NDA "no longer in effect" but details remain sealed. Multiple additional allegations during confirmation. Senate did not investigate. Trump defended him publicly. Pattern documented: workplace harassment at CVA (female staffers reported sexism, financial mismanagement, alcohol use), no investigations completed at any institution.
- Iran war management: Requested $200B supplemental after implementing $11.1B Pentagon base cuts. Justified with slogan "it takes money to kill bad guys," operationally incoherent but useful for justifying unlimited spending. Pentagon faced degraded readiness (50,000+ civilian cuts, supply chain failures, IT failures during operations) from DOGE cuts while simultaneously requesting $200B for war. Contradiction reveals pattern: create operational intensity through readiness cuts, demand emergency funding to address intensity, justify contractor production acceleration without addressing readiness decline.
- Defense contractor relationships: Pentagon cuts exempt weapons production while slashing personnel, logistics, IT. F-35 jets, missiles, munitions see budget increases. Lockheed Martin ($1M+ to election deniers since 2025), RTX, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics all benefit. Hegseth maintains financial interests in contractor profits. No ethics disclosures divesting contractor stocks before taking office. Financial disclosures incomplete as of March 2026. Defense procurement decisions now flow through Defense Secretary with prior ideological alignment to contractors and ongoing financial interest in contractor profits.
- Rhetorical signature moves: (1) Warrior authenticity theater: emphasizes combat deployments and military rank while implementing policies degrading military readiness and personnel support. Rhetoric obscures policy outcomes. (2) Populist anti-elite positioning: presents self as outsider challenging Pentagon bureaucrats despite holding two Ivy League degrees and three combat deployments (insider status). Criticizes "woke indoctrination" at universities he graduated from while cutting military family education benefits at those same institutions. Anti-elite positioning performative. Actual moves concentrate power in defense contractors. (3) "It takes money to kill bad guys": tautological justification for unlimited war spending. Avoids discussing strategic objective, timeline, or success metrics. Allows avoiding questions about why $200B supplemental comes after $11.1B base cuts.
- Class analysis: Defense contractors win (weapons production accelerates, procurement now controlled by ideologically-aligned Defense Secretary with prior financial interest, Iran war escalation drives munitions demand, estimated $50-100B profit acceleration over five years). Trump base wins rhetorically (Hegseth communicates Pentagon policies in language resonating with voters, frames cuts as "reform" while protecting weapons production). Working-class military families lose (50,000+ civilian cuts create supply chain delays, maintenance delays, logistics failures; housing benefits cut; education tuition assistance cut; services reduced). Working-class veterans lose (VA privatization accelerates, integrated care dismantled, specialist access reduces, out-of-pocket costs increase). Military institution loses (readiness degrades while production continues, contradiction never resolved).
- Sources: 20 working URLs, Tier 2-3, all verified

### Editorial decisions:
- Positioned Hegseth as media personality, not Pentagon professional. Lack of Pentagon experience is central to his profile, not a weakness to downplay.
- Framed TV-to-Pentagon pipeline as institutional transformation. Pentagon leadership now selected for media persuasion capability over management expertise.
- Documented Koch network completion: outside advocacy → Pentagon implementation. Privatization now inside government.
- Treated DOGE contradictions as design intent, not policy accident. Cuts to readiness justify emergency spending that accelerates contractor production.
- Positioned sexual assault settlement as pattern: credible allegations consistently unresolved at every institution.
- Centered working-class impacts: military families, veterans, defense-dependent communities bear cost while contractors and Trump base benefit.
- Class analysis throughout: who benefits (contractors, Trump base rhetorically, Hegseth's ideological network), who pays (working-class military and veterans).

5. Sexual assault settlement pattern: credible allegations at CVA, Fox News, and during confirmation. No investigations completed. Senate confirmation proceeded despite allegations and lack of Pentagon experience. Confirmation standard now: ideological alignment > character assessment > qualification verification.
6. By 2030 trajectory suggests 60-70% of VA patients in community care networks (private contractors). Integrated VA system becomes structurally impossible to restore. Working-class veteran loses to contractor profit extraction at three tiers (administrative overhead 8-12%, provider margins 15-25% higher than VA, dual-enrollment Medicare capture).

- Positions Substack as the source for connections and math corporate media won't do

- Key distinction: mainstream reports events, we report systems

Researched mainstream coverage (Fortune, Time, Jacobin, Popular Info, WaPo, CNN, Al Jazeera, Grist, Dallas Fed) and mapped 6 underreported angles unique to the vault:
1. DOGE-to-war pipeline as designed system (not ironic coincidence)
2. Donor ROI math (33,000:1 defense, 160:1 fossil fuel — nobody else has calculated this)
3. Three donor constituencies converging on one war
4. Trump flip-flop as donor arbitrage (not incompetence)
5. Kushner full loop: $110B deal → $2B collection → "mediation" → war → $5B new raise
6. Working class cost denominated against $480-600B donor profit

- **Post 9 (Wed Apr 1): "The $219 Million Oil Bet"** — Fossil fuel windfall, Strait of Hormuz, 160:1 ROI. Outlined.
- **Post 10 (Fri Apr 3): "The $2 Billion Man"** — Kushner full loop, Saudi PIF, war fundraising. Outlined.

### Files created (2):

- `Guerilla Propaganda/The Donor Map - Substack Series/Post 8 - The 33000 to 1 Return.md` — Full draft ready for editing/posting

### Editorial decisions:
- "What Nobody's Reporting" chosen as series identity over "Follow the Money," "The Donor Map," and "The Class Report"
- Iran war prioritized over Obsidian Publish (Publish saved for Wednesday reset)
- Post 8 originally framed around "$6M to election objectors = 33,000:1 ROI" — David pushed back ("do donations to election objectors really position itself straight to the Iran war?"). Correct instinct: the election objector angle was correlation, not causation. **Reworked** to lead with the permanent defense lobbying machine ($191M/year, 766 lobbyists, 450,000% ROI per Public Citizen) and DOGE removing oversight as the original angle. Much stronger.
- **CRITICAL WORKFLOW NOTE FOR ALL FUTURE SESSIONS — TWO RULES:**
 1. **Riff first, write second.** Do NOT jump straight to full drafts. The editorial process is: riff → pressure-test logic → David decides → then write. David's editorial pushback caught a real weakness in Post 8. The back-and-forth IS the quality control. Bring data, connections, and gaps. Let David direct what gets written.
 2. **Go along to get along.** When discussing topics, follow David's lead. If he wants to explore an angle, explore it with him. Don't gatekeep, don't redirect, don't refuse to engage. Be a thinking partner, not a gatekeeper. Push back when the *evidence* is weak (like the election objector correlation) — that's useful. But don't push back on *direction*. David steers the conversation. The AI's job is to bring honest data and let him decide where it goes. The sweet spot: not a yes-man, not a lecturer. A collaborator who keeps the data honest and follows editorial direction.

### Post 8 has two versions in the file:
- V1 (election objector framing) — superseded
- V2 (permanent machine + DOGE brakes framing) — current draft, still needs David's final approval before scheduling

**Posts 9 and 10 are outlined only.** Per new workflow: discuss angles with David first before drafting. Outlines in Series Framework note.

### Session 38e — March 23, 2026 (Obsidian Publish Strategy + Roadmap)

**Context:** Continued from Session 38d. Shifted from infrastructure work to strategic planning for Obsidian Publish and long-term vault completeness.

### Major strategic decisions made:
1. **Obsidian Publish approach:** Password-protected beta first. Only "ready" notes published. No broken wikilinks — every published note must link only to other published notes or plain text. Narrative presentation layer for outside readers (journalists, researchers, staffers), not raw vault formatting. Landing page with thesis statement and graph view as entry point. Custom domain deferred — use default Obsidian Publish URL for beta.

2. **Target audience clarified:** Not casual readers. The published vault is a **source book** for journalists and researchers — people who can use the connective tissue that their own beat reporting misses. The graph view is the hook. The dossier format (Who They Are / What They Want / Who They Fund / What They've Gotten) works for this audience. Narrative intro paragraphs needed at top of each published note for cold readers.

3. **Full link chain audit completed.** Ran automated analysis of all 47 ready notes, extracted every wikilink, checked whether targets exist and are at ready status.
 - Results: 259 not-ready link targets, 95 missing files. Every single ready note has at least one dead link.
 - Key finding: many "missing" files are wikilink naming mismatches (e.g., `[[_JD Vance Master Profile|JD Vance]]` → file is `_JD Vance Master Profile`). Mechanical fixes, not research gaps.
 - Top bottlenecks: Trump (35 ready refs, 222 total), Koch (15/120), Newsom (11/136), Musk (9/62), AIPAC (5/114)

4. **Mission statement and defined universe established.** David's directive: "I don't want to half ass this database... the end goal is to have notes on every relevant politician today, branching out to every donor node, and making it so there isn't a leaf unturned." This is a long-term systematic mapping project, not a reactive content pipeline.

5. **Baseline thresholds set for "complete" coverage:**
 - Politicians: full profile if 2+ criteria met (committee chair, $500K+ from single donor, leadership, cabinet/SCOTUS, documented pipeline, top 10 state governor). Placeholder for all other federal officeholders.
 - Individual mega-donors: full profile at $1M+ per cycle
 - Industry blocs: full profile at $10M+ combined lobbying + contributions
 - Super PACs: full profile at $20M+ per cycle
 - Dark money: full profile at $5M+ with documented pipelines
 - Total defined universe: ~700-850 notes
 - Thresholds are adjustable baseline — can be revised as coverage matures.

6. **Two-track approach:** Backbone (defined universe checklist — is every required note present?) + Organic (thread-following, contradictions, story angles). Backbone ensures nothing major is missing. Organic work is where the analysis happens. Both run in parallel.

### Files created/modified:
- `topics/Stories/Vault Maintenance/Publish Roadmap - The Donor Map Database.md` — CREATED: Full roadmap with mission statement, defined universe, vault status audit, bottleneck map (Critical Tier + Tiers 1-3), 5-phase execution plan, publish strategy milestones, session workflow SOP. This is now the strategic document that every future session references.

### Vault status snapshot (March 23, 2026):
- 762 total .md files
- 47 ready (6.2%)
- 469 developed (61.5%)
- 167 draft (21.9%)
- 15 raw (2.0%)
- 57 no status (7.5%)
- ~110 politician profiles exist (need ~150-200 full + 350-400 placeholders)
- ~90 donor-related profiles exist (need ~200 total)

### Roadmap phases:
- Phase 1: Mechanical fixes (wikilink standardization, naming mismatches) — 1-2 sessions
- Phase 2: Big Five promotions (Trump, Koch, Newsom, AIPAC, Musk) — 3-5 sessions
- Phase 3: Tier 1 promotions (13 high-impact notes) — 4-6 sessions
- Phase 4: Tier 2 + high-value drafts — 5-8 sessions
- Phase 5: Volume build (ongoing, months)
- Milestone: 100 ready = minimum viable publish, 200 = serious database, 400 = comprehensive, 600+ = complete model

### Editorial workflow reminder (from Session 38c, still in effect):
1. Riff first, write second
2. Go along to get along — follow David's editorial direction, push back on evidence not direction

### Next session priorities:

### Session 38g — March 23, 2026 (Structural Infrastructure: Machine-Readable Vault)

**Context:** Continued from Session 38f. David requested five structural infrastructure improvements to make the vault self-documenting and machine-readable for future sessions. Entirely infrastructure work — no research, no content changes.

### What was done:
### 1. YAML Frontmatter — Added to Every File
Standardized YAML block prepended to all 779 .md files in the vault (excluding Lear Avenue Job, .obsidian, and other non-vault directories). Script handles:
- Stripping old/inconsistent YAML blocks and replacing with standardized format
- Auto-classifying file type based on path and content analysis
- Extracting content-readiness from existing inline tags
- Detecting highest source tier cited
- Setting parent links for sub-notes (auto-resolves master profile in folder hierarchy)

Schema:
```yaml
---
title: "Human-readable title"
type: politician | donor | pac | corporation | story | methodology | reference | index | daily-update | sub-note
content-readiness: raw | draft | developed | ready
last-updated: YYYY-MM-DD
source-tier: 1-4 | null
parent: "" | null
---
```

Type distribution: sub-note (296), politician (202), donor (97), story (91), corporation (35), pac (17), reference (15), daily-update (13), index (8), methodology (5)
Status distribution: developed (631), draft (61), ready (50), raw (37)

### 2. _VAULT_INDEX.md Created
Canonical manifest at `topics/_VAULT_INDEX.md` listing all 779 files with columns: File (wikilink), Path, Type, Status, Source Tier, Last Updated. Includes summary tables by type and status. Generated from frontmatter data — can be regenerated anytime by running the manifest script.

### 3. CLAUDE.md Rewritten
Replaced the minimal CLAUDE.md (8 lines pointing to Session Timeline) with comprehensive vault instructions covering:
- Quick start checklist for new sessions
- Core thesis statement
- Full folder structure with descriptions
- Naming conventions (master profiles, sub-notes, donor nodes, daily updates, wikilinks)
- YAML frontmatter schema with field definitions
- Type taxonomy table
- Status system with promotion criteria
- Source tier system with examples and format standard
- Formatting rules (headers, callouts, temporal mapping tables)
- Note anatomy for donor nodes, master profiles, and sub-notes
- Analytical patterns list (9 patterns)
- Epstein two-vault system rules
- Session workflow SOP
- Key reference documents table

### 4. Session Timeline Split
- Archived Sessions 1-29 (March 18-22) to `topics/Stories/Vault Maintenance/_SESSION_HISTORY_ARCHIVE.md` (605,870 chars)
- Main Session Timeline trimmed from 7,202 lines → 1,814 lines (78.9% reduction)
- Recent sessions (30+) remain in the main file for quick orientation
- Created `topics/_SESSION_HISTORY.md` as a pointer file explaining the split
- Archive link note added at top of Session Log section

### 5. _README.md Files Added
7 README files written to major folders:
- `topics/_README.md` — Vault root overview with folder structure and file counts
- `topics/Politicians/_README.md` — Folder structure, naming conventions, type rules
- `topics/Donors & Power Networks/_README.md` — Sector folder listing with counts, note anatomy

- `Guerilla Propaganda/_README.md` — Content pipeline structure, editorial rules
- `GP-Epstein-Research/README.md` — Already existed (kept as-is)

- 0 field completeness issues

- CLAUDE.md has all 9 required sections

#### Formatting patterns fixed across all 16 files

- **Callout blocks:** ExxonMobil, Boeing, PhRMA had `[!money]` and `[!contradiction]` callouts without required `>` prefix — would not render in Obsidian without fix
- **Duplicate tag lines:** ExxonMobil, Boeing, PhRMA, UDP all had extraneous `tags: #donor` lines — merged into primary tag line
- **Source format standardization:** Three distinct non-standard formats found: dash format (`— Name: URL [Tier X]`), dot format (`· Tier X`), double-dash format (`-- Name — desc: URL [Tier X]`). All standardized to `(Tier X)` parenthetical.
- **Header hierarchy:** UDP had ## headers; Harris and Pelosi had mixed ## and bold; Sanders, Netanyahu, Saban, Bianco had all bold. All converted to ### per CLAUDE.md rules.

Hosted Night Two of 2024 Democratic National Convention for Kamala Harris while on CNN payroll as "Republican commentator." CNN cut away from her DNC speech to air Gavin Newsom interview. Married Republican lobbyist Al Cárdenas (FL GOP chairman) Mar 2019 — household income spans Democratic TV commentary and Republican lobbying. FARA record: lobbied for Nicaraguan President Arnoldo Alemán (later convicted, imprisoned for $100M embezzlement) and El Salvadoran President Tony Saca (serving 10-year sentence for $300M theft). Key pattern: **Centrist Laundering** + **Both-Sides Illusion** (household monetizes both sides). FEC: $0 confirmed. 9 Chrome-verified sources.

**Deepened:**

3. **Greg Gutfeld** (`Right/Greg Gutfeld.md`) — 6 → 11 sources. Key changes: (a) salary corrected $7M → $9M per Variety/Deadline April 2024 contract extension reporting; (b) expanded pre-Fox career section (American Spectator intern → Men's Health editor-in-chief → Stuff editor-in-chief [fired 2003] → Maxim UK editor 2004–2006); (c) pharma ad section expanded with Media Matters and Daily Beast sourcing (pharma paradox: vaccine manufacturers fund network running most vaccine-skeptical content in cable news); (d) 2 new Timeline rows (Q1 2025 cable news 25-54 demo dominance; 2026 no-independent-platform observation); (e) updated last-updated date.

#### Key Findings

**Chris Wallace — CNN+ post-mortem:** The failure of CNN+ is the most expensive proof of the "serious journalist" brand's limits. $8.5M/yr anchor for a platform that lasted 30 days and peaked at <10K daily users. The platform dependency pattern is explicit: Wallace's 55-year career was 100% institutional. Without the network, the brand had no independent floor.

**Ana Navarro — FARA/household conflict:** CNN employs Navarro as a cross-partisan "Republican" commentator while her husband lobbies for Republican corporate clients. Neither CNN nor ABC requires disclosure of this conflict. The FARA record (from The Grayzone, Tier 4 — cites primary FARA documents) shows Navarro lobbied for two Latin American governments later convicted of massive corruption — the same origin countries whose refugee conditions she now advocates against. Independent FARA verification recommended at https://efile.fara.gov.

**Greg Gutfeld — salary vs. FEC:** Corrected to $9M/yr (April 2024 contract). At $9M salary + $0 FEC, Gutfeld extends the Fox institutional host pattern (Hannity $45M/$0, Ingraham $15M/$0, Watters $5M/$1K Obama 2012). The pharma paradox sourced: vaccine manufacturers fund Fox while Fox hosts undermine vaccine confidence.

### Lobbying Firm Builder — March 27, 2026 (lobbying-firm-builder scheduled task, Run 7)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder
**Chrome status:** Available — 11 URLs Chrome-verified VALID
**FEC API status:** Not used this session (no donation data needed)

#### Summary

All 22 Phase 1 + Phase 2 lobbying firm profiles remain at `ready` status. Continuous mode deepening activated. Two profiles targeted for substantive new 2025–2026 research:

1. **Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld** — Major update correcting a key factual error (Nippon Steel characterized as "failed" lobbying — actually the deal CLOSED June 18, 2025) and adding the Verhoff bundler-to-lobbyist pipeline.

2. **Holland & Knight** — Extended the Freedhoff revolving door section to capture the full 2025–2026 TSCA rollback timeline, documenting H&K's active role advising chemical industry clients on exploiting each deregulatory step.

#### Key Findings

**Akin Gump / Nippon Steel — Bundler-to-Lobbyist Pipeline (2024–2025):**
- Geoffrey Verhoff was Trump's **largest 2024 campaign bundler** ($3.6M raised for Trump 47 Committee, Vice Chairman of RNC Finance 2017–2021)
- In Q1 2025 alone, Verhoff generated **$3.4M in new billings** — immediate monetization of Trump access post-inauguration
- Nippon Steel paid Akin Gump **$5.5M+ total** in lobbying fees (led by Verhoff + Brian Ballard of Ballard Partners)
- Trump reversed his Biden-era position in May 2025; deal **closed June 18, 2025** with golden share, American CEO, $14B investment pledge
- Sludge headline captures it directly: "Trump Flip-Flopped on U.S. Steel Deal After Lobbying Blitz by His Top Bundlers"
- ROI calculation: $5.5M lobbying investment secured a $14.9B acquisition (fees = 0.04% of deal value)
- Pattern flagged: **Bundling Operation** — Verhoff raised campaign contributions for Trump while simultaneously serving as Akin Gump's lobbyist for clients who needed Trump access

**Holland & Knight / Freedhoff — Regulatory Shuttle in Real Time (2025–2026):**
- Freedhoff (Biden's top TSCA enforcer) joined H&K April 2025; immediately began advising chemical industry on Trump-era deregulation
- Complete TSCA rollback sequence documented: TSCA deadlines extended (Jun 2025) → Risk Eval Framework weakened (Sep 2025) → PFAS reporting rule stripped (Nov 2025) → SCCAP rule rescission proposed (Feb 2026) → Senate GOP TSCA reform bill targeting evidence burden reduction (Mar 2026)
- H&K published client guidance at each step, including a March 2026 analysis: "EPA's Proposed Rule Signals Rollback of Chemical Safety Requirements"
- ACC ($520K/year client) is the primary beneficiary — the chemical industry's top trade group is now advised by the woman who wrote the rules being dismantled
- Pattern flagged: **Regulatory Shuttle** — Freedhoff moved regulator → H&K lobbyist → active advisor on rollback of her own regulatory work

#### New Piece Built

**"The Think Tank Money Map — Budget, Funding Sources, and the Illusion of Independence"** (`topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/The Think Tank Money Map — Budget, Funding Sources, and the Illusion of Independence.md`)

Compiled entirely from existing vault profiles — no new research required, all underlying data Tier 1 (IRS 990 filings). Key findings:

**Budget rankings:**
- RAND ($462M, 77% federal) and Aspen ($443.8M, inflated by $175M Bezos gift) dominate
- Centrist category total: ~$1.247B / Conservative: ~$416M+ / Liberal: ~$351M

- RAND 77% federal ($356M/yr); Urban 54% federal ($70M/yr); Atlantic Council 22% federal ($15M/yr)
- Combined federal funding to 5 institutions: ~$448M/yr — more than entire conservative ecosystem

- AEI: $86.7M from DonorsTrust/DCF (2003–2010)
- Heartland: $16.56M from Donors Capital Fund (2005–2013)
- Hudson: $8.9M from DonorsTrust; Federalist Society: $5.5M in one year

**Size-to-impact inversion:**
- Claremont $13.2M → VP Vance + Eastman Jan 6 memo + MAGA framework (highest ROI in vault)
- Federalist Society $22.5M → 6/9 SCOTUS justices, Chevron eliminated, $1.6B Leo multiplier
- Heritage $100.9M → 53% of 532 Project 2025 recommendations implemented

**Asset concentration:**
- CFR $692M net assets; Hoover ~$600M endowment; Aspen $647M; Brookings $551M; RAND $506M; Heritage $415M total assets
- Combined: 4 conservative institutions hold $1.5B+ in assets for permanent infrastructure

**Class analysis:** $2B annual industry converting donor-class preferences into expert consensus. Federal government subsidizes ~$740M/yr in foregone taxes on donations. The system pays for its own ideological justification.

#### Summary

**Promoted to READY (3 profiles, 7 hosts):**
- **Jeremy Boreing**: FEC $0 confirmed (0 API results for BOREING, JEREMY). Updated FEC Record section with confirmed data; promoted YAML and footer to `ready`.
- **Chapo Trap House**: All 4 hosts confirmed $0. Separate Chrome API queries run for MENAKER, WILL; BIEDERMAN, FELIX; CHRISTMAN, MATT; FROST, AMBER — all 0 results. Full multi-host FEC section written with per-host analytical callouts. Promoted to `ready`.
- **Red Scare**: Both hosts confirmed $0. NEKRASOVA, DASHA and KHACHIYAN, ANNA both 0 results. Per-host analytical callouts added. Promoted to `ready`.

**New builds (2 profiles):**
- **Lee Camp** (`Left/Lee Camp.md`, DEVELOPED): Stand-up comedian → RT America *Redacted Tonight* (Oct 2014–March 2022, 8 years Russian state-funded TV) → MintPress News (July 2022, Iran-adjacent funding questions). Vault's clearest case of "perverse independence theater" — anti-corporate media on foreign state TV. After RT shutdown, promoted Ukraine bioweapons and Great Reset conspiracy theories. FEC $0 confirmed. 4 Chrome-verified sources; 2 UNVERIFIED (NYT/WaPo block automated access — Chrome protocol failure, not missing sources). Developed: 150+ lines, 9 sections, Timeline table 9 rows.
- **Owen Jones** (`Left/Owen Jones.md`, DEVELOPED): British Guardian columnist (since 2014), 809K YouTube subs (Oct 2025), author of *Chavs* (2011) and *The Establishment* (2014). Left Labour March 2024 (Starmer rightward shift). Raffi Berg/BBC libel case won March 2026 (High Court: opinion not fact). UK institutional-left parallel: Guardian editorial ceiling pushed riskiest Gaza/BBC work to Drop Site News — same institutional constraint architecture as U.S. corporate media but different mechanism. FEC N/A (British citizen). 4 Chrome-verified sources; 3 UNVERIFIED (Guardian blocks automated Chrome access). Developed: 180+ lines, 11 sections, Timeline table 9 rows.

#### Key Analytical Findings

**Chapo/Daily Wire FEC symmetry:** Both the Daily Wire talent (Boreing $0, Shapiro $0, Walsh $0, Knowles $0) and the Chapo DSA-socialist hosts (Menaker $0, Biederman $0, Christman $0, Frost $0) give nothing to federal campaigns. The mechanism is opposite: Daily Wire talent exercises influence through platform scale and strategic restraint; Chapo hosts have explicit anti-electoral ideology. The FEC record is identical; the political theory behind it is inverted.

**Red Scare $0 paradox:** Nekrasova endorsed Trump 2024 and met Thiel's inner circle in 2022. Her FEC record is $0. The anti-institutional nihilist aesthetic of Red Scare doesn't convert to campaign giving even when the political alignment is explicit — "we've always loved Trump" as cultural statement, $0 as financial commitment.

**Lee Camp structural pattern:** The vault's clearest case that "independence from corporate advertisers" is not the same as "editorial independence." Camp's anti-corporate content was real, the platform was Russian state propaganda infrastructure, and his public response to RT's shutdown blamed the U.S. government rather than Russia. The migration to MintPress News (Iran-adjacent) continues the same structural pattern. Not hypocrisy — structural dependency on foreign state-aligned funding as the only available alternative to corporate advertiser dependency for radical left content.

- Funding structure: United Democracy Project (AIPAC super PAC arm) contributed $4,042,000; Anthony Davis (Linden Capital Partners) $1.5M; Blair Frank (Capital Group) $1M; Michael Sacks (GSM Grosnover, Northwestern trustee) $900K — 87% from 4 funders
- IL-8 spending: $3.3–3.9M supporting Bean; Chicago Progressive Partnership (AIPAC-linked, shares vendors/donors/treasurer) spent additional $700K opposing Ahmed

**Key findings — tactical innovations:**
- Disclosure timing exploit: ECW's February spending not disclosed until March 20 (3 days post-election) — by design

- Two-PAC structure: ECW (pro-Bean feminist brand) + Chicago Progressive Partnership (anti-Ahmed progressive-coded economic attacks) created surround-sound coverage without either revealing AIPAC origin

### Thin Profile Expansion — March 27, 2026 (thin-profile-expansion scheduled task)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** thin-profile-expansion

#### Summary

Expanded **Defense Contractors** donor node (`topics/Donors & Power Networks/Defense & Intelligence/Defense Contractors.md`) from a 33-line thin stub (falsely marked `ready`) to a full ~165-line donor node with complete anatomy. The original file had only a "Who They Are" section and 3 sources; the expanded version has all standard sections.

**Target selection:** Scanned all raw nodes in vault index — all 23 confirmed as redirect stubs (functioning as intended, skipped). Shifted to thin "ready" files under 60 lines. Selected Defense Contractors (33 lines, `ready`) as highest-impact target — the military-industrial complex is a paradigm case for the vault's core thesis (donors control politicians through bipartisan consensus).

**Key findings — Defense sector political spending (2024 cycle):**
- Total political contributions: $43.5M (45% Dem, 52% Rep — near-even bipartisan split)
- Total lobbying: $159.2M via 1,017 lobbyists (617 revolving door = 60.67%)
- Top contributors: Lockheed Martin ($4.67M), Northrop Grumman ($3.42M), RTX/Raytheon ($2.90M), General Atomics ($2.66M), L3Harris ($2.53M)
- Combined political investment (contributions + lobbying): ~$202.7M → preceded $900.6B FY2026 NDAA (4,400:1 return ratio)

**Key findings — Pentagon contract data (2020-2024):**
- $2.4 trillion in Pentagon contracts to private firms (54% of DOD discretionary spending)
- Top 5 primes captured $771B: Lockheed Martin ($313B), RTX ($145B), General Dynamics ($116B), Boeing ($115B), Northrop Grumman ($81B)
- This exceeds total U.S. diplomacy/foreign aid budget ($356B) over same period

**Key findings — Revolving door:**
- POGO documented 380+ high-ranking DOD officials cycling to industry (25 Generals, 9 Admirals, 43 Lt. Generals, 23 Vice Admirals)
- Trump admin: Mattis (General Dynamics board), Esper (Raytheon lobbyist), Shanahan (Boeing executive)
- Biden admin: Austin (Raytheon board, $1.7M departure package)

**Analytical patterns flagged:** Both-Sides Illusion (bipartisan defense consensus masking donor capture), Revolving Door (systematic Pentagon-to-industry pipeline), Donor-Class Override (spending floor rises regardless of party control)

**Note:** defense.gov now redirects to war.gov (DOD → DOW renaming under Trump admin). Updated URL in profile accordingly.

**Data method:**
- OpenSecrets defense sector summary, totals, lobbying pages (Chrome) — 2024 cycle data (Tier 1)
- Quincy Institute/Brown University "Profits of War" report (Chrome) — 2020-2024 contract analysis (Tier 2)
- POGO "Brass Parachutes" report (Chrome) — revolving door documentation (Tier 2)
- The Intercept (Chrome) — Biden Pentagon contractor appointments (Tier 2)
- Roll Call (Chrome) — FY2026 NDAA passage (Tier 2)
- Responsible Statecraft (Chrome) — DOD spending contractor percentage (Tier 2)

### Thin Profile Expansion — March 27, 2026 (thin-profile-expansion scheduled task, Run 2)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** thin-profile-expansion

#### Summary

Expanded **Ocean Conservancy** donor node (`topics/Donors & Power Networks/Dark Money/Ocean Conservancy.md`) from a 33-line thin stub (incorrectly marked `ready`) to a full ~195-line donor node with complete anatomy, covering the corporate capture pattern embedded in mainstream environmental nonprofit fundraising.

**Target selection:** All 23 raw nodes confirmed as redirect stubs (skipped per task instructions). Prioritized next item from prior session's queue: Ocean Conservancy (33 lines, thin ready). High-value target: the Stemming the Tide corporate capture scandal is a paradigm case for the vault's thesis (corporate donors control advocacy scope), and OC sits in the Dark Money folder with minimal content.

**Key findings — corporate capture structure:**
- Coca-Cola, Dow Chemical, and American Chemistry Council co-funded OC's 2015 "Stemming the Tide" report (coauthored with McKinsey & Company) and sat on its steering committee
- Report blamed Asian countries for 60% of ocean plastic; endorsed incineration; excluded production-side solutions — all positions favorable to corporate funders
- 200+ groups signed letter condemning the report within months; OC issued formal apology and retracted the report in July 2022 — 7 years later
- Corporate partnership structure (Trash Free Seas Alliance) remains: Dow and ACC retain steering committee seats; Coca-Cola remains a partner

**Key findings — federal grant dependence:**
- $14.2M+ in federal grants since 2008
- 2024 alone: $5.2M NOAA grant + $1.7M EPA grant (marine debris), both from agencies OC lobbies
- $620K lobbying spend in 2024, $680.7K in 2025 — targeting Congress and NOAA on budget and fisheries legislation

**Key findings — UN Plastics Treaty (INC-5, November 2024):**
- OC supports production limits and phasedowns — positions more progressive than its corporate partner relationships would suggest
- Treaty stalled at Busan negotiations; 200+ petrochemical industry lobbyists (including companies connected to OC's own funders) dominated INC-5
- OC's formal treaty position vs. its corporate funding model is the active contradiction the file documents

**Analytical patterns flagged:** Corporate Capture (Coca-Cola/Dow steering committee constrains advocacy scope), Both-Sides Illusion (OC publicly supports production limits while partnering with producers), Genuine Win + Structural Limit (30x30 marine protection advances; plastics production limits don't)

**Data method:**
- WebSearch only — Chrome unavailable this session
- OpenSecrets org profile (lobbying amounts, ID: D000064969) — Tier 1 (UNVERIFIED)
- ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer (IRS 990 data) — Tier 1 (UNVERIFIED)
- Plastics News + Waste Dive (Stemming the Tide retraction) — Tier 2 (UNVERIFIED)
- Packard Foundation grantee page — Tier 2 (UNVERIFIED)
- Trellis, Plastic Soup Foundation, InfluenceWatch, Ballotpedia — Tier 3 (UNVERIFIED)

### Donor Node Builder — March 27, 2026 (donor-node-builder scheduled task)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** donor-node-builder

#### Summary

Expanded **HBW Resources** donor node (`topics/Donors & Power Networks/Dark Money/HBW Resources.md`) from a 33-line thin stub (falsely marked `ready`) to a full 160-line donor node anatomy. Corrected an editorial error in the prior version — the file had incorrect Turkey/Qatar FARA tags that did not match the firm's actual practice.

**Target selection:** Scanned all raw/draft nodes in vault index — all 23 raw nodes confirmed as redirect stubs (functioning as intended, skipped). Shifted to thin "ready" files under 60 lines per prior session's thin-profile-expansion recommendation. Selected HBW Resources (33 lines, `ready`) as highest-impact target due to strong API/Chrome data availability and significant investigative journalism coverage.

**Key finding — editorial correction:** The prior HBW Resources note incorrectly described the firm as a FARA-registered foreign agent for Turkey and Qatar. OpenSecrets lobbying data, Senate LDA searches, and investigative journalism show HBW Resources is a **domestic energy lobbying firm** — no Turkey or Qatar FARA registrations documented. Tags and related links corrected accordingly.

**Key findings — HBW business model:**
- HBW founded Consumer Energy Alliance (CEA) in 2006 with BP, Chevron, ExxonMobil, Marathon, Shell, Statoil backing
- CEA has no independent staff — all representatives are HBW employees
- CEA has paid HBW $9.1M of $21.1M total revenue since 2010 (43% management fee)
- HBW also founded Western States and Tribal Nations (WSTN) — astroturfed tribal nation branding for LNG pipeline lobbying
- Jordan Cove LNG campaign: WSTN ran full-page ads in The Oregonian claiming tribal support while actual Oregon tribes (Klamath, Confederated Tribes of Coos) opposed the pipeline
- 2025 clients: Consumer Energy Alliance, Jicarilla Apache Nation ($360K), NorthWestern Corp, Western States & Tribal Nations — $720K total

**Data method:**
- OpenSecrets lobbying firm profile (Chrome) — annual client data 2018–2025 (Tier 1)
- OpenSecrets org summary — employee contributions 2024 (Tier 1)
- OpenSecrets lobbyists page — revolving door data 2024 (Tier 1)
- Street Roots investigative journalism — Jordan Cove WSTN astroturf story (Tier 2) (Chrome)
- DeSmog — Consumer Energy Alliance corporate funding analysis (Tier 2) (Chrome)
- Energy and Policy Institute — CEA investigative archive (Tier 2) (Chrome)
- Facing South — Drive to Drill (2015), Atlantic Coast Pipeline (2019) (Tier 2) (Chrome)
- SourceWatch — HBW Resources profile (Tier 3) (Chrome)

### Automated Story Discovery — March 26, 2026 (story-discovery scheduled task, run 6)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** story-discovery

#### What was built

Created `topics/Stories/Internal/Daily Updates/2026-03-26 Story Discovery Run 6.md` with 5 story leads across 4 vault sectors.

**Story leads discovered:**

1. **Diamond — Pharma's $8.8B Orphan Drug Loophole:** Congressional Republicans inserted orphan drug exemptions into the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that CBO estimates will cost Medicare $8.8 billion over a decade. PhRMA spent $20.6M on lobbying in H1 2025 and funneled $17.5M to American Action Network (dark money aligned with House GOP leadership). Keytruda, Opdivo, and Darzalex — blockbuster cancer drugs — shielded from price negotiation. Textbook Donor-Class Override with Dark Money Symmetry layer.

2. **Diamond — Fossil Fuel $219M → Billions in Returns:** Brennan Center documented the most explicit donor-to-policy pipeline in modern politics. $219M industry investment in 2024 cycle → 145+ environmental rollbacks in first 100 days, endangerment finding repeal (Feb 2026), $18B in tax incentives, LNG export approvals. Energy Transfer Partners CEO Kelcy Warren's personal wealth grew ~10% after project approval. Trump explicitly described it as a "deal."

3. **Gold — Defense Contractor Buyback Crackdown:** Trump EO (Jan 7, 2026) prohibits underperforming defense contractors from buybacks/dividends. Warren-Hawley bipartisan bill to codify. But defense industry spent $184M on donations over past decade; 7/10 top House recipients sit on Armed Services Committee. Question: real enforcement or theater?

4. **Silver — PE Healthcare FTC Retreat:** Trump FTC retreating from Biden-era private equity healthcare scrutiny. Maine passed first explicit PE hospital ownership ban (June 2025). Federal→state enforcement shift pattern.

5. **Bronze — Armed Services Committee Saturation:** Background data reinforcing defense donor saturation strategy — 7/10 top House, 6/10 top Senate recipients on Armed Services.

### Automated Donor Node Expansion — March 26, 2026 (donor-node-builder scheduled task)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** donor-node-builder

#### What was built

Expanded **2 thin donor nodes** from 33-line stubs marked "ready" to fully developed profiles with Chrome-verified sources and class analysis.

**1. Boeing Defense** (`topics/Donors & Power Networks/Defense & Intelligence/Boeing Defense.md`)
- **Before:** 33 lines, 3 sources, missing Who They Fund, What They've Gotten, Revolving Door, Class Analysis
- **After:** 134 lines, 13 Chrome-verified sources (5 Tier 1, 5 Tier 2, 1 Tier 3 + 2 existing)
- **Status change:** ready → developed (downgraded — thin "ready" file was non-compliant; now has substantive content but needs editorial review)
- **Key findings:** Boeing's PAC gives to 260+ House members in a single cycle (saturation strategy). 70% of Boeing's 114 lobbyists are former government officials. KC-46 tanker program has $9.55B in cost overruns on a $4.9B contract but continues. Boeing won $20B F-47 NGAD contract in March 2025 with cost-plus structure (government absorbs risk). 737 MAX criminal case dismissed Nov 2025 despite 346 deaths — ultimate too-big-to-fail protection.
- **Analytical patterns:** Donor-Class Override (defense contracts continue despite catastrophic failures), Revolving Door (Patrick Shanahan DOD-to-Boeing, Heidi Grant DSCA-to-Boeing, Nikki Haley board appointment), Both-Sides Illusion (62/37 R/D PAC split ensures bipartisan protection)

**2. Small Dollar Donors - ActBlue** (`topics/Donors & Power Networks/Dark Money/Small Dollar Donors - ActBlue.md`)
- **Before:** 33 lines, 3 sources, missing What They Want, Who They Fund detail, timeline, contradiction, Class Analysis
- **After:** 110 lines, 7 Chrome-verified sources (3 Tier 1, 2 Tier 2, 2 Tier 3)
- **Status change:** ready → developed (downgraded — thin "ready" file was non-compliant; now has substantive content)
- **Key findings:** ActBlue processed $3.82B in 2024 cycle (more than double WinRed's $1.8B). Platform makes $0 in direct PAC contributions — pure conduit. 3.95% fee on $3.82B = ~$151M in platform revenue. Harris launch generated $81M in 24 hours (July 2024). Republican investigations from TX AG Paxton, VA AG Miyares, and House Admin Committee alleging donor fraud. ActBlue implemented CVV verification in response.
- **Analytical patterns:** Genuine Win + Structural Limit (small-dollar democratization is real but the platform itself is a private monopoly), Both-Sides Illusion (ActBlue vs WinRed creates appearance of grassroots competition but both platforms centralize control)

### Automated Publication Audit — March 26, 2026 (publish-audit Run 1)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** publish-audit

#### What was audited

Full pass over the 181 files approved in the Publication Manifest (Session 32f). Vault at 1,248 unique .md files at time of audit.

**Audit method:** Python script walked all vault files (excluding Media & Influence Pipeline, Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure, Lobbying Firms & K Street). Checked each approved file for: YAML content-readiness, tag presence, (URL NEEDED)/(UNVERIFIED) citations, wikilinks to unpublished files, formatting violations (H1/H2 headers, bold section headers), and links to excluded sections.

**Key findings:**

1. **16 Trump sub-notes: Manifest filename mismatch.** All 16 files listed with abbreviated titles in the Manifest have been renamed to longer, more descriptive titles in the vault since March 22. All 16 are `ready`. The Manifest needs updating before publishing these files. Full correction table in Publication Audit Report.

2. **8 files: (URL NEEDED) citations.** Per Quality Standards, these cannot be `ready`. Affected files: Alaska 2026 Senate Race, COVID No-Bid Contracts, Corporate Subsidies (Newsom), Florida 2026 Special Senate Election, Immigration Donors and Backers (Newsom), New Hampshire 2026 Senate Race, San Quentin COVID Disaster, Sanctuary State SB 54.

3. **CalRx note: backslash-escaped wikilinks.** `[[Blue Shield of California\]]`, `[[UnitedHealth Group - Optum\]]`, `[[Anthem - Elevance Health\]]` contain malformed backslash characters that will break in Obsidian Publish.

4. **4 profiles previously flagged as missing tags: now resolved.** Newsom, Trump, Vance, Buttigieg all have `content-readiness: ready` in YAML and inline hashtags present. Manifest notes are stale.

5. **Pervasive dead-link warnings.** 144 of 155 audited files contain wikilinks to unpublished donor nodes. Top targets: Elon Musk (12+ files), AIPAC (10+ files), Koch Network (8+ files), CCPOA (7+ files), Leonard Leo (5+), Peter Thiel (5+). Not errors — expected structural gap. Publish top 10 donors before launch to resolve majority.

6. **Cross-Politician Contradiction Map links to internal docs.** Wikilinks to `[[Research Methodology and Data Sources]]` and `[[Session Timeline]]` in an approved file — both held from publication. Convert to plain text before publishing.

7. **Epstein exclusion confirmed clean.** No Epstein vault files in audit scope. No approved files link into excluded sections.

- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Session Timeline.md` — this entry

### Automated Media Profile Builder — March 26, 2026 (media-profile-builder Run 2)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** media-profile-builder

#### What was built

Built `Hasan Piker.md` — the 10th media pipeline profile and the 4th in the Left category. Profile status: `draft`.

**Key research findings:**
- Funding model is entirely platform-dependent (no dark money, no institutional patron, no identified corporate backer)
- 2021 Twitch data breach confirmed $2,810,480 in payouts Aug 2019–Oct 2021 (~$1.5M/year from subscriptions alone); 4th highest-earning Twitch streamer Sep 2021 at $210,946 in a single month
- FEC search: **zero federal campaign contributions** under "Hasan Piker" or "Hasan Dogan Piker" across all election cycles — notable for a multi-millionaire with 2.8M followers and documented electoral influence
- 2024 Election Night: 7.5M total viewers, 313K peak concurrent — only explicitly left-wing streamer in top 10
- Amazon (Twitch parent) takes ~50% of subscription revenue — structural contradiction: socialist content generates revenue for Bezos proportional to political influence
- March 2026: joined Nuestra América Convoy to Cuba with Jeremy Corbyn — most recent off-platform action
- $2.7M West Hollywood home (Aug 2021) and Porsche Taycan sparked national "champagne socialist" controversy

**Class analysis angle (non-standard for this section):** Piker is NOT a donor-capture case. He is a **platform capture / containment** case — his political energy and his audience's political energy are metabolized into Twitch subscriptions. Amazon benefits from his socialist content without directing it. This is the most structurally sophisticated form of corporate content management: the platform requires no management because the audience capture model does the work.

**12 Chrome-verified sources** — all VALID, 0 BROKEN. All logged in Source URL Audit Log.

### Automated Lobbying Firm Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (lobbying-firm-builder Run 3)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Revenue | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|---------|-----------|
| `Squire Patton Boggs.md` | draft (NEW) | $23.5M (2025) | Tommy Boggs K Street legacy via merger; 64.8% revolving door (highest in cohort); Esper/SecDef hire; Saudi Khashoggi retention (2016–2021); RSS-FARA maneuver; appropriations + transportation access specialists |

#### What was built

Created `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/Squire Patton Boggs.md` — full lobbying firm profile following the Framework anatomy. 10 Chrome-verified sources (Tier 1–3). Content-readiness: draft.

**Key analytical findings:**

- **The revolving door rate of 64.8% is the highest in the cohort.** 35 of 54 registered lobbyists (2023) are former government employees. This means the firm's majority operation is ex-official access rather than substantive policy expertise. The firm sells former official relationships, not legal work.
- **Mark Esper's advisory-not-lobbying status reveals the revolving door's highest tier.** A former Secretary of Defense who won't file lobby disclosures but "advises" multinational corporations on national security policy is exercising the same function as a registered lobbyist at a higher access tier with zero public accountability.
- **The Saudi-Khashoggi retention is the vault's clearest ethics-subordinated-to-retainer example.** Squire Patton Boggs continued providing legal services to the Saudi entity whose head was directly implicated in orchestrating Jamal Khashoggi's murder for 35 months after the killing. The client was dropped only after advocacy pressure from the organization Khashoggi himself had founded. The fee structure did not change; the reputational pressure did.
- **The RSS-FARA maneuver reveals the firm's transparency floor.** Registered India's Hindu nationalist paramilitary (RSS) under the Lobbying Disclosure Act rather than the Foreign Agents Registration Act — routing the engagement through an intermediary (One+ Strategies / State Street Strategies). Legal scholars flagged FARA applicability; the firm chose the less transparent option. Terminated after investigative journalism.
- **Squire Patton Boggs is primarily an appropriations machine, not a pharma or defense lobby.** #1 issue area: Federal Budget & Appropriations (164 reports, 46 clients). #2: Transportation (91 reports, 30 clients). The firm's client base includes dozens of cities, transit systems, and counties seeking federal grants — a different model from the corporate sector lobbying firms.
- **Revenue correction:** The Framework listed ~$38M/yr as estimated revenue. Actual OpenSecrets data: $22.66M (2023), $23.5M (2025). The estimate was significantly above actual figures. Profile uses verified data.

#### URL verification

10 URLs Chrome-verified VALID. 0 BROKEN. All logged to Source URL Audit Log.

**VALID (all 10):** OpenSecrets lobbying summary (D000067299), OpenSecrets lobbyists 2023 (64.8% revolving door data), OpenSecrets issues 2023 (#1: appropriations), Bloomberg Law Esper hire, The Hill McGovern/DHS hire, Bloomberg Law Assange/Wau Holland, Prism Reports RSS lobbying hired, Prism Reports RSS terminated, Middle East Eye Khashoggi/Saudi contract ended, Wikipedia Squire Patton Boggs.

#### Files created/modified

- `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/Squire Patton Boggs.md` — NEW (draft, 10 Chrome-verified sources, Tier 1–3)
- `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/_Lobbying Firms Index.md` — Squire Patton Boggs updated (not started → draft), count updated (2 → 3 profiles), stats updated (2 draft → 3 draft)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` — "Lobbying Firms — Squire Patton Boggs" section added (10 VALID, 0 BROKEN)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Session Timeline.md` — this entry

#### Research gaps flagged

- **Thomas Andrews background:** Former Democratic Rep. Maine 1st District (1991-1995) confirmed as former MoC lobbyist at the firm. His specific lobbying outcomes for DJI Technology (Chinese drone co. with national security scrutiny) warrant a dedicated entry once a China-adjacent donor network node is built.
- **Revenue estimate correction flagged:** Framework doc lists "~$38M/yr" for Squire Patton Boggs. Actual 2023 and 2025 data is $22–23.5M. The Framework estimate overstates revenue by ~60%. Framework should be updated if accurate firm comparison data is needed.
- **PAC contribution split:** The ~54% Democrat / 44% Republican PAC split cited is from pre-2020 OpenSecrets data (from web research). Current-cycle breakdown should be verified directly from OpenSecrets PAC profile (C00401083) before promoting to `developed`.
- **WikiLeaks/Assange lobbying outcome:** The plea agreement (June 2024) followed the Wau Holland lobbying — but the causal relationship between the $1.2M lobbying and the DOJ outcome is correlation, not proven causation. The timeline table notes this analytically.

#### Next lobbying-firm-builder priority

Run 4: **Capitol Counsel** — midsize firm with extremely high revolving door percentage; priority #4 in build queue.
