### Automated Lobbying Firm Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (lobbying-firm-builder Run 4)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Revenue | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|---------|-----------|
| `Capitol Counsel.md` | draft (NEW) | $25.4M (2025) | 73.7% revolving door (highest in cohort); Kott/Manchin orbit → pharma+fossil fuel lobbying; Roberts (Senate Ag + Intel Chair) joins weeks after retirement; Kardon/17yr Wyden CoS; McCrery/Ways & Means Ranking Member; Finance Committee access machine |

#### What was built

Created `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/Capitol Counsel.md` — full lobbying firm profile following the Framework anatomy. 13 Chrome-verified sources (Tier 1–3). Content-readiness: draft.

**Key analytical findings:**

- **73.7% revolving door rate — highest in the cohort.** 28 of 38 registered lobbyists (2024) are former government employees. Compared to Squire Patton Boggs (64.8%), Akin Gump (55.4%), Brownstein Hyatt (53.1%). Capitol Counsel's core product is literally former government employees renting out their former relationships.
- **Jonathan Kott is the vault's clearest documented revolving door access pipeline.** Left Manchin's office → ran $12M dark money operation (Big Tent Project Fund) crushing Bernie Sanders → returned to Coons's office → joined Capitol Counsel June 2021 → registered to lobby ExxonMobil, AFPM, Healthcare Leadership Council within weeks. The three industries Manchin was protecting during Build Back Better obstruction. Documented by The Lever, CNBC, Jacobin.
- **Pat Roberts joined within weeks of retiring** after 24 years in the Senate (chaired Agriculture AND Intelligence). The revolving door timing is not metaphorical — it is literally weeks. Major clients include Lockheed Martin and Comcast.
- **Revenue correction:** The Framework listed ~$15M/yr as estimated revenue. Actual OpenSecrets data: $25.4M (2025), $25.8M (2024). The estimate understated actual revenue by ~40%. Profile uses verified data.
- **Finance Committee concentration is the firm's strategic differentiator.** Raffaelli (former Bentsen/Finance counsel), Finley (Baucus/Finance political director), Kardon (Wyden/Finance CoS), McCrery (Ways & Means Ranking Member), Gold (Senate floor procedure) give the firm unmatched coverage of the two tax-writing committees. PhRMA, pharma companies, Investment Co Institute, and banking clients cluster around this access.
- **The bipartisan model is a deliberate business architecture.** Not ideological — strategic. Each major hire mapped to a specific relationship gap: Republican Ways and Means (McCrery, 2009), Democratic Finance Committee (Kardon, 2014), Senate floor procedure (Gold, 2014), Manchin/moderate Dem obstruction orbit (Kott, 2021), Agriculture/Intelligence (Roberts, 2021).

#### URL verification

13 URLs Chrome-verified VALID. 0 BROKEN. All logged to Source URL Audit Log.

**VALID (all 13):** OpenSecrets lobbying summary (D000032306), OpenSecrets lobbyists 2024 (73.7% revolving door data), OpenSecrets issues 2024 (health #1), The Lever (Kott/Manchin Aide), The Hill (Inside a K Street success story), Washington Post (Capitol Counsel shot to top of K Street 2013), Roll Call (Wyden World/Kardon hire), The Hill (Pat Roberts retirement to lobbying), CNBC (Manchin aides lobby for oil/pharma), PR Newswire (Kott hire announcement), PR Newswire (Roberts hire announcement), Jacobin (Manchin aide corporate lobbyist), Wikipedia (John D. Raffaelli).

### Automated Lobbying Firm Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (lobbying-firm-builder Run 5)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Revenue | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|---------|-----------|
| `BGR Group.md` | draft (NEW) | $71.5M (2025) | 86.1% revolving door (highest in cohort); David Urban/Trump 2016+2024 access = 58% revenue surge in 2025; Haley Barbour/RNC Chair founding; Sean Duffy revolving door (CNN pundit → BGR lobbyist → Transportation Secretary); extensive FARA foreign govt client roster |

#### What was built

Created `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/BGR Group.md` — full lobbying firm profile following the Framework anatomy. 12 Chrome-verified sources (Tier 1–3). Content-readiness: draft.

**Key analytical findings:**

- **86.1% revolving door rate — highest in the cohort.** 31 of 36 registered lobbyists (2024) are former government employees. This surpasses Capitol Counsel (73.7%), Squire Patton Boggs (64.8%), Akin Gump (55.4%), Brownstein Hyatt (53.1%). BGR is structurally the most revolving-door-dependent major firm in the vault's tracked cohort.
- **The 58% revenue surge in 2025 is the clearest dollar measurement of Trump access in the vault.** BGR went from $45.1M (2024) to $71.5M (2025) — a $26.4M increase in a single year. Managing Director David Urban was a senior advisor to Trump's 2016 and 2024 campaigns. Corporate clients paid a market premium for what Urban's Trump relationship was worth in the first year of the new administration.
- **Haley Barbour's founding represents the Republican establishment's original revolving door institutionalization.** Reagan Political Director → RNC Chairman → BGR founding partner → Mississippi Governor → return to BGR lobbying. The man who built the Republican Party's 1994 "government is the problem" messaging infrastructure built the most effective government-access brokerage in Washington. The anti-government ideologue became the government-access salesman.
- **Sean Duffy is the vault's most documented sequential revolving door.** Congressman → CNN political commentator (while simultaneously registered BGR lobbyist) → BGR partner lobbying airline industry against Trump's stated consumer protection positions → confirmed Transportation Secretary November 2024. One person. Five years. Congress → media → lobbying → cabinet.
- **The foreign government FARA portfolio reveals BGR's most analytically significant function.** Azerbaijan, India, Kurdistan Regional Government, Bahrain ($1M+ in 2021), Saudi Arabia (dropped post-Khashoggi) — these are not democratic states seeking honest policy representation. They are purchasing U.S. defense relationships, arms sales approvals, and diplomatic protection through the same revolving door mechanism used for domestic corporate clients.
- **Revenue correction:** The Framework listed ~$18M/yr for BGR. Actual 2025 OpenSecrets data: $71.5M; 2024: $45.1M. Revenue dramatically exceeds the Framework estimate. Profile uses verified data.

#### URL verification

12 URLs Chrome-verified VALID. 0 BROKEN. All logged to Source URL Audit Log.

**VALID (all 12):** OpenSecrets BGR lobbying summary (D000021679, 2025: $71.5M), OpenSecrets BGR lobbyists 2024 (86.1% revolving door), OpenSecrets BGR issues lobbied 2024, OpenSecrets Sean Duffy revolving door profile, OpenSecrets News $5B lobbying record 2025, OpenSecrets News Trump-aligned firms revenue surge, OpenSecrets News foreign govts/defense lobbying (Bahrain $1M+), Responsible Statecraft (revolving door to dictators analysis), Newsweek (Duffy lobbied against Trump positions), Media Matters (Duffy CNN/BGR conflict of interest), Jackson Free Press (BGR/Mississippi Ukraine-Russia), Wikipedia BGR Group.

#### What was built

Created `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Right/Charlie Kirk.md` — full media influence profile following the Framework anatomy. 18 Chrome-verified sources (Tier 1–3). Content-readiness: **developed**.

**Promoted to developed (not draft):** All 18 sources Chrome-verified VALID, 4 Tier 1 government records (ProPublica 990 explorer, FEC PAC record, two OpenSecrets records), 6 required sections present, Format 3 timeline table with 10 rows, 3 `[!money]` callouts, 1 `[!contradiction]` callout, full class analysis section.

**Key contextual finding:** Charlie Kirk was assassinated September 10, 2025 at Utah Valley University while hosting a campus debate. The shooter, Tyler Robinson, 22, was charged with aggravated murder. Kirk's widow Erika Kirk was named TPUSA CEO September 18, 2025. The profile covers Kirk's historical role and the continuing organization — the donor-class infrastructure survived his death intact.

#### Key analytical findings

- **The donor dependency chain is unusually clean.** TPUSA raised ~$389–400M under Kirk, but the revenue came overwhelmingly from mega-donor foundations (Bradley Impact Fund $23.6M, Wayne Duddlesten Foundation $13.1M, DonorsTrust $4M+) rather than organic grassroots giving. Kirk's salary grew from $27K to $390K as the donor money flowed. The "grassroots campus activism" branding was built on top-down institutional funding from the start.
- **The Wayne Duddlesten $13.1M was unreported until September 2025.** A Forbes investigation published the same week as Kirk's assassination revealed a previously undisclosed Texas real estate donor as one of TPUSA's largest individual foundation backers. This is DonorsTrust-style opacity without a DAF vehicle — a private foundation with no public fundraising.
- **Jan 6 as content-to-action case study.** Kirk announced 80+ buses to the Stop the Steal rally, sent 7. Julie Fancelli (Publix heiress) gave $1.25M to fund them. Kirk paid Kimberly Guilfoyle $60K to speak. Kirk then pleaded the Fifth before the Jan 6 committee. The media personality's platform became direct election-denial infrastructure.
- **The post-assassination donor reaction confirms the infrastructure thesis.** Newsweek reported TPUSA received a surge in donations and chapter signups after Kirk was killed. The brand was replaceable; the donor class had built an institution, not a personality cult.
- **Salem Media connection adds the corporate radio layer.** Kirk's radio show reached 195+ stations with 500+ affiliates — built on top of TPUSA infrastructure, not despite it. Salem's Christian conservative ownership created content alignment; Kirk got a national radio audience that pre-existing TPUSA chapters had primed to receive him.

- **The revolving door is ideological, not transactional.** PSA's founders don't lobby — they provide the Democratic donor class with something more durable: an authoritative media voice that channels progressive frustration back into establishment party politics. - **Soros Fund Management's board seat makes PSA structurally accountable to the Democratic megadonor class.** First outside financing Crooked Media took. Soros Fund Management is building a progressive audio empire (Semafor, April 2024). PSA is the flagship. Editorial independence structurally compromised.

- **The union fight is the class analysis in concrete form.** 61 WGAE workers spent 13+ months getting a first contract from a company that tells millions of listeners to organize. Company filed motions to exclude workers from bargaining unit (NLRB ULP charge). Contract ratified only after one-day walkout.
- **The Gaza editorial failure revealed the structural limit.** Staff demanded critical coverage of Biden's weapons supply to Israel. Hosts resisted. Coverage tracked Democratic talking points.

#### Key analytical findings

- **Both-Sides Illusion at sector scale.** Lockheed gave $2.47M to Democrats and $2.13M to Republicans in 2024. RTX gave $1.54M to Democrats and $1.28M to Republicans. The industry funds both parties because it needs whoever wins to believe defense spending is a civic obligation.
- **The investment is surgical.** Campaign contributions flow to Armed Services and Appropriations committee chairs: Ken Calvert (Defense Appropriations, $835K), Mike Rogers (Armed Services Chair, $535K), Rob Wittman (Vice Chair, $499K). The money funds the overseers.
- **Think tank capture is the most underreported mechanism.** $32M to DC think tanks 2019-2023. Atlantic Council received $2.53M from contractors in 2024 alone. The "mainstream" range of defense policy analysis is defined by think tanks funded by the people who build the weapons.
- **The F-35 as cost-overrun business model.** $1.7T lifetime cost, 10 years late, 80% over budget. Lockheed paid $1.7B in mid-2025 despite 50% aircraft readiness. Suppliers in 45 states — political distribution, not engineering.
- **Five consecutive audit failures, zero budget consequences.** Pentagon has failed its financial audit every year since 2018. Congress increased the budget every year. The industry's political operation is more powerful than oversight mechanisms.
- **$2.4T in contracts 2020-2024.** 54% of all DOD spending. The political investment buys the size of the market itself.

#### Key analytical findings

- **Hollywood's political value is cultural, not merely financial.** TV/Movies/Music industry PAC contributions: $3.59M in 2024 (OpenSecrets). But the real value is the fundraising event infrastructure Katzenberg built — $15M LA weekend (Dec 2023), $30M Clooney-Roberts event (Jun 2024, record), $28M Harris fundraiser (Sep 2024). No other donor bloc can organize events at this scale.
- **Katzenberg as the Democratic Party's Hollywood logistics operation.** 1,776 individual federal contributions since 1979. Obama's top bundler ($6.6M combined). Biden 2024 campaign co-chair. When Katzenberg privately warned Biden cash was drying up (July 2024), it was a power signal — the Hollywood money-man telling the president his coalition was fracturing. Biden withdrew weeks later.
- **Saban is the vault's cleanest example of explicit single-issue donor control.** "I'm a one-issue guy and my issue is Israel." $16M to Democrats in 2016. $7M to Priorities USA (Clinton super PAC). $13M founding grant to Brookings Saban Center (installed former AIPAC deputy director Martin Indyk as founding director). Directly emailed Democratic senators who raised Gaza concerns. Called Bernie Sanders "a self-hating Jew." Biden's Israel policy described by Saban himself as "pristine."
- **The Saban Center is think tank capture as donor infrastructure.** $13M founding grant placed Saban's foreign policy preferences inside Brookings — Washington's most prestigious centrist think tank — for 20+ years. The Saban Forum produces annual policy output that shapes Democratic foreign policy consensus. This is the most cost-effective lever in the vault: $13M buys 20 years of institutional influence-shaping.
- **The "Dembargo" episode reveals the structural limit of donor control.** Some Hollywood donors paused giving over Gaza in 2024 — a rare moment where the cultural-progressive wing of the donor class conflicted with the Israel-lobby wing. Saban enforced the Israel-lobby position; Katzenberg managed the transition to Harris. The fracture was temporary; the infrastructure survived.
- **MPA lobbying machine ($3.1M/2024) is the industry's regulatory capture arm.** Top priorities: copyright extension, site-blocking legislation, AI training data licensing. The entertainment industry's revenue model depends on IP monopolies that require continuous legislative reinforcement.

Goldman Sachs - Wall Street Titan selected: first in queue. Pre-expansion audit confirmed: YAML type incorrectly set to `story` (should be `sub-note`), Ballotpedia source was a self-referential broken URL, only 2 sources total, content-readiness fraudulently marked `ready`. The companion Goldman Sachs.md node is already developed at 182 lines covering regulatory/financial angles; this expansion focuses on the revolving door / alumni network angle — complementary not duplicative.

#### What was expanded

| File | Before | After | Status Change |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|
| `Donors & Power Networks/Wall Street/Goldman Sachs - Wall Street Titan.md` | 32 lines, 2 sources (1 broken Ballotpedia), type: story, content-readiness: ready (fraudulent) | 153 lines, 9 Chrome-verified sources (8 VALID, 1 BROKEN removed), type: sub-note, parent: Goldman Sachs | fraudulent `ready` → `developed` |

#### Key analytical findings

- **The revolving door rate peaks at 88% in 2016 — highest among all full-service investment banks.** Goldman led Citigroup, Bank of America, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Wells Fargo in the percentage of lobbyists who previously held government jobs. By 2023: 86.67% (26/30); by 2024: 71.87% (23/32). The lobbying operation is structurally dependent on former government employees renting out prior relationships.
- **The Government Sachs alumni pattern spans every administration 1993–2021.** Robert Rubin (Clinton Treasury Secretary), Hank Paulson (Bush Treasury Secretary), Mark Patterson (Obama Treasury CoS), Steve Mnuchin (Trump Treasury Secretary), Gary Cohn (Trump NEC Director), Dina Powell (Trump Deputy NSA), Steve Bannon (Trump chief strategist). The Both-Sides Illusion operates at the apex of state power: Goldman placed alumni at Treasury under both parties simultaneously across 30 years.
- **The Paulson-TARP connection is the vault's clearest documented regulatory capture case.** Goldman's CEO-turned-Treasury-Secretary administered a $700B bailout from which Goldman received $10B in TARP funds and $12.9B from the AIG rescue — while holding approximately 24 calls with Goldman's sitting CEO Blankfein around the AIG announcement. The conflict was documented; no action was taken.
- **The Gary Cohn–tax cut pipeline is documented.** Goldman's ex-COO runs the NEC, leads the 2017 tax bill process, cuts the corporate rate from 35% to 21%, and Goldman's corporate tax savings in year one were estimated at $1B+. Cohn resigned after the bill passed. The policy return on his two-year government placement was material and immediate.
- **The $5.06B DOJ settlement (2016) and $550M SEC ABACUS settlement (2010) represent the cost of doing business, not a deterrent.** Goldman's profits from the mortgage securities and CDO products these settlements covered exceeded settlement amounts by orders of magnitude. Neither settlement required Goldman to admit criminal wrongdoing (ABACUS) or produced individual prosecutions (DOJ). The political operation that protected Goldman's regulatory environment enabled the fraud and minimized the consequences.
- **Ballotpedia source was a fabricated URL.** The existing stub cited `ballotpedia.org/Goldman_Sachs_-_Wall_Street_Titan` — confirmed BROKEN on Chrome load test. Title shows: "The page you're looking for does not exist." The URL was constructed from the vault note's own title rather than a real Ballotpedia article. Removed from profile; logged in Source URL Audit Log as BROKEN.

Created `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Right/Ben Shapiro.md` — full media influence profile following the Framework anatomy. 19 Chrome-verified sources. Content-readiness: draft.

**Note on draft vs. developed:** Profile is filed as `draft` because the OpenSecrets CID for Shapiro personally and any FEC PAC ID for The Daily Wire could not be confirmed this run. The profile is substantively complete (19 sources, 8 sections, timeline table, 4 callout blocks, full class analysis) but is missing primary government record sources (Tier 1). **Key analytical findings:**

- **Wilks-to-climate-denial pipeline is the vault's cleanest funder-to-content example.** Farris Wilks invests $4.7M in 2015, retains co-ownership, and The Daily Wire becomes a top-10 U.S. climate disinformation disseminator. No lobbying required — the content itself is the political product. $4.7M seed buys a 25-million-listener platform normalizing fossil fuel skepticism as conservative identity.
- **Wilks-PragerU-DW triangle is coordinated infrastructure, not coincidence.** $6.5M to PragerU (2013) → PragerU co-founder Prager becomes DW shareholder → PragerU's Allen Estrin introduces Shapiro/Boreing to Wilks employee Robinson → $4.7M seed round. Same fossil fuel family owns equity in both major Wilks-funded conservative media properties.
- **Owens firing reveals Israel > audience size in editorial hierarchy.** Owens had substantial audience; Shapiro fired her anyway when Gaza positions crossed his line. Editorial control overrides audience capture logic on this specific issue.
- **2025 financial pressure exposes Independence Theater limits.** $200M revenue claim alongside 25% layoffs, bankruptcy attorney, and Milken Conference investor search reveals structural tensions: outrage content maximizes subscriber radicalization but constrains Fortune 500 ad relationships; kids programming $100M commitment abandoned in 3 years; Wilks co-ownership creates ideological constraints new investors may not share.
- **"Facts don't care about your feelings" is the intellectual legitimizer function.** Shapiro provides epistemological cover for donor-class policy preferences by framing them as objective conclusions. The brand works because the audience doesn't recognize the funding model set the intellectual parameters.

### Automated Donor Node Builder — March 25, 2026 (Run 1)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** donor-node-builder

#### Queue assessment

Scanned all 13 files with `content-readiness: raw` in `Donors & Power Networks/`. **All 13 are redirect stubs** — thin wikilink redirectors pointing to canonical donor nodes elsewhere in the vault. No substantive raw nodes exist for expansion.

Moved to Priority 2: thin "ready" nodes under 60 lines that are not redirects. Identified 30+ thin stubs at 31-32 lines. Selected **Insurance Industry** (32 lines, 36 vault references — highest connectivity among non-redirect thin nodes) for full expansion.

#### What was expanded

| File | Before | After | Status Change |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|
| `Donors & Power Networks/Healthcare/Insurance Industry.md` | 32 lines, 2 sources, Who They Are section only | ~190 lines, 16 Chrome-verified sources, all required sections | ready (thin) → developed |

#### Key analytical findings

- **The ACA as industry achievement, not industry defeat.** The insurance industry initially opposed the ACA, then shaped it to entrench private insurance as the delivery mechanism. Post-ACA, the five largest health insurers saw stock prices increase 300-800%. The "reform" delivered 20 million new subsidized customers to private plans.
- **Medicare Advantage as extraction machine.** MA plans were paid $84B more than traditional Medicare would have cost in 2025 — a 120% overpayment. When the government attempted to audit and recoup, industry lobbying killed the enforcement mechanism entirely.
- **AHIP battle plan leaked.** 2018 Intercept investigation revealed the "Partnership for America's Health Care Future" coalition strategy to "disqualify government-run health care as a politically viable solution" — coalition talking points became mainstream Democratic position on healthcare.
- **UnitedHealth's 50-lobbyist bipartisan revolving door.** Former staffers from both parties' leadership (Bush, Trump, Cruz, Cornyn, Scalise, Biden) now translate industry priorities into legislative language.
- **Both-Sides Illusion at sector scale.** Industry funded both Baucus (who wrote the ACA) and Grassley (who opposed it) simultaneously — the outcome was guaranteed either way.

#### URL verification

- **16 URLs verified via Chrome browser load test** — all VALID
- All logged to Source URL Audit Log

#### Files modified

- `topics/Donors & Power Networks/Healthcare/Insurance Industry.md` — expanded (32 → ~190 lines, 2 → 16 sources, ready thin → developed)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` — added 16 verified URLs
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Session Timeline.md` — this entry

Full citation pass on individual dollar figures would be needed for `ready` promotion. Several wikilinks (e.g., `[[Affordable Care Act]]`, `[[Insurance Industry Bloc]]`) may not resolve — need verification.

### Automated Election Cycle Updater — March 25, 2026 (Run 1)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** election-cycle-updater

#### Profiles checked (4)

| Profile | Action | Data Changed? |
|---------|--------|--------------|
| [[_Jon Ossoff Master Profile]] (GA Senate 2026) | Q4 2025 FEC filings research, Republican challenger COH data | YES — significant update |
| [[_Antonio Villaraigosa Master Profile]] (CA Gov 2026) | CA SOS filings check, debate cancellation | MINOR — debate story added |
| [[_Xavier Becerra Master Profile]] (CA Gov 2026) | CA SOS filings check, debate cancellation | MINOR — debate story added |
| [[_Katie Porter Master Profile]] (CA Gov 2026) | Fairshake PAC activity check | NO CHANGE |

#### Key findings

**Jon Ossoff — Major Fundraising Update:**
Profile had "$12M by 2026" — actual Q4 2025 FEC data shows $25M+ COH entering 2026, the most of any competitive incumbent senator (R or D). Q4 2025: $9.9M raised from 303,000+ donations at $37 average, 99% unitemized (under $200). Donations from 157/159 Georgia counties. Republican challengers (Collins, Carter, Dooley) combined have ~$8M COH vs. Ossoff's $25M. Trump has not endorsed any Republican challenger. This is the clearest small-dollar defensive mobilization pattern in the vault — not tech/entertainment donor investment, but anti-MAGA insurance purchases from 303,000 small-dollar donors.

**CA Governor Debate Cancellation — March 24-25, 2026:**
USC/KABC co-sponsored debate excluded all four candidates of color (Villaraigosa, Becerra, Yee, Thurmond) via polling/fundraising threshold criteria that advantaged the six white candidates. Nine state lawmakers (including Assembly Speaker Rivas and Senate President Pro Tem Limón) formally objected; USC cancelled the debate hours before it was scheduled. Analytically significant: debate criteria function as structural mechanisms that compound existing racial wealth gaps in political fundraising into barriers to earned media access. Becerra's exclusion is particularly telling — he has the highest COH in the field ($3.8M) but it came from transfers, not current donor enthusiasm, so it doesn't meet the polling threshold. Cash accumulated over 24 years cannot purchase debate access.

**Katie Porter / Fairshake:**
No confirmed new Fairshake spending in the governor's race. War chest still estimated ~$116M+ (post-2024 cycle). No announcement of governor's race targeting found. Profile current.

#### Stories flagged

Two Gold stories created in `topics/Stories/Daily Updates/2026-03-25 Election Cycle Update.md`:
1. **Ossoff's $25M Small-Dollar Dominance** — Anti-MAGA defensive mobilization replaces donor-class bundling; structural question of whether small-dollar defensive giving changes political behavior
2. **CA Debate Exclusion Structural Filter** — Polling/fundraising thresholds as structural mechanism compounding racial wealth gaps into earned media exclusion

Georgia Recorder (Ossoff $25M), CBS Atlanta (Ossoff $25M), The Hill (Ossoff war chest), Newsweek (Ossoff fundraising), Ossoff campaign press release (Q4 small-dollar data), FEC Ossoff candidate page (S8GA00180), OpenSecrets Ossoff (CID N00040675 confirmed correct), NBC News (CA debate cancelled), OPB (CA debate cancelled), CalMatters Opinion (CA debate), CalMatters (CA Governor fundraising reports — distinct from broken `/california-governors-race-funding/` already in audit log).

### Automated Media Profile Build — March 25, 2026 (media-profile-builder Run 2)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** media-profile-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| `Tucker Carlson.md` | developed (NEW) | Fox → 1789 Capital seed round → TCN subscription → investor buyout; Dominion texts; Putin interview; Independence Theater pattern |

#### What was built

Created `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Right/Tucker Carlson.md` — full media influence profile following the Framework anatomy. 14 Chrome-verified sources. Content-readiness: developed.

**Key analytical findings:**

- **Independence Theater at maximum scale.** Carlson's post-Fox narrative is that he escaped corporate control to become a truth-teller. The receipts show: 1789 Capital seed round funded by Rebekah Mercer (Breitbart/Parler investor) and Omeed Malik (Trump donor) → same donor-class infrastructure, different letterhead. The investor buyout (June 2025) completes the theater — he now genuinely owns TCN, but the content has always served the interests of that donor class regardless of the org chart.

- **The Dominion discovery is the vault's most complete Two-Audience Problem documentation in media.** Carlson texted "Sidney Powell is lying," "I hate [Trump] passionately," called fraud claims "ludicrous" — while broadcasting all of it to 4 million nightly viewers. The gap between his private reality and his on-air performance is not incidental: it's the structural function of an audience-capture model where the host cannot moderate without losing the subscription base.

- **ALP nicotine pouches as audience-to-consumer pipeline.** The 50/50 joint venture with publicly traded Turning Point Brands converts Carlson's radicalized subscriber base into a consumer base for a nicotine product. This eliminates the advertiser intermediary entirely. When mainstream advertisers fled (2018-2020), ideologically aligned advertisers replaced them. Now the audience IS the product pipeline, and Carlson earns on both ends — subscription ($9/mo) and brand revenue (ALP boosted Turning Point's nicotine division 63% to $70M in summer 2025).

- **Putin interview as Independence Theater endpoint.** 200M+ views on X normalized Putin as a truth-telling alternative to the American "regime" — on behalf of an audience whose economic interests have nothing to do with Russian foreign policy and everything to do with domestic class dynamics that Carlson systematically avoids.

| [[_John Cornyn Master Profile]] | 80 lines, 7 sources (thin stub marked "ready") | 149 lines, 11 sources | ready → developed | Full OpenSecrets 2019-2024 cycle data ($5.9M FIRE sector, $2.6M securities, $1.6M real estate, $1.4M oil & gas), Bipartisan Safer Communities Act deep-dive (Uvalde context, NRA dynamics, structural limits analysis), F-35/Lockheed Martin $5B+ secured funding documented, judicial nominee contribution pipeline ($44K+ from 12 nominees who all became judges), Cornyn-Cruz Both-Sides Illusion analysis, 2026 primary defense (Schwarzman, Rees-Jones, Davis backing), Format 1 donation-to-policy timeline table (4 sectors, 11 entries), 4 analytical patterns, 2 callout blocks |

#### URL verification

- **22 URLs verified via Chrome browser load test** (21 VALID, 1 BROKEN replaced)
- PBS Kushner Opportunity Zones article (`pbs.org/newshour/nation/kushner-linked-firm-targets-richer-areas...`) confirmed 404 — replaced with NBC News article in Tim Scott profile
- Texas Tribune Cornyn donors URL (`texastribune.org/cornyn-donors/`) confirmed 404 — replaced with 2020/12/03 dated article
- All verified URLs logged to Source URL Audit Log

#### Key findings

- **Tim Scott's Banking Committee chairmanship is the structural payoff.** $13M career from the FIRE sector, and he now chairs the committee regulating all of them. His 2024 presidential campaign ($45.8M from billionaire donors, <5% voter support) was not a viable presidential bid — it was a donor audition that resulted in the Banking Committee gavel. The crypto industry's $170M+ 2024 election spending maps directly to Scott's announced Banking Committee priority of "regulatory clarity" for digital assets.

- **Cornyn's judicial pipeline is underexplored.** The Roll Call investigation finding that Cornyn backed all 12 judicial nominees who contributed to his campaigns ($44K+ total), all of whom became federal judges, is a clean donor-to-bench revolving door case. This pattern needs a dedicated sub-note.

- **Both profiles were marked "ready" at 77-80 lines.** This confirms the vault's status inflation problem. Neither profile had donation-to-policy timeline tables, dataview queries, or sufficient sourcing to meet the `ready` promotion criteria. Both are now honestly graded as `developed`.

#### Editorial decisions

- Demoted both profiles from `ready` → `developed` — they were thin placeholders incorrectly marked ready. Honest grading: neither meets the `ready` gate (full citation pass, all wikilinks resolve, Format 1 tables, class analysis present). Both now meet `developed` criteria (50+ lines, 3+ sources, 3+ sections).
- Removed broken PBS URL from Tim Scott profile rather than keeping it with UNVERIFIED tag — the article is genuinely gone (404 confirmed).
- Used the corrected American Prospect URL (redirect from `prospect.org/economy/...` to `prospect.org/2023/04/21/...`).

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run

#### What was built

| Profile | Before | After | Status Change | Key Additions |

| [[Judicial Crisis Network]] | 57 lines, 4 sources (thin stub marked "ready") | 151 lines, 11 sources | ready → developed | Full IRS 990 financial history (FY2019-FY2024, peak $65.1M revenue FY2024), Leo network architecture mapped (Marble Freedom Trust $55.5M disbursement, Rule of Law Trust $202M war chest, CRC Advisors $6M consulting fees), $1.6B Barre Seid donation documented (Tripp Lite stock transfer, ~$400M tax avoidance), RAGA funding ($20.3M cumulative since 2014, top funder 2023-2024), Honest Elections Project voter suppression arm documented, Concord Fund rebrand timeline, 2024 cycle contributions ($4.04M — Truth & Courage PAC $2.5M, CLF $750K), compensation structure analysis ($0 executive comp, $749K to three "counsels"), revolving door (100% of lobbyists ex-government), donation-to-policy temporal mapping table (6 entries), 3 analytical patterns, 2 callout blocks, class analysis |
| [[Comcast - NBCUniversal]] | 59 lines, 5 sources (thin stub marked "ready") | 118 lines, 8 sources | ready → developed | OpenSecrets lobbying data ($13.9M in 2024), FEC PAC data (C00248716, $2.02M raised 2025-2026 cycle), dual-class share structure (Roberts family 33% voting power from ~1% economic ownership), broadband monopoly analysis (40% of zip codes single-provider), five-objective political agenda mapped (net neutrality, municipal broadband bans, BEAD subsidy capture, media ownership, ACP expiration), revolving door documented (Ajit Pai → CTIA CEO March 2025, Meredith Attwell Baker FCC → Comcast lobbyist 4 months after merger vote, David Cohen → US Ambassador), 16-state municipal broadband ban analysis, bipartisan PAC strategy (360 of 435 House members funded in 2016), donation-to-policy temporal mapping table (6 entries), 4 analytical patterns, 2 callout blocks, class analysis |

#### URL fixes in existing files

- `Judicial Crisis Network.md` — WaPo URL `washingtonpost.com/investigations/2023/04/27/leonard-leo-donation-dark-money/` returned "Page Not Found" → replaced with ProPublica Barre Seid article. Old OpenSecrets dark-money URL (`opensecrets.org/dark-money/recipient?id=JCN`) replaced with org summary URL (D000026924).
- `Comcast - NBCUniversal.md` — FCC URL `fcc.gov/restoring-internet-freedom` returned "Content Display Disabled" (page taken down by FCC) → removed. WaPo URL `washingtonpost.com/technology/2021/05/12/comcast-broadband-subsidies/` returned 404 → removed.

#### Key findings

- **JCN's financial profile reveals the scale of judicial capture.** $65.1M in revenue (FY2024) from 100% anonymous contributions, zero earned income. The Concord Fund is a pure pass-through: anonymous donors put money in, political spending comes out. The $1.6B Barre Seid donation (via Tripp Lite stock transfer) transformed Leo's network from campaign-cycle dependent to permanently endowed. The $6M in consulting fees paid to Leo's own firm (CRC Advisors) in a single year reveals the self-dealing architecture. JCN's $44M SCOTUS investment produced a 6-3 supermajority that has delivered rulings worth hundreds of billions to the donor class.

- **Comcast's political operation is the vault's clearest monopoly rent extraction case.** $13-17M annual lobbying produces net neutrality repeal (allows paid prioritization), municipal broadband bans in 16 states (eliminates competition), and positions to capture $42.5B in BEAD broadband subsidies. The FCC revolving door is textbook: Meredith Attwell Baker voted to approve Comcast's NBCUniversal acquisition, then became a Comcast lobbyist four months later. Ajit Pai repealed net neutrality as FCC Chairman, then became CEO of the wireless industry's top lobbying organization in March 2025. For every dollar Comcast spends on lobbying, it extracts approximately $5,000 in monopoly revenue.

- **The FCC "Restoring Internet Freedom" page has been disabled.** The FCC's own page documenting the 2017 net neutrality repeal now shows "Content Display Disabled by the content owner." This is notable — the regulatory agency took down the public record of its most consequential deregulatory action.

**Comcast (8):** OpenSecrets lobbying profile, FEC PAC committee (C00248716), Roll Call net neutrality, Revolving Door Project FCC-telecom, Techdirt Pai CTIA, Techdirt 16-state municipal broadband ban, Ballotpedia Comcast. Plus 2 broken URLs documented (FCC page disabled, WaPo 404).

#### Editorial decisions

- AIPAC shell PAC profiles (Biss, Bean, Miller): super PAC spending treated equivalently to direct donations in Format 1 — the analytical framework treats outside spending as donor action, not separate category
- Billionaire patronage profiles (Stratton): personal wealth contributions through outside PACs tracked as donor action
- Small-dollar/grassroots profiles (El-Sayed, Platner, Wahls): fundraising model tracked as structural constraint rather than donor-specific entries

### Automated Profile Build — March 25, 2026 (Run 5)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** gp-profile-builder

#### Files created/expanded

| Profile | Before | After | Status Change | Key Additions |
|---------|--------|-------|---------------|---------------|
| [[_Chip Roy Master Profile]] | 73 lines, 3 sources (thin stub marked "ready") | 150 lines, 11 sources | ready → developed | Full OpenSecrets career data ($12.08M career, House Freedom Fund $474K + Club for Growth $320K as top contributors), FIRE sector breakdown ($1.69M career, Securities & Investment $721K), sector-organized donation-to-policy temporal mapping tables (4 sector groups, 11 entries total), Speaker fight analysis (Rules Committee seat as strategic prize), debt ceiling confrontation (2023 McCarthy "reckoning"), OBBBA holdout (2025 Budget Committee no vote → present), "fairy dust" tax scoring critique documented, solar industry retaliation ad campaign, 4 analytical patterns, 3 callout blocks |
| [[_Chris Murphy Master Profile]] | 75 lines, 6 sources (thin stub marked "ready") | 157 lines, 10 sources | ready → developed | Full OpenSecrets career data ($49.34M career, FIRE $6.85M dominant sector), Insurance Capital contradiction documented ($1.01M insurance industry, Hartford Financial Services $140K as #5 contributor), Koskoff firm connection (Sandy Hook litigation firm = top contributor), gun control legislative timeline (Sandy Hook 2012 → Manchin-Toomey failure 2013 → filibuster 2016 → Bipartisan Safer Communities Act 2022), foreign policy realist portfolio (Saudi arms sales opposition, Yemen war, Murphy-Young-Paul bipartisan resolutions), Medicare for All distancing documented ("consumer choice" framing), sector-organized donation-to-policy tables (3 sectors, 12 entries), 4 analytical patterns, 3 callout blocks |

#### URL fixes

- `_Chris Murphy Master Profile.md` — WaPo URL `washingtonpost.com/politics/2022/06/12/murphy-gun-control-senate/` returned "Page Not Found" → replaced with `washingtonpost.com/magazine/2022/07/05/chris-murphy-uvalde-gun-laws/` (verified). Old URL documented as BROKEN in Source URL Audit Log.

#### Key findings

- **Roy's donor architecture is the clearest case of ideological infrastructure funding in the House.** House Freedom Fund ($474K) + Club for Growth ($320K) = $795K from two organizations alone. Combined with 29% small-dollar contributions and only 6.21% PAC money, Roy's financial model is ideological rather than transactional. The catch: the ideology — spending cuts + tax cuts — mathematically produces deficits, not fiscal responsibility. Roy's "fairy dust" criticism of Senate tax scoring is analytically correct, but his own position (permanent TCJA + spending cuts) still increases deficits because the spending cuts ($1.02T Medicaid from OBBBA) don't offset the revenue loss ($4.6T TCJA permanent). The Club for Growth model is deficit production branded as fiscal conservatism.

- **Murphy's FIRE-to-healthcare constraint is the vault's clearest geographic capture case.** $6.85M career from FIRE (14% of total) vs $1.24M from Labor (2.5%). Insurance industry alone at $1.01M. Murphy can be maximally progressive on guns because gun control doesn't threaten his donors' business model — it actually synergizes with trial lawyer donors (Koskoff firm = Sandy Hook litigation = top contributor). But Murphy cannot be structurally progressive on healthcare because commercial insurance IS Connecticut's economy. The "consumer choice" framing is the tell: it sounds progressive but preserves private insurance.

- **Found and replaced 1 broken WaPo URL** in existing Murphy profile sources.

Plus 1 broken WaPo URL documented.

### Automated Donor Node Build — March 25, 2026 (Run 11)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** donor-node-builder

#### What was built

| Profile | Before | After | Status Change | Key Additions |
|---------|--------|-------|---------------|---------------|
| [[AFL-CIO]] | 50 lines, 4 sources | 182 lines, 11 sources | ready → developed | Full OpenSecrets data (corrected ID D000000088 — old D000000079 returned Verizon), $2.9M contributions + $12.2M outside spending (100% Democratic), $5.2M lobbying (2024) rising to $6.3M (2025), revolving door comparison (27.3% vs. corporate 65-87%), federated multiplier analysis ($50-80M affiliate spending), PRO Act timeline (6 entries), NLRB gains section (Cemex doctrine, joint employer, all reversible), 2024 union household vote gap (Harris +8 only), tariff contradiction (Teamsters split), class analysis on structural asymmetry vs. corporate lobbying (15:1 ratio), 3 analytical patterns, 2 callout blocks |
| [[Blue Cross Blue Shield Association]] | 54 lines, 5 sources | 176 lines, 8 sources | ready → developed | Full OpenSecrets data ($10.4M contributions, $27.1M lobbying rank #5 nationally), federation lobbying multiplier breakdown (20+ subsidiaries, Elevance $7.47M alone), revolving door (91 of 138, 65.9%), top recipient table (Harris $800K, Trump $133K — 6:1 Democratic lean), $2.8B antitrust settlement documentation (largest healthcare antitrust in history, final approval Aug 2025), ACA-as-corporate-welfare temporal mapping (7 entries), public option kill analysis, geographic leverage model, class analysis comparing BCBS lobbying ($28M) to AFL-CIO ($5.2M), 4 analytical patterns, 3 callout blocks |

#### URL fixes in existing files

- `AFL-CIO.md` — OpenSecrets ID corrected: D000000079 (returned Verizon Communications data) → D000000088 (correct AFL-CIO profile). Old ID documented as BROKEN in Source URL Audit Log.

#### Key findings

- **The AFL-CIO's structural trap is the vault's clearest demonstration of loyalty without leverage.** 100% of AFL-CIO outside spending goes to Democrats — zero bipartisan hedging. Yet the PRO Act (labor's #1 priority since 2019) has never received a Senate floor vote, even during the 2021-2023 Democratic trifecta. Corporate donors who oppose the PRO Act outspend labor on lobbying by roughly 15:1. The AFL-CIO pays for loyalty; corporate donors pay for veto power. Because labor has no credible defection threat, the Democratic Party can accept labor's money and mobilization without delivering labor's legislative priorities. Labor is rewarded with reversible NLRB appointments rather than durable legislation.

- **BCBS's #5 lobbying rank quantifies the insurance industry's structural power.** $27-28M annually in lobbying — more than most corporations, more than the AFL-CIO and its affiliates combined. The federation structure is the political innovation: 34 companies lobbying separately creates 34 pressure points. A single BCBS subsidiary (Elevance Health, $7.47M) outspends the AFL-CIO's entire direct lobbying budget ($3.4M). The $2.8B antitrust settlement (largest in healthcare history) confirmed the anti-competitive territorial model — but the fundamental cartel structure remains intact. BCBS paid a fraction of what the model is worth and kept the architecture.

- **AFL-CIO vs. BCBS makes the class analysis visible:** AFL-CIO (12.5M workers) spends $5.2M lobbying + $15M total political spending to get zero legislative wins on its #1 priority. BCBS (115M enrollees) spends $27.9M lobbying + $10.4M contributions to preserve a business model that extracts $50B+ annually in administrative costs. The ACA was designed around BCBS's model; the PRO Act was designed to challenge corporate power. One passed; the other never got a vote. The asymmetry is the vault's thesis in miniature.

#### Verified URLs (19 total — all logged to Source URL Audit Log)

**BCBS (8):** OpenSecrets org profile, OpenSecrets lobbying profile, AHA antitrust settlement, BCBS settlement official site, Healthcare Dive Medicare Advantage rates, CMS admin costs, CMS marketplace products, Ballotpedia BCBS.

| [[_John Kennedy Master Profile]] | 73 lines, 3 sources (thin stub marked "ready") | 128 lines, 9 sources | ready → developed | Full OpenSecrets data with corrected CID (N00026823, old N00026202 was broken), $51.6M raised (2019-2024), donor class map with sector totals (FIRE $3.5M, Energy $951K, Construction $1.18M), dual-engine fundraising model (47% small-dollar/$24.3M + 49% large individual/$25.4M), Cancer Alley section with HRW 2024 documentation, CFPB anti-regulation record (2 bills documented), temporal mapping table (7 entries), party-switch analysis, 4 analytical patterns, 2 callout blocks |

#### Key findings

- **Baldwin's fundraising architecture reveals the class structure of swing-state progressivism.** Her 2024 race was one of the most expensive Senate contests ever ($90M+ candidate fundraising, $119M+ outside spending). Yet her donor base is overwhelmingly professional-class: FIRE sector ($6.4M career) dwarfs Labor ($2.3M career, 95% PAC-driven). The 5.4% PAC reliance ($3.1M) is the progressive branding; the $32.2M in large individual contributions from lawyers, securities, and education professionals is the financial reality. She accepts $350K+ from corporate PACs while campaigning against corporate influence — the Two-Audience Problem in its purest form.

- **Kennedy's dual-engine fundraising model is the vault's clearest case of populist brand monetization.** 47% small-dollar ($24.3M) from grassroots conservatives who think they're funding an independent populist + 49% large individual ($25.4M) from FIRE ($3.5M) and energy ($951K) donors who get Banking Committee anti-CFPB votes and reliable pro-energy positions. PAC reliance at 3.39% is among the lowest in the Senate. The $15.9M cash-on-hand surplus (not up until 2028) converts personal brand into institutional Republican power.

- **Kennedy CID correction:** The vault's existing John Kennedy references used CID N00026202, which returns "No Data Found" on OpenSecrets. The correct CID is N00026823. Fixed in both master profile and sub-note.

### Automated Donor Node Build — March 25, 2026 (Run 9)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** donor-node-builder

#### What was built

| Profile | Before | After | Status Change | Key Additions |
|---------|--------|-------|---------------|---------------|
| [[JPMorgan Chase]] | 40 lines, 1 source | 147 lines, 11 sources | ready → developed | Full 2024 contribution data ($8M total, both-sides breakdown), lobbying timeline (2010-2024, $44.33M cumulative), temporal mapping table (6 entries), revolving door section (William Daley pipeline, NY Fed board conflict, Dimon/Treasury floating), Basel III Endgame rollback documentation, $13B settlement class analysis, CFPB/Credit Card Competition Act lobbying record, 70% government-alumni lobbying staff stat, 4 analytical patterns identified |

#### Key findings

- **The Both-Sides Illusion quantified in a single cycle:** JPMorgan gave $1,000,000 to Make America Great Again Inc and $723,291 to Kamala Harris's presidential campaign simultaneously in the 2024 cycle — while also funding the RNC ($1.16M), DNC ($534K), Trump ($127K), and Haley ($77K). Every major player in the 2024 election received JPMorgan funds. This is the vault's clearest single-cycle example of systematic bipartisan capture at the presidential level.

- **The revolving door is structural, not incidental:** 70% of JPMorgan's registered lobbyists in 2024 (45 of 64) previously held government jobs. In 2023, 72% (36 of 50). The bank doesn't just employ former government officials — it has built a lobbying operation that is institutionally dependent on government alumni. The William Daley case (JPMorgan lobbying chief → Obama White House Chief of Staff, where he had worked to block CFPB creation) is the clearest single example.

- **Basel III Endgame rollback is the vault's cleanest recent donor-class override:** Dimon reportedly circumvented the Fed's Vice Chair for Supervision to directly pressure Fed Chair Powell — who met with bank CEOs 12+ times during deliberations. The final rule was scaled back ~50% from the original proposal. Capital requirements — the primary regulatory tool for preventing bank failures — were weakened through executive relationship pressure, not congressional lobbying.

- **The "too big to prosecute" pattern predates Trump:** The 2013 $13 billion settlement gave JPMorgan broad civil immunity for mortgage fraud that contributed to the 2008 crisis. No criminal charges were filed against individuals. Dimon remained CEO. The settlement was negotiated in secret. This is the Obama DOJ's record — the same pattern critics attribute only to Trump-era deregulation predates it by a decade.

### Automated URL Verification — FEC / Congress.gov / Senate.gov — March 25, 2026

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** url-verify-fec-congress

#### Summary

Verified all government source URLs (FEC, Congress.gov, Senate.gov) found in vault via Python concurrent requests (FEC/Senate.gov) and Chrome in-browser fetch (Congress.gov, which blocks Python due to Cloudflare). All 378 unique URLs checked. Results fully logged in [[Source URL Audit Log]].

| Domain | Total | VALID | BROKEN |
|--------|-------|-------|--------|
| FEC | 183 | 149 | 34 |
| Congress.gov | 174 | 150 | 24 |
| Senate.gov | 21 | 17 | 4 |
| **Total** | **378** | **316** | **62** |

**Overall: 16.4% broken rate across government sources.**

#### Key findings

**FEC broken pattern:** 34 broken FEC URLs — a mix of candidate IDs (H/S codes that don't resolve) and committee IDs that return 404. Also includes two generic listing pages (`/data/candidate/` and `/data/committee/`) that don't resolve. Broken candidate IDs are concentrated in 2026 Senate race files and a handful of master profiles where the ID was incorrectly entered. Committee breakdowns tracked to PAC nodes, Jan 6 research files, and McCarthy/Lucas profiles.

**Congress.gov broken pattern — critical issue:** 21 of 24 broken Congress.gov URLs follow a systemic pattern where **sub-note titles were used as fake member slugs** (e.g., `congress.gov/member/the-silicon-valley-progressive-and-tech-industry-alignment`, `congress.gov/member/tiktok---bytedance`). These are not citations to real content — they're artifacts of a URL construction error where note titles were formatted as Congress.gov paths. The 2 other broken URLs are generic committees paths (`/committees/house-appropriations`, `/committees/house-energy-and-commerce`) which use the wrong URL format. One PDF link also broken.

**Senate.gov broken pattern:** All 4 broken Senate.gov URLs are generic search queries (`?q=...`) that redirect to the Senate homepage — not valid citations. These appear to originate from a "Search URL Upgrade Checklist" file and are already tracked as needing replacement.

#### Files modified

Affected files span ~15 profiles and donor nodes. These citations are structurally broken and lower source quality across many notes.

### Automated Profile Build — March 25, 2026 (Run 2)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** gp-profile-builder

#### Files expanded

- `Politicians/Democrats/House/Pete Aguilar/_Pete Aguilar Master Profile.md` — ready (62 lines, thin stub) → developed (122 lines, substantive)
- `Politicians/Democrats/House/Rashida Tlaib/_Rashida Tlaib Master Profile.md` — ready (62 lines, thin stub) → developed (141 lines, substantive)
- `Politicians/Republicans/House/Lauren Boebert/_Lauren Boebert Master Profile.md` — ready (62 lines, thin stub) → developed (169 lines, substantive)

#### Key findings

- **Aguilar Appropriations-logistics contradiction mapped:** Represents a district with 4,000+ warehouses generating 600,000+ daily truck trips where 86% of logistics workers earn below living wage. Raised $30M+ for DCCC in 2024 cycle, paid $3M in member dues, traveled to 19 states for 200+ campaign events. Credit unions are top donor sector. Federal housing funds ($24M Redlands, $17M community projects FY2024) stabilize warehouse-dependent economy without challenging extraction. Classic Genuine Win + Structural Limit: federal subsidies offset damage of extractive logistics economy without threatening the extraction itself.

- **Tlaib AIPAC immunity quantified:** AIPAC offered $20M to recruit primary challengers — both targets refused. November 2023 censure (234-188, 22 Democrats voting for) triggered $3.7M grassroots fundraising surge in Q4 2023 alone (22,700 first-time donors, $75 average). Vault's strongest case study for small-dollar model as genuine alternative to corporate PAC dependency. Her district demographics (largest Arab-American population in U.S., majority-Black Detroit) make her functionally immune to the strategy that defeated Bowman in NY-16. No corporate PAC money; $6.7M+ raised in 2024 cycle entirely grassroots.

- **Boebert energy-proxy structure documented:** Husband Jayson Boebert earned $938,987 from Terra Energy Partners (2019-2020) while Lauren introduced Colorado Energy Prosperity Act protecting 1.6M acres for oil/gas development. Failed to disclose husband's income until August 2021. Seeded into Congress by Club for Growth ($20M to far-right candidates) and Ted Cruz PAC ($136,250 direct transfer). 2024 fundraising collapsed 81% after moving from competitive CO-3 (546-vote margin) to safe CO-4 (R+26) — proving culture-war fundraising model fails when electoral urgency disappears. Multiple FEC complaints (mileage fraud, personal expense charges, disclosure failures) — all blocked by partisan FEC deadlock.

#### Build methodology note

Continuing the thin-profile approach from Run 1: targeting master profiles under 80 lines regardless of YAML status. These three profiles (all 62 lines, all marked "ready") represent the vault's weakest analytical entries. All expanded to developed status with substantive donor analysis, sourced claims, analytical pattern identification, and vault-standard formatting.

### Automated Donor Node Build — March 25, 2026 (Run 8)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** gp-donor-node-builder

#### What was built

| Profile | Before | After | Status Change | Key Additions |
|---------|--------|-------|---------------|---------------|
| [[ALEC - American Legislative Exchange Council\|ALEC]] | 45 lines (stub) | 227 lines | ready → developed | Full funding analysis ($10.92M revenue, Bradley $3.6M, Koch $2.3M, 35% undisclosed), model legislation table (9 major bills with dates/outcomes: Stand Your Ground 30+ states, Voter ID 62+ bills, Right-to-Work 27 states, Ag-Gag, Critical Infrastructure, Dark Money Protection 17 states, Grid Stability 15+ bills), 2012 corporate exodus documentation (60+ companies left, budget recovered via dark money foundations), 501(c)(3) IRS challenge (Common Cause 2012 complaint, unresolved), dark money network pipeline (Koch→ALEC→AFP→SPN→Federalist Society), 2024-2026 activity (anti-renewable grid bills, anti-ESG, AI deregulation toolkit), temporal mapping table (14 entries), class analysis with 2 patterns (Donor-Class Override, structural loop), 19 sourced citations |
| [[US Chamber of Commerce]] | 79 lines (stub) | 241 lines | ready → developed | Year-by-year lobbying timeline (2010-2024, $746M+ cumulative), TCJA ROI calculation ($82M lobbying → $400B+ member benefits, 1:5,000 ratio), SCOTUS amicus brief strategy (Citizens United, Janus, Epic Systems — all won 5-4), dark money donor concentration (55% from 16 companies), 2024 dark money evolution ($0 reported spending via electioneering loophole), Trump relationship arc (alignment → J6 break → recalibration → selective friction on H-1B), PRO Act defeat ($100M+ anti-labor campaign), foreign funding controversy (2010 AmCham funds), revolving door (Donohue 25-year tenure, Clark career), temporal mapping table (11 entries), class analysis with 4 structural functions, 12 sourced citations |

#### Key findings

- **ALEC's self-reinforcing dark money loop is the vault's clearest example of structural regulatory capture of the transparency system itself.** 35% of ALEC's funding ($19.1M, 2017-2021) comes from undisclosed sources. ALEC then drafts the Personal Privacy Protection Act (2017), which prevents states from requiring dark money donor disclosure. 17 states have adopted this model. Dark money funds ALEC → ALEC protects dark money → more dark money flows anonymously. No other node in the vault documents this kind of circular self-protection mechanism.

- **The US Chamber's 1:5,000 lobbying-to-benefit ratio on the TCJA is the vault's highest documented ROI for a non-defense lobbying campaign.** $82M in 2017 lobbying expenditure → $400B+ in corporate tax benefits for member companies. The Chamber is now lobbying for permanent TCJA extension (provisions expiring 2025), which would deliver trillions in additional corporate tax savings. For comparison: GEO Group's 127:1 annual ROI (Run 7) is impressive for a single contractor, but the Chamber's operation benefits the entire corporate class simultaneously.

- **Both nodes document the "dark money evolution" in real time.** ALEC moved from corporate dues to foundation dependency after the 2012 exodus. The Chamber shifted from reported independent expenditures to unreported electioneering communications in 2024, reporting $0 in outside spending despite continued political activity. Both organizations are adapting their dark money architectures to maintain influence while reducing disclosure — the regulatory environment created by Citizens United continues to be exploited by the organizations that lobbied for it.

- **The thin "ready" profile problem persists as the vault's most critical quality gap.** Both profiles were labeled "ready" despite being structurally below publishable quality (45 and 79 lines respectively, vs. gold standard Elon Musk at 328 lines with 52 sources). Set to "developed" pending editorial review.

#### Kushner status — RESOLVED

All 4 Kushner sub-notes existed at developed, promoted to ready:
1. 666 Fifth Avenue and Financial Desperation (67L, 4 sources Tier 1-2)
2. Abraham Accords as Business Development (74L, 6 sources)
3. Affinity Partners and the Saudi Pipeline (86L, 6 sources)
4. Kushner Companies and the Slumlord Record (78L, 7 sources)

Master profile was already ready (Session 38j). Kushner folder is 100% complete.

#### Key technical decisions

- **Bold→### conversion scope:** Applied sed pattern `^\*\*([^*]+)\*\*\s*$` → `### $1` to 41 files with H0-H2 headers. This converts standalone bold section headers but preserves inline bold within paragraphs (e.g., `**Federal level:**` at the start of a paragraph line is not converted because it doesn't end with `**`).
- **Inline bold tolerance:** Files with up to 18 inline bold lines within paragraphs were promoted without formatting changes. These are content emphasis patterns (labels like `**The Genuine Win + Structural Limit**` within analysis paragraphs), not section headers.
- **Low-URL files:** Promoted 6 files with only 1 URL and 5 files with 0 URLs. The 0-URL files are index/reference/bridge notes that use wikilinks to sourced content rather than direct source citations. The 1-URL files are sub-notes under sourced master profiles — sources are in the parent notes.
- **Pete Hegseth duplicate:** Stale duplicate at `Veterans & Military/_Pete Hegseth Master Profile.md` promoted to ready by the bold→### batch (it matched criteria). Still needs deletion — canonical version in Trump Cabinet.
- **Marc Andreessen duplicate:** Also promoted — file has a `[!warning] DUPLICATE` callout. Still needs consolidation with canonical `Tech & Crypto/Marc Andreessen & Horowitz.md`.

- Project 2025 as personnel/policy factory. $100.9M revenue (2023). $1.6B Seid donation documented. 66+ staff to Trump 1.0, 31+ to Trump 2.0. Policy win table (TCJA, SCOTUS list, Schedule F, EPA dereg). December 2025 staff exodus to Pence group. Temporal mapping table. ROI calculation (Koch $5.7M → $1-1.4B annual tax savings).

**2. Federalist Society** (`Donors & Power Networks/Dark Money/Federalist Society.md`)
- raw → ready. ~280 lines. 21 sources (4 Tier 1, 17 Tier 2).
- 40-year judicial pipeline. 84% of Trump appellate judges are FedSoc members. 6/9 SCOTUS justices. Leo financial empire table (7 entities). $1.6B Marble Freedom Trust. DonorsTrust providing 25%+ of budget. SFFA litigation funding pipeline. Temporal mapping from 1982 founding through 2024 Chevron elimination. ROI on Chevron alone ($100B+ regulatory cost shift). Trump "sleazebag" quote.

**3. National Rifle Association** (`Donors & Power Networks/Super PACs/National Rifle Association.md`)
- raw → ready. ~220 lines. 17 sources (7 Tier 1, 10 Tier 2).
- $54.4M peak (2016) to $10M (2024) decline documented. LaPierre corruption verdict ($4.35M). Senate Finance Committee Russian money investigation. Membership decline (5M claimed → 3.8M actual). Heller/McDonald/Bruen judicial pipeline. PLCAA manufacturer immunity. Scorecard partisan shift (25% Dem A-grades 2010 → 0% by 2022). NSSF now outspending NRA on lobbying.

**4. Sheldon & Miriam Adelson** (`Donors & Power Networks/Mega-Donors/Sheldon Adelson.md`)
- raw → ready. ~250 lines. 20 sources (6 Tier 1, 14 Tier 2).
- $524M+ combined political spending since 2016. $172.7M 2020 cycle (record). Miriam $100-106M to Preserve America PAC (2024). Israel policy pipeline: $82.6M → Jerusalem embassy (12 months), Golan Heights (27 months), Abraham Accords (48 months). Trump publicly acknowledged transaction. AIPAC break (2007), alternative hardline channels built. RJC funding (half of Victory Fund). Online gambling opposition.

**5. Pfizer Inc.** (`Donors & Power Networks/Pharma & Healthcare/Pfizer.md`)
- raw → ready. ~230 lines. 20 sources (7 Tier 1, 13 Tier 2).
- PAC bipartisan split (63% R / 37% D). $219M cumulative lobbying (1999-2018). COVID profit machine: $5.95B Warp Speed → $36.8B Comirnaty revenue (6.2x return in one year). Germany funded R&D, not US. Paxlovid 2.6x markup ($529 → $1,390). Revolving door: FDA CDER Director → Pfizer CMO, FDA Commissioner → Pfizer board (2 months). IRA "loss" that preserved most pricing power. RFK Jr. $500M mRNA defunding.

#### Key editorial decisions

- Sheldon Adelson profile retitled to "Sheldon & Miriam Adelson" to cover the dynasty, not just the deceased founder
- NRA classified as `type: pac` (not donor) — its primary function is political spending, not research

### Automated Donor Node Build — March 24, 2026 (gp-donor-node-builder)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run

**Note:** Northrop Grumman and DeVos Family were also targeted for this run but had already been built to `ready` status by a concurrent automated process (Session 40-auto). Save America PAC was the only remaining raw priority node available for this task.

#### Key findings — Save America PAC

- **$250 million "Big Rip-Off":** January 6 Committee documented that Trump raised $250M on claims of an "Official Election Defense Fund" that did not exist. Campaign sent 25+ fundraising emails per day with deceptive tactics (fake forwarded messages, misleading sender lines).
- **Legal fee machine:** $50M+ spent on Trump's legal fees in 2023 alone (86% of H1 2023 spending). Only $210K to 42 candidates in 2022 cycle — 0.19% of total raised.
- **Pardon economy documented:** Daughter of Venezuelan billionaire Velutini donated $3.5M to MAGA Inc; Velutini received pardon (January 2026). Mother of convicted fraudster Walczak donated $1M; Walczak pardoned (April 2025).
- **Zero enforcement:** Despite January 6 Committee findings, Jack Smith investigation, and FEC complaints — no criminal charges filed for fundraising fraud. Smith withdrew subpoena October 2023. Leadership PAC structure creates enforcement vacuum.
- **Analytical patterns flagged:** Two-Audience Problem (fundraising appeals vs. actual spending), Donor-Class Override (pardon economy inverts normal donor-to-policy pipeline)

#### Key findings

### Adam Smith (Armed Services Ranking Member):
- Top 3 career donors: Boeing ($163,450), Northrop Grumman ($150,250), Raytheon ($117,000) = $430,700 from three weapons manufacturers
- 60% of campaign from PACs; leadership PAC (ADAM PAC) virtually 100% defense-funded
- Northrop Grumman is #1 donor source — Virginia-based, zero Pacific Northwest facilities
- Called fellow Democrats "extremists" for proposing Pentagon cuts while cashing $235,750 from defense PACs same cycle
- Pattern: Two-Audience Problem (progressive domestic, defense-aligned on budget); Both-Sides Illusion (bipartisan NDAA)

### Gregory Meeks (Foreign Affairs Ranking Member):
- $523,000+ career from AIPAC and pro-Israel groups; 75.37% PAC funding; 1.16% small donors
- CREW "Most Corrupt" (2011): undisclosed $40K gift, $170K+ below-market home, Azerbaijan SOCAR travel

- Pattern: Two-Audience Problem (Israel); Donor-Class Override (private equity); ethics pipeline

Section 199A permanence in "One Big Beautiful Bill" sends ~60% of benefits to top 1% (ProPublica). District is poorest in Missouri (15.5% poverty rate, median $56K). Carried interest preserved, 1031 exchanges preserved, bonus depreciation made permanent. The trailer-to-gavel biography as populist cover for donor-class tax policy.

### 2. Roger Wicker (Armed Services Chair)
- `Politicians/Republicans/Senate/Roger Wicker/_Roger Wicker Master Profile.md`
- raw → ready. 13 sources (6 Tier 1, 6 Tier 2, 1 Tier 3).
- #1 Senate recipient of defense industry contributions ($343,065/2024). Mississippi defense economy = 4.3% state GDP ($31.2B invested since FY2014). Huntington Ingalls Pascagoula shipyard (11,000 jobs). "Peace Through Strength" = $55B/year additional spending. $11.5B multi-ship Ingalls contract. $5.1B DDG-51 procurement. $2.97B Lockheed Aegis contract. Geographic revolving door: state economy depends on spending he authorizes.

### 3. Mike Crapo (Finance Chair)
- `Politicians/Republicans/Senate/Mike Crapo/_Mike Crapo Master Profile.md`
- raw → ready. 13 sources (6 Tier 1, 4 Tier 2, 1 Tier 3).
- Wall Street's tax chairman. 50%+ PAC funded, 84% of individual donations from out-of-state (financial services). Stephens Inc. pipeline: 19+ executives from one Arkansas investment bank. - raw → ready. 13 sources (4 Tier 1, 6 Tier 2, 3 Tier 3).
- The investigator who doesn't investigate himself. Unilateral subpoena power deployed as partisan weapon. Biden investigation: 18 months, zero charges, central informant (Smirnov) charged with felony lying. Dropped Trump profit probe and Kushner $2B Saudi deal investigation. - raw → ready. 13 sources (6 Tier 1, 6 Tier 2, 1 Tier 3).
- The reform Democrat whose reforms never pass. Billionaires Income Tax introduced 4+ times, never advanced. Section 702 reform, privacy overhaul — all aspirational, none enacted. Pallone's energy PAC donations grew 10x with his E&C gavel. Smith's defense PAC haul tracks his Armed Services chairmanship. Meeks' AIPAC giving surged with Foreign Affairs leadership. Cole's entire fundraising apparatus is built on Appropriations control of $1.7T. The committee IS the fundraising vehicle.

#### Cross-batch analytical findings

**The Committee-as-Product pattern continues:** Every batch 2 profile reinforces the jurisdiction premium finding from batch 1. Himes' Wall Street money tracks Financial Services. Reed's defense money tracks Armed Services. Thompson's 434 lobbying orgs track Homeland Security procurement. Scott's union money tracks Education & Workforce. The committee IS the fundraising vehicle.

**New pattern — Structural Capture Without Corruption:** Reed and Scott are the two cleanest profiles in the vault in terms of personal corruption. Reed has the lowest net worth in the Senate. Scott's donors are labor unions. Neither is personally enriched by their positions. But both serve the structural function the donor class needs — Reed ensures bipartisan defense spending, Scott writes labor bills that die in the Senate. Structural capture doesn't require bribery. It requires alignment.

**New pattern — The Senate Kill Switch:** Bobby Scott's career is the proof case. The House is where labor/progressive legislation is written and passed. The Senate is where it dies. The donor-class veto operates through the Senate's procedural architecture (filibuster, committee holds, leadership discretion). Grassley's drug pricing bill hit the same wall from the other direction — McConnell killed bipartisan reform to protect pharma donors. The Senate is the kill switch.

#### Cross-session analytical findings

**The Jurisdiction Portfolio (new pattern — Westerman):** Bruce Westerman didn't just take donor money from the industries his committee regulates — he bought their stocks. Oil companies (BP, Shell, Chevron, ConocoPhillips) and mining giants (Freeport-McMoRan, BHP, Rio Tinto) in his personal portfolio while his committee advanced legislation opening public lands to extraction at reduced royalty rates. This is the triple alignment: donor money + legislative action + personal financial investment. The most complete conflict of interest documented in the vault.

**The Gavel Premium (documented — Thompson, Walberg):** Glenn Thompson received 1-in-7 PAC dollars from first-time donors in his first half-year as Agriculture chair. Marc Rowan gave Tim Walberg $21,000 months after Walberg became Education chair. Apollo Education Group PAC followed with $7,500 more. The gavel premium isn't theoretical — it's documented in real time across multiple committees.

**The Boeing Triangle (Larsen + Cantwell + Graves):** Three profiles — Larsen (D-WA, T&I RM), Cantwell (D-WA, Commerce), and Graves (R-MO, T&I Chair) — all receive significant Boeing/aviation money. Larsen and Graves sit on opposite sides of the same committee. Cantwell oversees Boeing's FAA regulator from the Senate. Boeing funds all three. The bipartisan protection is complete: no matter which party controls which chamber, Boeing has its people on the relevant committees.

**The Anti-Pattern (Scott vs. Walberg):** Bobby Scott (D, Education & Workforce RM, built Session 43) and Tim Walberg (R, Education & Workforce Chair, built this session) sit across from each other on the same committee representing opposite donor classes. Scott: funded by unions, writes labor protection bills that die in the Senate. Walberg: funded by DeVos/private equity, dismantles labor protections and public education from the chairmanship. Same committee, opposite functions, opposite money sources. The committee is the battlefield.

#### Cross-session analytical findings

**The Dynasty Pattern (Capito):** Shelley Moore Capito is the vault's clearest dynastic case — three generations entangled with the same industry. Father Arch Moore convicted of extorting a coal company ($573K). Daughter Shelley chairs the committee regulating fossil fuels. Son Arch Moore Capito employed by natural gas company (ECA) whose executives donate $60K+ to her campaigns. The family-industry fusion spans 50+ years. This isn't revolving door. It's permanent open doorway.

**The Koch-to-Policy Pipeline (Moran):** The cleanest donor-to-policy loop documented in the vault. Koch Industries → #2 career donor. Koch funds Concerned Veterans for America. CVA designs VA privatization agenda. Moran chairs Veterans' Affairs Committee. Moran implements "community care" = privatization. Three nodes (donor, advocacy group, committee chair), one policy outcome, all headquartered in Wichita.

**The AIPAC Gavel Purchase (Mast):** $700K from AIPAC bought the Foreign Affairs gavel — the committee controlling $3.8B/year in Israel military aid, arms export oversight, and UN policy. Trump personally installed Mast over more senior members. This is the jurisdiction premium at its most extreme: AIPAC doesn't just fund friendly votes, it purchased the gavel that controls the entire U.S.-Israel policy apparatus.

**The PACT Act Trap (Bost):** The structural trap that makes committee chairs complicit in gutting their own achievements. Bost built the PACT Act. DOGE came to cut 83,000 VA workers including PACT Act staff. Bost can't resist without losing his gavel. Result: "full confidence" in the people dismantling his signature law. The chairmanship is the leash.

**The Meta-Corruption (Steil):** Wall Street funds the chairman who writes campaign finance law, ensuring the campaign finance system continues to allow Wall Street to fund politicians. $721,840 from securities & investment to the man who oversees the FEC and writes election rules. This is corruption that protects corruption — the regulatory capture of the regulatory system itself.

#### Cross-session analytical findings (batches 2-3)

**The Anti-Pattern Cluster:** Batches 2-3 surfaced a cluster of Democratic RMs whose committee work shows minimal donor capture — McGovern (Rules), Velazquez (Small Business), Grijalva (Natural Resources), Takano (Veterans' Affairs), Boyle (Budget), Blumenthal (Veterans' Affairs). When committee leaders are funded by labor/progressive grassroots rather than the industries they oversee, the policy agenda serves constituents instead of donors. These anti-patterns prove the vault's thesis by contrast.

**The Loyalty Purchase (Crawford):** The Intelligence Committee chairmanship is the vault's clearest case of a gavel purchased with political loyalty rather than donor money. Turner was removed for independence on Ukraine/Russia. Crawford was installed for compliance. The most sensitive oversight body in Congress is now chaired by someone selected for willingness not to challenge the president. The currency isn't money — it's allegiance.

**The Selective Science (Babin):** The Science Committee chairman is anti-science on exactly one topic: the scientific question that threatens his petrochemical district. He champions NASA (aerospace donors benefit), defunds climate research (fossil fuel donors benefit). Same committee, two jurisdictions, two opposite relationships to science — selected by the money.

**The Deregulator-Turned-Regulator (Markey):** Markey's 50-year career arc — from authoring the Telecom Act of 1996 (enabling corporate consolidation) to co-authoring the Green New Deal (proposing structural transformation) — is the vault's strongest case of genuine policy evolution. His donor base actually shifted with him: telecom industry PACs → progressive small donors. Rare in the vault.

**The Class Distance Pattern (Blumenthal):** A $100M+ real estate fortune senator ranking on the Veterans' Affairs Committee — serving a population whose average household income is $50K. Not donor capture; class distance. The wealth that makes him donor-independent also makes him constituency-distant. The inverted Self-Funding as Independence pattern.

## Session 46. March 24, 2026. Vault-Wide Source Link Audit (Complete)

**Type:** Infrastructure — source link formatting standardization and dead URL verification

**Trigger.** David reported Miriam Adelson's source links were all broken on Obsidian Publish. Investigation revealed three systemic formatting issues across the vault preventing proper link rendering.

---

### Three formatting issues identified and fixed

### Issue 1: Nested parentheses in Wikipedia/Ballotpedia URLs (41 fixes across 32 files)
URLs containing literal parentheses — e.g., `Rick_Crawford_(politician)` — broke markdown link rendering because the closing `)` in the URL was interpreted as ending the markdown link. Fix: URL-encoded `(` → `%28` and `)` → `%29` in all affected links.

### Issue 2: Non-standard dot-tier format (299 fixes across 28 files)
Source citations using `· Tier X` instead of `(Tier X)` were not rendering as clickable links on Obsidian Publish because the dot-tier format interrupted the markdown link syntax. Converted all instances to standard parenthetical format. Largest concentrations: Kamala Harris sub-notes (75), Newsom 2028 (36), research logs (44+), Bernie Sanders sub-notes (33).

### Issue 3: Bare URLs not wrapped in markdown links (41 fixes across 14 files)
Multiple format variants found and converted:
- Em-dash bare URLs (`— Source: https://url [Tier X]`) → 134 fixes in earlier pass
- "Primary sources to pull" reference sections in Newsom D&B files (14 fixes)
- Substack Post 2 source section with name/URL on separate lines (6 fixes)
- Valinor Enterprises inline source citations (4 fixes)
- Marc Andreessen inline source citations (3 fixes)
- Pro-Israel Deep Dive inline source references (2 fixes)
- Dan Osborn and Jon Husted website links (2 fixes)
- Research log database reference links (6 fixes)
- Session History Archive verification notes (3 fixes)

---

### Browser verification results

All Miriam Adelson source URLs confirmed live:
- OpenSecrets donor detail page ✓
- Times of Israel $100M donation article ✓
- CNBC Adelson PAC funding article ✓
- Las Vegas Sands investor relations ✓
- CasinoBeats net worth article ✓
- ScheerPost Iran war manifesto article ✓
- The Nation Israel foreign policy article ✓
- Al Jazeera Iran war/Netanyahu article ✓
- OpenSecrets donor lookup (Adelson family) ✓
- Wikipedia Rick Crawford (URL-encoded parens) ✓

Bloomberg Billionaires link not testable (site blocked by browser safety restrictions) but Bloomberg paywalled URLs are a known cross-vault issue documented in prior sessions.

**Root cause of David's Obsidian Publish issue:** The dot-tier format (`· Tier X`) was preventing markdown links from rendering as clickable on Publish. The underlying URLs were valid — the display rendering was broken by non-standard formatting. This has been fully resolved.

---

### Files modified this session

**32 files** — nested parentheses URL encoding
**28 files** — dot-tier to parenthetical tier conversion
**14 files** — bare URL to markdown link conversion

Key files with multiple fix types:
- Miriam Adelson (9 dot-tier fixes)
- Newsom 2028 profile (36 dot-tier fixes)
- Kamala Harris sub-notes (75 dot-tier fixes)
- Bernie Sanders sub-notes (33 dot-tier fixes)
- CCPOA donor node (19 em-dash bare URL fixes)
- Chad Bianco sub-notes (70+ em-dash bare URL fixes)
- Gulf State Money (15 em-dash bare URL fixes)
- Netanyahu sub-notes (21 em-dash bare URL fixes)

**New file:** `topics/Stories/Vault Maintenance/Sources Master Node.md` — comprehensive database index (created in Session 45, documented here for completeness)
