### Automated Lobbying Firm Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (lobbying-firm-builder Run 6)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Revenue | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|---------|-----------|
| `Holland & Knight.md` | draft (NEW) | $49.7M (2024) | 45.6% revolving door; Michal Freedhoff (Biden EPA OCSPP AA) → H&K three months after leaving → lobbies for American Chemistry Council ($520K/yr); both Republican and Democratic TSCA architects on staff; Perlmutter/cannabis SAFER Banking Act; Tom Davis/defense contractors; bipartisan Finance Committee machine |

#### What was built

Created `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/Holland & Knight.md` — full lobbying firm profile following the Framework anatomy. 11 Chrome-verified sources (Tier 1–3), 1 BROKEN (Nasim Fussell hire press release — hklaw.com 404), 1 URL NEEDED (Fussell). Content-readiness: draft.

**Key analytical findings:**

- **The Freedhoff hire is the vault's cleanest documented regulatory-capture-via-revolving-door case.** Michal Freedhoff spent four years as Biden's top chemical safety regulator (EPA OCSPP Assistant Administrator, 2021–2025). She oversaw TSCA enforcement, PFAS rulemaking, and pesticide regulation. H&K's top-billing 2024 client is the American Chemistry Council — the chemical industry's primary DC trade group ($520K/yr), which spent four years fighting Freedhoff's regulatory agenda. She joined H&K in April 2025, three months after leaving EPA. H&K already employed both the principal Republican TSCA Senate negotiator and one of the chemical industry's longest-serving lobbyists. It now also has the chief Biden-era TSCA enforcer. The chemical industry purchased access to their own regulator at the exact moment she exited government.

- **Ed Perlmutter reverse-revolving-door case is fully documented.** Perlmutter spent 8 terms in Congress (2007–2023) as the primary sponsor of the SAFE Banking Act / SAFER Banking Act — cannabis banking reform that passed the House seven times but failed in the Senate every time. He retired in January 2023 and joined Holland & Knight. His first major client: National Cannabis Roundtable, lobbying the Senate to pass SAFER Banking. Within 12 months, the bill cleared the Senate Banking Committee for the first time in its history. The former sponsor of the bill became the lobbyist for the bill.

- **Tom Davis's revolving door timing is the clearest defense contractor access purchase in the cohort.** Davis chaired the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform (2003–2007) — the committee that directly oversees federal contractor accountability. He joined H&K in January 2019. His client roster: TransDigm Group (defense aerospace, historically targeted by DoD IG for excess profit-taking via sole-source contracts), ViaSat (satellite communications and defense), Veritas Capital portfolio companies (GuideHouse government consulting, Chromalloy aircraft components). Davis's committee oversight experience is precisely the access defense contractors need to manage procurement scrutiny.

- **Alphabet ($640K/yr combined) is the clearest tech lobbying outcome.** Waymo ($320K) and Wing Aviation ($320K) are H&K's largest tech clients. FAA's beyond-visual-line-of-sight drone authorization expanded, autonomous vehicle regulatory framework advanced without new federal restrictions, Waymo's commercial robotaxi expansion proceeded across multiple cities without new legislation. $640K/yr for deregulation-by-inaction.

- **The Alibaba Group retention ($320K) is analytically notable.** A Chinese e-commerce giant on the US national security watch list paying $320K/yr to a top DC lobbying firm with bipartisan congressional relationships — during a period of heightened US-China tech decoupling scrutiny — suggests active work to manage regulatory threats to Alibaba's US operations. This warrants deeper research.

- **The bipartisan access architecture is deliberately engineered.** Rich Gold (Clinton EPA), Yasmin Nelson (Wyden/Stabenow/Booker), Ed Perlmutter (8-term Democrat), Michal Freedhoff (Biden EPA) = Democratic coverage. Christopher Armstrong (Senate Finance under Hatch), Tom Davis (Republican House Oversight Chair), Christopher DeLacy (John Warner/R-VA, House Science R-side), Nasim Fussell (Senate Finance under Grassley) = Republican coverage. The full Senate Finance Committee — both parties — is covered by former staffers at the same firm.

- **Revenue data correction for the Framework:** The Framework listed H&K at "~$30M/yr." Actual data: $43.2M (2022), $48M (2023), $49.7M (2024), $54.6M (2025 reported). Framework estimate understates by ~40-80%. The correction was not applied to the Framework (that's a separate task) but is noted here.

#### URL verification

11 URLs Chrome-verified VALID. 1 BROKEN. 1 URL NEEDED.

**VALID (all 11):**
- `opensecrets.org/federal-lobbying/firms/summary?cycle=2024&id=D000000330` (OpenSecrets H&K 2024 profile — $49.7M, 318 clients)
- `opensecrets.org/federal-lobbying/firms/lobbyists?id=D000000330&year=2024` (OpenSecrets lobbyists — 90 lobbyists, 45.6% revolvers, 6 former MoC)
- `eenews.net/articles/bidens-epa-chemicals-chief-lands-new-job/` (E&E News Freedhoff hire)
- `hklaw.com/en/news/pressreleases/2025/04/former-epa-chemical-safety-assistant-administrator-michal-freedhoff` (H&K Freedhoff announcement)
- `hklaw.com/en/news/pressreleases/2019/01/former-congressman-tom-davis-joins-holland--knight` (H&K Tom Davis announcement)
- `hklaw.com/en/news/pressreleases/2023/09/yasmin-nelson` (H&K Yasmin Nelson announcement)
- `hklaw.com/en/news/intheheadlines/2025/01/biglaw-firms-report-record-lobbying-revenues-for-2024` (H&K BigLaw revenues)
- `hklaw.com/en/news/pressreleases/2025/01/holland-knight-forms-national-security-defense-industry-group` (H&K defense group)
- `hklaw.com/en/professionals/g/gold-rich` (Rich Gold profile)

## Automated Story Discovery — March 26, 2026 (story-discovery Run 2)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** story-discovery

### What was done

Conducted news scan for unreported donor-to-policy connections. Found an existing Run 1 entry in the story discovery file (created earlier today). Appended Run 2 findings covering stories not captured in Run 1.

16 URLs Chrome-verified VALID. 3 marked UNVERIFIED (flagged inline). All 16 VALID logged to Source URL Audit Log under "Story Discovery Run 2 — March 26, 2026."

### Stories surfaced (Run 2 — new findings)

| Tier | Story | Vault Status |
|------|-------|-------------|
| 💎 Diamond | Iran War Defense Contractor $200B Windfall — Lockheed/Raytheon pre-war production contracts, $200B supplemental request, stock gains | NOT IN VAULT |
| 🥇 Gold | GENIUS Act: $250M crypto industry spend → stablecoin law signed July 2025; Gillibrand $217K donations | NOT IN VAULT |
| 🥇 Gold | PBM Reform: Targets middlemen (PBMs), spares pharma manufacturers; 2028/2029 effective dates | NOT IN VAULT |
| 🥈 Silver | Housing bill: 350-home threshold, 15-year sunset, deregulatory provisions bundled in | NOT IN VAULT |
| 🥈 Silver | Ro Khanna Iran WPR vote — anti-war position against defense contractor donor base | PARTIAL (Khanna profile needs update) |
| Update | AIPAC Illinois March 18 results confirmed: 2 wins (IL-2 Miller, IL-8 Bean), 2 losses (IL-9 Fine, IL-7 Conyears-Ervin) | Supplements Run 1 DIAMOND-02 |

### Key analytical findings

**Iran War as contractor profit machine:** The January 2026 pre-war production contracts with Lockheed (THAAD quadrupled, Patriot scaled up) preceded public hostilities by ~6 weeks. The $200B supplemental request, layered on an existing $839B+ NDAA, is the largest single-war supplemental request in a generation. Lockheed is simultaneously a Trump White House ballroom donor (DIAMOND-01 in Run 1) — the ballroom donation, the pre-war production contracts, and the $200B ask are potentially a single access chain.

**GENIUS Act as cleanest 2025 quid-pro-quo timeline:** $250M crypto industry spend in 2024 cycle → 18 Democratic senators cross over → bill signed into law 6 months after seating. Gillibrand ($217K crypto donations) as principal Democratic co-sponsor. The Merkley conflict-of-interest amendment blocked — meaning the same Congress that received crypto money will benefit personally from crypto deregulation.

**PBM reform as Villain Framing case study:** Congress publicly attacked PBMs (middlemen) while accepting $500K+ per member from pharma manufacturers (source of pricing power). Legislation punishes PBMs, leaves manufacturer pricing untouched. 2028/2029 effective dates provide post-electoral cover.

**AIPAC Illinois 2-for-4:** Despite $22M+ in spending, AIPAC won only 2 of 4 targeted House races. The two wins came in majority-Black districts where front PAC branding was localized; the two losses came in progressive districts where the AIPAC connection became a liability. Daniel Biss (IL-9 winner) — Jewish progressive — blasted AIPAC in his victory speech.

### Media Pipeline — Ethan Klein Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (Automated Run)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** media-profile-builder
**Profile built:** Ethan Klein (Left / multi-platform)

#### What was built

| File | Action | Status |
|------|--------|--------|
| `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Left/Ethan Klein.md` | CREATED | developed |
| `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/_Media Pipeline Index.md` | UPDATED | Ethan Klein marked developed; stats updated 4→5 developed |
| `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` | UPDATED | "Media Pipeline — Ethan Klein Profile" section added (17 VALID, 1 BROKEN) |
| `topics/Vault Maintenance/Session Timeline.md` | UPDATED | this entry |

#### Key findings

**Platform dependency:** YouTube/Google is the foundational revenue source. Two suspensions in 2022 (NRA joke; Holocaust comment re: Shapiro) demonstrated YouTube's content veto power. Klein cannot survive platform removal — no alternative channel generates comparable revenue.

**BBTV extraction:** Klein partnered with creator network BBTV in 2017. In April 2023, he publicly accused BBTV of withholding $620,000 in YouTube membership revenue. BBTV repaid after public pressure. Illustrates the MCN extraction layer built into the creator economy.

**FEC records:** OpenSecrets shows two likely Ethan Klein (CA) donations in the 2020 cycle — $1,000 to Bernie Sanders (Encino, CA; occupation listed as "NASA TOILET CLEANER" — consistent with Klein's humor) and $500 to Joe Biden (Agoura Hills, CA; occupation ENTERTAINER). Total documented political giving: $1,500. No PAC or dark money connections.

**October 7 audience capture:** The Leftovers podcast with Hasan Piker (Sep 2021–Nov 2023) ended after a near-4-hour October 7 debate episode. Klein's pro-Israel/anti-Piker positioning after October 7 progressively segmented his audience toward right-liberal and Destiny-adjacent (DGG) viewers.

**DGG sponsorship loop:** January 2025 "Content Nuke" against Hasan Piker (allegedly co-produced with DGG community members) followed by Redact (Dan Saltman, DGG-adjacent) sponsoring H3 Podcast in June 2025. Content-to-sponsorship pipeline documented.

**Copyright enforcement as political targeting:** June 2025 lawsuit against three streamers for reacting to the Content Nuke. Klein publicly described it as a deliberate "trap." Ironic given h3h3Productions' own landmark 2017 fair use victory.

**Teddy Fresh hedge:** Hila Klein's clothing brand (CEO since 2017) is the only revenue stream not mediated by YouTube, BBTV, or sponsor politics. Functions as a financial independence buffer. Teddy Glow (makeup line) launched November 2025.

#### Source decisions

- **FEC contributions:** Two California-address records assigned to H3H3 Klein based on address (Encino/Agoura Hills, LA area), occupation joking style ("NASA TOILET CLEANER"), and 2020 political pattern (Sanders primary + Biden general). Cannot be 100% confirmed without first-person disclosure; noted as "likely" in profile.
- **TheWrap anti-SLAPP article:** URL broken (404). Substituted with Dexerto article covering the same ruling.
- **Medium "Troubling Pivot" article:** Verified but treated as Tier 4 — unsigned partisan blog post. Not cited in sources section; used for background framing only.
- **Patreon:** Graphtreon confirms Patreon inactive since July 2020 (peak ~$2,661/month, ~1,174 patrons). Not a meaningful revenue source.
- **Sponsor list:** No comprehensive historical list available. Lower Street advertising page confirms current sponsors (Seek, Stamps.com, Angi, LinkedIn, Etsy). Historical sponsors (Squarespace, HelloFresh, Manscaped) documented via secondary sources.

#### URL verification

17 URLs Chrome-verified VALID. 1 BROKEN (TheWrap). All logged to Source URL Audit Log under "Media Pipeline — Ethan Klein Profile."

#### Analytical patterns flagged

- **Audience Capture** — primary pattern; content shifts toward most-engaged segment's interests
- **Platform Dependency** — YouTube controls monetization eligibility and distribution
- **Independence Theater** — "independent" creator fully dependent on Google algorithm and brand sponsors
- **Sponsor Veto** — two suspensions demonstrate advertiser-flight risk shapes content
- **Both-Sides Illusion (partial)** — DGG/Destiny-adjacent audience overlap with ostensibly "left" branding

#### Media Pipeline status after this run

- Profiles: 0 ready / 5 developed / 3 draft / 0 raw / 7 planned
- Left queue: Cenk Uygur next (priority #7 in build order)

#### Next session priorities

1. **Cenk Uygur** — next in Left queue; TYT funding rounds (Buddy Roemer, Jeffrey Katzenberg), 2024 presidential run
2. **Hasan Piker** — after Cenk; Twitch/Amazon revenue model, socialist content on corporate platform
3. **Megyn Kelly** — next in Centrist queue; Fox → NBC → independent funding transitions

### Automated Think Tank Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (think-tank-builder Run 6)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** think-tank-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Budget | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|--------|-----------|
| `American Enterprise Institute.md` | draft (NEW) | $68M revenue / $353M assets (FY2024) | Carlyle Group/D'Aniello $20M + board chairmanship; Harlan Crow board member (SCOTUS-Thomas ethics scandal); DonorsTrust $86.7M (2003–2010); Iraq surge blueprint; climate denial $10K scientist payments; Kevin Hassett → Trump CEA → TCJA; 0/5 transparency |

#### What was built

Created `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Conservative/American Enterprise Institute.md` — full think tank profile following Framework anatomy. 16 Chrome-verified sources (Tier 1–3). Content-readiness: draft.

**Key analytical findings:**

- **The Harlan Crow / AEI / Clarence Thomas triangle is the vault's sharpest documented donor-court pipeline.** Crow has been on the AEI board for 20+ years while simultaneously providing undisclosed luxury travel, private school tuition, and real estate transactions to Justice Thomas (documented by ProPublica). The same donor class that funds AEI's anti-regulatory research also finances informal patronage of the Supreme Court justices who rule on those regulations. These are not separate systems — they are nodes in a single class infrastructure.
- **The Carlyle Group connection makes AEI's defense spending research self-serving at the board level.** D'Aniello chairs both AEI and Carlyle — a private equity giant with major defense industry holdings. AEI research consistently supports larger military budgets, sustained overseas deployments, and expanded defense contracting. The Iraq surge — AEI's most direct policy success — extended a war generating billions for defense contractors including Carlyle portfolio companies. The $20M building donation in 2014 was infrastructure investment in the institution producing the intellectual cover for Carlyle's preferred policy environment.
- **DonorsTrust is the primary funding vehicle, not individual donors.** $86.7M from DonorsTrust (2003–2010) was AEI's single largest funding source over that period. This is the dark money ATM routing anonymous donor money into AEI's operating budget. DonorsTrust board member Kimberly Dennis simultaneously sits on AEI's board of trustees — a confirmed institutional linkage between the anonymizing vehicle and the policy shop it funds.
- **0/5 transparency score confirms the dark money architecture.** AEI has never published a public donor list. What is known about its funding comes from IRS 990 board rosters, leaked documents, and investigative reporting. The institution's intellectual credibility depends on appearing independent; its funding requires opacity.
- **Kevin Hassett's AEI → Trump CEA pipeline is the TCJA's intellectual supply chain.** Hassett was AEI's Director of Economic Policy Studies before becoming Trump's Council of Economic Advisers chair. His AEI research on corporate taxation shaped his government work. The TCJA (2017) cut the corporate rate 35%→21% — a permanent change benefiting AEI's entire corporate donor base while individual cuts sunset in 2025.
- **The 2007 IPCC scientist payment offer is the clearest documented climate denial purchase.** AEI offered $10,000 per article to scientists willing to criticize the IPCC Fourth Assessment Report — funded by an institution receiving millions from ExxonMobil, whose former CEO sat on AEI's board. This is not climate skepticism; it is climate denial as a purchased service.

#### URL verification

16 URLs Chrome-verified. 15 VALID, 1 BROKEN. All logged to Source URL Audit Log.

**VALID (15):** ProPublica 990 (FY2024 — confirmed Harlan Crow, Dick Cheney, D'Aniello, Kimberly Dennis, Clifford Asness on board; $68M revenue, $353M assets), OpenSecrets AEI profile, ProPublica Trump Town AEI, ProPublica Supreme Connections AEI, ProPublica Clarence Thomas luxury trips (Crow), ProPublica Clarence Thomas tuition (Crow), AEI press release $20M D'Aniello gift, WaPo D'Aniello $20M, DeSmog AEI climate denial, EWG AEI climate/suitcase of cash, Think Tank Funding Tracker (0/5 transparency), SourceWatch AEI, InfluenceWatch AEI, Militarist Monitor AEI, Ballotpedia AEI, Wikipedia AEI.

**BROKEN (1):** `https://accountable.us/` — redirects to accountable.us homepage; AEI-specific page no longer accessible. Not used in profile.

#### Files created/modified

- `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Conservative/American Enterprise Institute.md` — NEW (draft, 16 Chrome-verified sources, Tier 1–3)
- `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/_Think Tank Index.md` — AEI updated (not started → draft), count updated (5 → 6 profiles, Conservative 3→4), stats updated (5 draft → 6 draft)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` — "Think Tanks — American Enterprise Institute Profile" section added (15 VALID, 1 BROKEN)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Session Timeline.md` — this entry

#### Research gaps flagged

- **Specific IRS 990 donor schedule:** AEI's Schedule B (donor list) is filed with the IRS but not publicly disclosed — only accessible via FOIA or investigative reporting leak. The $86.7M DonorsTrust figure (2003–2010) is from aggregated SourceWatch/Conservative Transparency reporting, not a single verified document.
- **Scaife Foundations dollar amounts:** Scaife is confirmed as an AEI funder but specific amounts by year are from secondary aggregation (Conservative Transparency), not from a primary IRS document verified this run.
- **Olin Foundation:** The John M. Olin Foundation (major funder of conservative intellectual infrastructure through 2005 when it spent down) is not documented in this profile. InfluenceWatch references Olin connection — worth adding in a future pass.
- **Peter Wallison FCIC dissent:** This is the profile's strongest policy→outcome link after Iraq surge, but the specific paper citations should be added: *Dissent from the Majority Report of the Financial Crisis Inquiry Commission* (January 2011). Add with Congress.gov citation in a future pass.
- **Frederick Kagan "Choosing Victory" direct URL:** AEI.org hosts the report but the specific URL was not Chrome-verified this run (to avoid over-extending). Confirm URL for the Kagan 2006 report before promoting to `developed`.

- Liberal: 1/4 (CAP built; Brookings, EPI, Roosevelt remaining)

### Automated Lobbying Firm Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (lobbying-firm-builder Run 7)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder

#### Profile built

**K&L Gates** — `topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/K&L Gates.md`

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Status | draft (new) |
| Revenue | $17.2M (2024), 124 clients |
| Lobbyist count | 44 |
| Revolving door | 63.6% (28 of 44) |
| Former Congress members | 2 active (Doyle, Gordon; Denham left for Dentons 2023) |
| Source tier | 1 (OpenSecrets) |
| URLs verified | 9 VALID / 0 BROKEN |

#### Key analytical findings

- **Maritime dominance is K&L Gates' defining differentiator.** American Maritime Partnership ($1,140,000) + Lake Carriers' Association ($790,000) + Consortium of State Maritime Academies ($240,000) + Transportation Institute ($240,000) + American Association of Port Authorities ($90,000) = $2.5M+ in maritime billing — ~14.5% of the firm's total 2024 revenue from a single industry. No other major lobbying firm matches this maritime concentration. The Jones Act (Merchant Marine Act of 1920) is the legislative anchor: K&L Gates deploys its transportation-specialized revolving door hires (Denham, former Transportation subcommittee chair; Doyle, Energy & Commerce) to keep 104-year-old shipping protectionism politically viable in every administration.

- **Pitney Bowes ($610K) + Package Coalition ($480K) = $1.09M** makes mail/shipping logistics the firm's second-largest sectoral concentration, closely related to maritime. K&L Gates is effectively the K Street shop for the physical goods movement economy.

- **The ByteDance/TikTok hire play:** K&L Gates deployed three former Congress members (Doyle, Denham, Gordon) plus Trahern (former Comcast Senate Democratic lobbyist) on ByteDance's account. The bipartisan team's job was time-buying and relationship management during the PAFACA divestiture fight. Even though the legislation passed, the K&L Gates hire significantly extended ByteDance's operational timeline by maintaining congressional relationships and enabling legal challenge strategy.

- **Trahern hire crystallizes the telecom revolving door:** Former Clinton White House → Daschle/Durbin Senate staff → GM government affairs → Comcast federal affairs (lead Senate Democratic lobbyist) → K&L Gates. The entire career is a progressive monetization of successive government access peaks. Comcast's Comcast-NBCUniversal transaction lobbying experience is directly deployed for ByteDance's regulatory fight.

- **The Finch Fulton-Bipartisan Infrastructure Law connection** is the firmwide ROI story for transportation clients. Fulton co-authored USDOT's infrastructure proposal that became the BIL's foundation while serving as Trump DOT deputy, then joined K&L Gates. The maritime, port, and logistics clients who received BIL-funded infrastructure investments were paying K&L Gates while Fulton was shaping the underlying policy architecture.

Expanded to `developed` status with full research on AIPAC/UDP spending patterns, 2022 and 2024 primary dynamics, the committee removal mechanics, and the systemic chilling effect on other members.

#### Key analytical additions

- **2022 primary specifics:** UDP spent ~$350K; Omar won by 2.15 points (50.35% vs. 48.2%) — an 18-point swing from her 2020 primary margin. Near-miss served disciplinary function regardless of outcome.
- **2024 strategic withdrawal:** AIPAC/UDP largely sat out the 2024 Omar rematch (gave $25 to Samuels). Rationale: capital deployed against Bowman and Bush had higher ROI — both lost. UDP spent $14.6M vs. Bowman, $8.6M vs. Bush in 2024.
- **Republican-funded Democratic PAC structure:** 46% of UDP contributions in 2024 came from donors who gave to Republican candidates/causes since 2020. Jan Koum ($5M), Bernie Marcus ($1M, Trump donor), Paul Singer ($1M, Haley donor), Bob Kraft ($500K).
- **H.Res.76 corrected:** The existing source cited H.Res.9 (COVID/China resolution) — wrong. Real resolution is H.Res.76 ("Removing a certain Member from a certain standing committee of the House"). Corrected in file and flagged as broken in audit log.
- **UDP 2024 total scale:** $95.1M total spending — more than double 2022's $44M. PAC had $40M cash on hand entering 2024.
- **Committee removal precedent section:** Added structural analysis of how committee removal functions as a discipline mechanism and connects to the 2021 MTG/Gosar precedent.
- **Chilling effect section:** Added analysis of the disciplinary signal the AIPAC spending strategy sends to other members.

## Session — Media Profile Builder Automated Run — 2026-03-26

**Type:** Automated scheduled task (media-profile-builder)
**Task:** Build Cenk Uygur media profile (9th profile in queue)

### What was built

**New file created:**
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Left/Cenk Uygur.md` — Draft media profile, 18 Chrome-verified sources

**Files updated:**
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/_Media Pipeline Index.md` — Updated Cenk Uygur status from "not started" to "draft"; total profiles 8→9; stats updated (4 draft, 5 developed)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` — Added Media Pipeline — Cenk Uygur section with 18 verified URLs

### Key findings

- **The Katzenberg contradiction:** Jeffrey Katzenberg — Hollywood Democratic mega-bundler (Obama, Biden, Harris) — holds equity in TYT through his WndrCo fund as part of the 2017 $20M round. The platform that most loudly criticized the Democratic donor class never disclosed on-air that its largest institutional investor was a core member of that class.
- **Republican PE money at the foundation:** The 2014 $4M investment came from former Republican Louisiana Governor Buddy Roemer's private equity fund (RRM), which received an advisory board seat. TYT's "independent progressive" brand was built on Republican PE money from Day 1.
- **Union-busting contradiction (2020):** Uygur, who advocates for labor on-air, urged his own workers not to unionize during IATSE's 2020 organizing drive. Unfair labor practice charges were filed alleging retaliatory firing and withheld raises. Documented in New Republic ("The Myth of the Progressive Boss") and HuffPost.
- **2024 presidential run:** Launched October 2023, raised $250K from 6,800+ donors, dropped out March 2024. FEC committee: P40015752. Constitutional eligibility (naturalized citizen) never resolved.
- **Post-2024 MAGA pivot:** After Trump's November 2024 win, Uygur spoke at Turning Point USA AmericaFest and declared "MAGA is not my mortal enemy." TYT contributor Mondale Robinson resigned live on air. The pivot mirrors the broader audience-capture dynamic: progressive branding served its audience-building function; when audience declined, the frame shifted.

### Source verification

- 18 URLs Chrome-verified via document.title check, 0 broken
- Sources span: Variety, Tubefilter, TheWrap, FEC.gov, The Hill, The New Republic, In These Times, HuffPost, Digiday (x2), Deadline, Axios, Hollywood Reporter, Wikipedia (x2), Ballotpedia
- Source tier distribution: 2 Tier 1 (FEC), 9 Tier 2, 7 Tier 3
- All logged in Source URL Audit Log under "Media Pipeline — Cenk Uygur Profile"

### Editorial decisions

- Profile classified as `draft` — full section structure and 18 verified sources, but needs: deeper research on specific TYT subscriber/revenue numbers post-2022, Katzenberg's specific board role (advisory vs. governance), the TYT layoff specifics from 2023–24, and whether any FEC-disclosable relationships exist between Katzenberg and TYT as entity (corporate vs. personal investment)
- Analytical patterns flagged: **Independence Theater**, **Audience Capture**, **Revolving Door (Media)**, **Both-Sides Illusion (Media)**
- `related:` wikilinks to Jeffrey Katzenberg, Greycroft, WndrCo — none of these are confirmed vault donor nodes; noted but not linked until confirmed
- The Both-Sides Illusion (Media) pattern is particularly strong here: Uygur built an audience criticizing both parties' donor classes while his own platform was funded by the same class

## Session — Think Tank Builder Automated Run — 2026-03-26

**Type:** Automated scheduled task (think-tank-builder)
**Task:** Build Manhattan Institute profile (7th profile in queue)

### What was built

**New file created:**
- `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Conservative/Manhattan Institute.md` — Draft profile, 13 Chrome-verified sources

**Files updated:**
- `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/_Think Tank Index.md` — Updated Manhattan Institute status from "not started" to "draft (2026-03-26)"; total profiles 6→7; stats updated (7 draft)
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` — Added Think Tanks — Manhattan Institute section with 13 verified URLs

### Key findings

- **Board = donor class roster:** Chairman Paul Singer (Elliott Management, ~$55B AUM) heads a board of trustees that reads as a hedge fund conference list: Ravenel Curry (Eagle Capital), former trustee Daniel Loeb (Third Point), former trustee Clifford Asness (AQR Capital), Robert Rosenkranz (Delphi Financial), Harvey Golub and Maurice Greenberg (AIG). The research consistently serves the same interests as the trustees.
- **Cabinet-level revolving door:** Three trustees or alumni moved directly into Trump administration cabinet/subcabinet positions: Diana Furchtgott-Roth → Deputy Secretary of Transportation, Paul I. Howard → HHS, David Malpass (Trustee) → Treasury Under Secretary then World Bank President. William Barr (Trustee) served as Trump AG. Betsy DeVos (Trustee) served as Trump Education Secretary.
- **Broken Windows pipeline:** MI Senior Fellow George Kelling co-authored the 1982 "Broken Windows" theory; City Journal (MI's publication, founded 1990) shaped Giuliani's 1993 campaign strategy through direct consulting by editor Fred Siegel; Giuliani implemented Broken Windows and stop-and-frisk as NYPD doctrine. The pipeline from MI research to city policing policy is documented and direct.
- **Christopher Rufo DEI model legislation:** Rufo (MI Senior Fellow, $282,293/yr FY2023) produced the model DEI legislation brief that 20+ states used to ban DEI offices. Florida's Stop WOKE Act (2022) was drafted with his consultation. The "Manhattan Statement" on higher education (2025) was endorsed by Education Secretary Linda McMahon — an MI product becoming federal policy within months of publication.
- **0/100 transparency score:** Charity Navigator rates MI 0/100 on accountability and transparency. Schedule B (donor names) withheld. Koch network: ~$3.4M documented. Bradley Foundation: ~$5.78M since 1986. Paul Singer's foundation: $525K in 2016 alone. The actual donor list is almost certainly more concentrated than what investigative journalism has surfaced.
- **FY2023 financials (IRS 990):** Revenue $26,924,098 / Expenses $21,583,113 / Total assets $40,635,970. 96%+ contributions. Reihan Salam (President): $686,688 compensation. Christopher Rufo: $282,293.

Needs before `developed` promotion: deeper Bradley Foundation grant specifics (year-by-year beyond 1986/2019 datapoints), Rufo's Stop WOKE Act consulting documented with a Tier 2 source (Wikipedia cited, should find ProPublica or investigative journalism confirmation), IRS 990 Schedule B supplementation if any donors come out in investigative reporting
- Analytical patterns flagged: **Idea Laundering** (Broken Windows → NYPD, Rufo CRT → state bans), **Model Legislation Pipeline** (DEI brief → 20+ state laws), **Revolving Door** (Furchtgott-Roth, Howard, Malpass, Barr, DeVos), **Villain Framing** (crime/poverty as behavioral), **Regulatory Capture Pipeline** (board members into cabinet)

This was the #1 priority identified by the previous thin-profile-expansion run. Expanded to `developed` status with full research covering Veterans Affairs/Armed Services Committee work, PACT Act blockade and reversal (2022), Agriculture Committee record (ethanol, crop insurance, CAFO), donation-to-policy timeline, and class analysis.

### Key analytical additions

- **PACT Act blockade and reversal (July–August 2022):** Ernst — a combat veteran — voted to block burn pit compensation for Iowa veterans as part of a Senate Republican procedural maneuver. Iowa veterans publicly condemned the vote. She reversed under pressure on August 3, 2022 (final passage 86-11). Documented by Iowa Capital Dispatch, NPR, Radio Iowa. The sequence reveals the Two-Audience Problem in concrete form: when given a choice between brand-consistent veteran advocacy and party conference procedure, she chose the party line and corrected only under public pressure.
- **VA MISSION Act + Community Care privatization:** Ernst's VETS Act telehealth provision was a genuine win for rural Iowa veterans AND part of the structural expansion of private sector VA billing. CBO documented community care costs grew from $7.9B (2014) to $17.6B (2021) — 122% increase. The Genuine Win + Structural Limit pattern applies.
- **Agriculture sector data (verified):** Career agribusiness total $1.84M (OpenSecrets industries, Career cycle). Includes Agricultural Services/Products ($534K), Crop Production & Basic Processing ($472K), Oil & Gas ($588K ethanol crossover). Defense only $318K career — surprisingly low for Armed Services Committee member, suggesting defense value is delivered through votes not PAC receipts.
- **FARMER Act, EATS Act, RFS record:** Crop insurance expansion (highest-coverage tiers benefit large commercial operations), CAFO deregulation framed as family farm protection, ethanol mandate defense. All deliver for corporate agribusiness while using family farm constituent framing.
- **Format 3 timeline table:** 9 rows covering 2013–2025. `[!money]` and `[!contradiction]` callouts added.

## Automated Run — 2026-03-26 — Thin Profile Expansion (thin-profile-expansion Run 2)

**Type:** Automated scheduled task (`thin-profile-expansion`)
**Task:** Expand thin and placeholder profiles from raw stubs to developed status

### Summary

**1 sub-note expanded from 41 lines (thin stub, fraudulent `ready`) to 183 lines (developed) | 14 URLs Chrome-verified | 0 broken**

Target selected: `topics/Politicians/Democrats/Senate/Tammy Baldwin/The HELP Committee and Prescription Drug Pricing.md` — #2 priority from previous thin-profile-expansion run. 41-line stub marked `ready` with only 3 sources and no timeline table. Expanded to `developed` with full section structure covering the FAIR Drug Pricing Act legislative history (2017–2023), insulin pricing fight (IRA Medicare cap + INSULIN Act private insurance failure), February 2024 HELP Committee pharmaceutical CEO hearing, health sector donor architecture analysis, 9-row Format 3 timeline table, and class analysis section.

### Profile expanded

**The HELP Committee and Prescription Drug Pricing** — sub-note under `_Tammy Baldwin Master Profile`

| Field | Before | After |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Status | ready (thin/fraudulent) | developed |
| Line count | 41 | 183 |
| Source count | 3 | 14 Chrome-verified |
| Broken URLs | 0 | 0 |
| Sections | 2 | 7 + timeline table |

### Key analytical additions

- **FAIR Drug Pricing Act structural tell:** Baldwin introduced this transparency bill in four Congresses (2017, 2019, 2021, 2023). It passed HELP Committee in May 2023 and never came to a Senate floor vote. The bill requires disclosure of price increases but does not cap prices or prohibit increases. Six years of reintroduction, no floor vote — the bill that never threatens actual pricing power is the bill that keeps getting introduced as a populist credential.
- **Insulin fight — real win, structural limit:** The IRA (2022) Medicare $35/month insulin cap is a genuine policy outcome for Wisconsin Medicare beneficiaries. The INSULIN Act (2023) extending that cap to private insurance did not pass. The structural limit is the mechanism: Medicare-administered drug pricing wins advance; private insurance wins that would require confronting insurer business models don't.
- **February 2024 HELP hearing:** Sanders convened pharmaceutical CEO testimony (J&J, Merck, BMS). All Democratic HELP members including Baldwin participated. CEOs refused to commit to price reductions. No floor legislation followed. The hearing is institutional pressure theater with documented record — real political friction with no structural outcome.
- **Donor architecture reveals the incentive:** Baldwin's health sector career total ($5,042,399) is dominated by Health Professionals and health services/HMOs — not pharmaceutical companies. The pharmaceutical subsector she most aggressively targets represents a small fraction of her health sector fundraising. The insurer and hospital donors who dominate her health total benefit from lower pharmaceutical prices. Her drug pricing populism serves the donor interests of her non-pharmaceutical health sector contributors while generating populist credibility against PhRMA.
- **CMS Medicare negotiation (Aug 2024):** IRA drug price negotiation produced 38–79% discounts on 10 Part D drugs, effective Jan 2026. Projected $6B Medicare savings if 2023 prices applied. This is real policy output from legislation Baldwin supported.

### Status grading

Promoted to `developed` (not `ready`):

- 7 ### sections + timeline table ✅
- 183 lines ✅
- Format 3 timeline table with 9 rows ✅
- `[!money]` and `[!contradiction]` callouts ✅

Full ready promotion requires a direct Tier 1 citation for that specific figure, plus a citation pass on the INSULIN Act Senate procedure (Byrd rule insulin cap failure in IRA).

## Session — Lobbying Firm Builder Automated Run — 2026-03-26

**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder (scheduled automated run)
**Date:** 2026-03-26
**Profile built:** Invariant.md
**Queue position:** #8 of 10 (Thorn Run Partners and Cornerstone Government Affairs remain)

---

### What was built

**`topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/Invariant.md`** — new file, `draft` status

Full profile of Invariant LLC, Washington D.C.'s 6th-largest lobbying firm (top 5 by 2025). Founded 2007 by Heather Podesta as Heather Podesta + Partners; rebranded March 2017 as "Invariant" after Trump's election to shed the Democratic brand identity and go bipartisan.

**Key data points captured:**
- Revenue: $42.26M (2024), $47.18M (2025); 189 clients (2024), 210 clients (2025)
- 59 lobbyists (2024); 32 revolvers = 54.2% revolving door rate
- Top clients: Apple ($680K/$710K), Palantir ($560K), SpaceX (14+ year retainer), RTX/Raytheon, Airbus, ARM Holdings, DraftKings, Kraken, Business Roundtable
- Invariant is highest-paid lobbying firm for both SpaceX AND Palantir simultaneously

**Revolving door hires documented:**
- Paul Arcangeli: HASC Staff Director 2004–2022 (18 years) → Invariant principal 2022; now lobbies Raytheon, Palantir, SpaceX, Airbus
- Shari Yost Gold: VP Kamala Harris Senior Advisor → Invariant Senior Advisor June 2022
- Kenneth Barbic, Matthew Fery, Christopher Gillott, Noah Kowalski, Maia Hunt Estes (all revolving door profiles, backgrounds consistent with client portfolios)

**Core analytical finding — the Palantir/DCCC nexus:**
Invariant bundled $4M+ to DCCC and hosted Schumer fundraisers in Q1 2025 while simultaneously lobbying Congress for Palantir's ICE surveillance contracts. Democrats who publicly criticized ICE deportations were being funded by the same firm lobbying to expand Palantir's border surveillance AI. The "Both-Sides Illusion" and "Two-Audience Problem" patterns documented explicitly.

**Analytical patterns flagged:**
- Both-Sides Illusion (Democrats and Palantir/SpaceX sharing same K Street infrastructure)
- Revolving Door Hub (54.2% rate; Arcangeli HASC → defense contractor access)
- Bipartisan Access Machine (engineered through 2017 rebrand, not organic)
- Bundling Operation (Invariant lobbyists as DCCC/DSCC bundlers)

**Sources:** 11 total — 10 VALID, 1 BROKEN (The Intercept 2017 article, pre-restructuring URL). All VALID URLs Chrome-verified via `document.title` check.

---

### Key editorial decisions

- Used Format 2 (Donor Node table format) adapted for lobbying firm per Framework doc instruction: `### Lobbying-to-Policy Timeline` with `| Date | Recipient/Target | Amount | Policy Return | Time Gap |` columns
- 8 timeline rows covering 2017–2025
- Broken Intercept URL not included in profile (marked BROKEN in audit log); The Hill + Wikipedia provided equivalent founding-story coverage at Tier 3
- Kept revolving door background details for Barbic/Fery/Gillott/Kowalski/Estes as "consistent with client portfolio" given unavailability of specific prior government positions without LegiStorm premium access; these should be verified and expanded in a future session

## Session — Think Tank Builder Automated Run — 2026-03-26

**Task:** think-tank-builder (scheduled automated run)
**Date:** 2026-03-26
**Profile built:** Economic Policy Institute.md
**Queue position:** #8 of 12 (Council on Foreign Relations, RAND Corporation, Bipartisan Policy Center, Roosevelt Institute remain)

---

### What was built

**`topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Liberal/Economic Policy Institute.md`** — new file, `draft` status

Full profile of the Economic Policy Institute (EPI) — the labor movement's primary research institution, founded 1986, headquartered Washington D.C.

**Key data points captured:**
- Budget: FY2024 revenue $11.9M, expenses $13.6M, total assets $19.6M (from ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer IRS 990 data)
- Funding (2021): foundations 79%, labor unions 14%, corporations 2%, individuals 3%
- Top donors 2021: AFL-CIO, AFT, NEA, SEIU, UAW, UFCW, USW at $100K–$500K+ tiers; Ford Foundation, Gates Foundation, Omidyar Network, Robert Wood Johnson at $200K+
- Board chaired by AFL-CIO President Elizabeth Shuler; Shawn Fain (UAW) on board as of 2024
- Staff: ~40–50 based on 990 compensation data

**Revolving door documented:**
- Heidi Shierholz: EPI economist (2007–2014) → Obama DOL Chief Economist (2014–2017) → EPI President (2021–)
- Thea Lee: EPI President (2017–2021) → Biden DOL Deputy Undersecretary for International Affairs (2021–)
- Robert Reich: EPI co-founder → Clinton DOL Secretary (1993–1997)
- Thomas Perez: Obama DOL Secretary → EPI board member (2022)
- AFL-CIO presidents Richard Trumka and Elizabeth Shuler as consecutive board chairs

**Policy pipeline documented:**
- $15 minimum wage research ("32 million workers" study) cited in Raise the Wage Act debates
- PRO Act advocacy — EPI research that employers violate labor law in 41.5% of union elections became core Congressional testimony
- NLRB Biden board analysis provided policy template for Biden NLRB reform agenda
- Overtime rule modeling supported Biden DOL 2024 expansion

**Notable anomalies flagged:**
- Peter G. Peterson Foundation ($50K–$99K) — deficit hawk funder; EPI opposes entitlement cuts
- Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation ($200K+) — Microsoft union-avoidance history; Gates charter school agenda conflicts with AFT/NEA positions
- Tobacco Institute funding (1980s) — documented in academic literature; early funder-directed research episode

**Analytical patterns:** Regulatory Capture Pipeline, Bipartisan Credibility Shield (academic branding over institutional funder alignment), Genuine Win + Structural Limit

**Sources:** 14 total — all Chrome-verified VALID. 1 BROKEN NPR URL documented (wrong article ID; correct ID 995542715 used).

---

### Key editorial decisions

- Categorized as `liberal` per Framework doc definition; "liberal" here is an analytical category tested against funding data, not an endorsement
- EPI Action (501c4 arm) noted but not profiled separately — same leadership/staff, different disclosure rules
- Gates/Peterson donor anomalies flagged as analytical data points, not errors; they represent the class dynamics of billionaire philanthropy to labor-aligned institutions
- Class analysis section emphasizes structural parallel between EPI (labor-funded "independent research") and Heritage/AEI (corporate-funded "independent research"); directional difference is real, structural function is identical
- Content-readiness set to `draft` — has all required sections and 14 verified sources, but needs additional IRS 990 data on specific union donation amounts (which require Schedule B which is not publicly disclosed by the IRS for 501c3s) and deeper policy-citation mapping before `developed` promotion

---

### URL verification

14 Chrome-verified VALID. 1 BROKEN (NPR wrong ID 994859680 → corrected to 995542715). All logged to Source URL Audit Log under "Think Tanks — Economic Policy Institute Profile."

## Session — Finance Research Automated Run — 2026-03-26

**Task:** gp-finance-research (scheduled automated run)
**Date:** 2026-03-26

---

### What was researched

**Priority action:** Verified 3 URLs carried over from March 25 as UNVERIFIED — all three confirmed VALID (Axios midterms spending, LegiStorm revolving door, Revolving Door Project DOGE analysis). These are now cleared for use in `ready`-status files.

**GEO Group** — Full 2024 campaign finance profile documented. $3.7M in contributions (96% to Republicans), $1.38M lobbying, $500K inaugural committee. Policy return: Day 1 reversal of Biden private prison order + $45B in new ICE contract solicitations within 60 days. Pam Bondi connection documented ($390K lobbying fee → U.S. Attorney General). Revolving door board fully mapped (Bible, Albence, Wood, Koren). Highest single-entity ROI documented in vault outside defense contractors.

**PhRMA** — Ranked 3rd of 9,200 lobbying organizations in 2024 at $31.72M. On pace for $37.9M record in 2025. 123 of 201 lobbyists (61%) former government officials. Orphan drug exemption expansion win in One Big Beautiful Bill (July 2025) documented. $4M dark money gift to American Action Network (House Republican leadership-aligned).

**RTX Corp (Raytheon)** — $13.5M lobbying in 2024 (19th of 9,200); $3.87M contributions; bipartisan both-sides contribution structure confirmed (gave to Kamala Harris AND RNC simultaneously). 71% revolving door rate (55 of 77 lobbyists former government). Classic Both-Sides Illusion donor pattern.

**United Auto Workers** — $6.2M 2024 cycle; Senate Majority PAC top recipient at $1.74M. 100% of outside spending for Democrats. Notable contrast: 0% revolving door rate vs. 71–92% for GEO Group and RTX. $40M organizing drive commitment (2025–2026) for non-union auto/battery workers in the South flagged as analytically significant.

**David Sacks** — Foundational profile data. $300K-per-head June 2024 Trump fundraiser co-host; White House AI and Crypto Czar December 2024. Craft Ventures conflict of interest documented. 2 URLs UNVERIFIED — require Chrome verification next session.

## Session — Media Profile Builder Automated Run — 2026-03-26 (Megyn Kelly)

**Type:** Automated scheduled task (media-profile-builder)
**Task:** Build Megyn Kelly media profile (11th profile in queue, 2nd in Centrist category)

### What was built

**New file created:**
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Centrist/Megyn Kelly.md` — Draft media profile, 19 Chrome-verified sources

**Files updated:**
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/_Media Pipeline Index.md` — Megyn Kelly status updated from "not started" to "draft"; total profiles 10→11; stats updated (5 developed → 5, 5 draft → 6); planned 5→4
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Source URL Audit Log.md` — Added "Media Pipeline — Megyn Kelly Profile" section with 19 verified URLs

### Key findings

- **The Fox loop closes:** Kelly departed Fox in 2017 with NBC's $69M deal, walked with ~$30M after show cancellation in 2019, spent 5 years building "independent" media through Devil May Care Media + Red Seat Ventures, then Fox Corp acquired Red Seat Ventures in February 2025. Her ad-sales infrastructure is now formally owned by Fox Corp. The independence narrative is editorial, not financial.
- **The Hope Hicks hire:** In July 2025, Kelly hired Hope Hicks — Trump's former White House communications director and Fox Corp's former EVP/Chief Communications Officer — as COO of Devil May Care Media. Trump-aligned communications infrastructure is now embedded in Kelly's operational leadership.
- **MK Media / Mark Halperin:** Kelly launched her MK Media podcast network in March 2025 with co-founder Mark Halperin, the former NBC/ABC political analyst ousted in 2017 following #MeToo allegations. The partnership is a mutual rehabilitation — Kelly's network gives Halperin a platform he couldn't rebuild through traditional institutional channels; Halperin's political reporting credibility gives MK Media a political journalism anchor.
- **The Trump reconciliation arc:** Kelly confronted Trump at the August 2015 GOP debate, spent two years as a Trump critic, quietly voted for Trump in 2020, and publicly endorsed him at a Pittsburgh rally the day before the 2024 election. The 9-year arc from adversary to rally speaker is the defining political trajectory of her "independent" period.
- **Audience outperformance:** Kelly's YouTube channel logged 116M views in July 2023 alone, exceeding NBC News (78M) and CBS News (83M) that month. By 2025, annual YouTube views exceeded 1.4 billion. The "independent" model delivered larger audiences than her NBC institutional deal.
- **The SiriusXM institutional anchor:** Three successive SiriusXM deals (2021, 2023, 2025) make SiriusXM — majority-owned by Liberty Media — the structural backbone of her revenue model. The 2025 deal gave her a dedicated channel (The Megyn Kelly Channel, SiriusXM Ch. 111). Undisclosed contract terms.

### Source verification

- 19 URLs Chrome-verified via document.title check, 0 broken
- Sources: NBC News, Variety (x3), NPR, PBS NewsHour, SiriusXM press releases (x2, Tier 1), Hollywood Reporter (x4), Deadline, The Hill, Semafor, ABC News, PBS Frontline, Wikipedia

### Automated Think Tank Profile Build — March 26, 2026 (think-tank-builder Run 9)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** think-tank-builder

#### Profile built

| Profile | Status | Budget | Key Angle |
|---------|--------|--------|-----------|
| `Council on Foreign Relations.md` | draft (NEW) | $79.1M revenue / $692M endowment (FY2024) | Carlyle Group/Rubenstein chairman, $2.4M+ Pentagon contractor funding, Froman USTR revolving door, Blavatnik $12M Russia-tied donation, Sue Mi Terry FARA arrest, Kennan/containment→Marshall Plan/NATO historical pipeline, corporate members: Goldman Sachs/BlackRock/JPMorgan/ExxonMobil/Lockheed/Northrop — 12 sources, all Chrome-verified |

#### Key research findings

- **$692M endowment** makes CFR substantially self-sustaining — but the 29% endowment draw co-exists with a corporate membership structure that includes every major Wall Street bank and defense contractor
- **Chairman lineage = Wall Street lineage:** David Rockefeller (Chase Manhattan, 1970-1985) → Peter Peterson (Blackstone, 1985-2007) → Robert Rubin (Goldman Sachs, 2007-2017) → David Rubenstein (Carlyle Group, 2019-present). Every CFR chairman for 50+ years has been a top-tier financier.
- **Froman revolving door:** Citigroup → CFR Fellow → Obama NSC → USTR (negotiated TPP) → Mastercard VP → CFR President. Critics specifically cited "taking care of his friends on Wall Street" during TPP negotiations.
- **Blavatnik $12M (2019):** Russia-tied oligarch (business partners sanctioned by U.S. Treasury) funds 100+ paid internships/year in the U.S. foreign policy apparatus. CFR refused to return donation despite unprecedented letter from 56 former officials.
- **Sue Mi Terry FARA (2024):** CIA/NSC → CFR Senior Fellow → arrested for acting as unregistered South Korean government agent. Illustrates the structural risk of CFR's model: fellows with government access become targets for foreign influence operations.
- **Pentagon cuts ties (2026):** Hegseth ordered military service members barred from CFR programs — the institution that credentialed 21 SecDefs and 15 CIA directors is now deemed insufficiently rigorous by the defense establishment it helped build.
- **Think Tank Funding Tracker transparency score: 3/5** — partial disclosure. Documented $2.4M from Pentagon contractors (Lockheed $250K, Northrop $250K, McKinsey $375K, Booz Allen $250K, Mitsubishi group $490K, etc.) is minimum known amount.
- **Historical policy pipeline:** Kennan's 1947 "X Article" in Foreign Affairs → containment doctrine → Truman Doctrine + Marshall Plan + NATO. Study groups credited with laying the "framework of thinking" for Marshall Plan and NATO. The modern pattern continues with less visible but structurally identical funding-to-research-to-policy cycles.

- Added **`### Policy Area Notes`** section covering CFPB/Financial Deregulation, Energy/Environmental Regulation, and Judicial Nominations — maps each committee seat to its donor service function
- Expanded **Cancer Alley section** with ProPublica EPA chemicals finalization and Inside Climate News 2024 legal challenge coverage
- Added new **Rhetorical Signature Move**: "Woke as regulatory cover" — documents how Kennedy's 2025 CFPB repeal bill used culture war framing to disguise bank deregulation as resistance to identity politics

---

### Key Findings

- Kennedy's CFPB campaign (2023–2025) maps with near-perfect precision to the Finance/Insurance/Real Estate sector's $3.49M investment: cost-benefit legislation → CRA resolution → repeal bill → pay structure attack → floor votes against overdraft and arbitration rules. This is a strong example of the **Donor-Class Override** pattern: the rules Kennedy killed protected consumers from overdraft fee traps and discriminatory lending patterns, directly against constituency interest but in service of financial sector donors.
- The "Woke as regulatory cover" rhetorical move is analytically significant and replicable: converts any CFPB enforcement mechanism into a culture war target, making opposition to consumer protection appear as resistance to identity politics rather than protection of bank profitability. Flag this pattern in future profiles using similar framing.
- Cancer Alley enforcement was blocked twice in 2024: EPA's air pollution rule opposed by Kennedy's office (April 2024), and a federal court partially blocked EPA's environmental justice authority in Louisiana. The judicial pipeline pattern — Kennedy screening nominees hostile to EPA authority — is producing structural results within his Senate tenure.

## Session — Lobbying Firm Builder Automated Run — 2026-03-26 (Thorn Run Partners)

**Task:** lobbying-firm-builder (scheduled automated run)
**Date:** 2026-03-26
**Profile built:** Thorn Run Partners.md
**Queue position:** #9 of 10 (Cornerstone Government Affairs remains)

---

### What was built

**`topics/Lobbying Firms & K Street/Thorn Run Partners.md`** — new file, `draft` status

Full profile of Thorn Run Partners (TRP), a Washington D.C.-based lobbying firm founded 2010. Ranked top 10 by POLITICO Influence and Bloomberg Government.

**Key data points captured:**
- Revenue: $30.05M (2024, 228 clients); $32.28M (2025, 261 clients — partial-year data through Jan 2026)
- 35 lobbyists (2024); 22 revolvers = 62.9% revolving door rate; 0 former Members of Congress
- Founded by Chris Lamond (R, former aide to Sen. Fred Thompson) and Andy Rosenberg (D, former aide to Sen. Ted Kennedy), previously together at Ogilvy Government Relations
- Explicit bipartisan-access-machine model: Lamond handles GOP relationships, Rosenberg handles Democratic relationships

**Top clients documented:**
- ASCAP ($440K) — music licensing/copyright royalties
- Alliance to Save America's 340B Program ($320K) — pharmaceutical discount program defense
- Applied Materials Inc ($280K) — semiconductor manufacturing, CHIPS Act
- American Society/Consultant Pharmacists ($280K) — PBM reform, Medicare Part D
- Anduril Industries ($200K) — defense autonomous systems procurement
- AARP ($160K) — Medicare, prescription drug pricing

**Revolving door hires documented:**
- Greg Lankler: 20+ yrs House incl. 10+ yrs House Appropriations Defense Subcommittee → TRP Nov 2019; now lobbies Anduril, General Atomics, RTX Corp, National Armaments Consortium, Oshkosh Corp
- B.G. Wright: 26 yrs Congressional staff, retired Army Colonel, House Appropriations CJS Subcommittee → TRP Jan 2021
- Catherine Finley: Senate Special Committee on Aging Staff Director → TRP; lobbies AARP, AmeriHealth Caritas, healthcare associations
- Stuart Chapman: Chief of Staff to Rep. Zack Space (D-OH), press secretary in Sen. Rockefeller's office → TRP; lobbies Comcast, T-Mobile, NFL
- Hayden Jewett: former aide to Rep. Roger Williams (R-TX) and Rep. John Culberson (R-TX, Appropriations) → TRP 2025

**Core analytical finding — the Lankler defense machine:**
Lankler spent 10+ years on the House Appropriations Defense Subcommittee — the committee that decides Pentagon contract appropriations. His TRP clients are the exact contractors who need those appropriations: Anduril, General Atomics, RTX Corp. In March 2026, the U.S. Army awarded Anduril a contract worth up to $20B for autonomous systems. TRP has lobbied for Anduril since at least 2022. The correlation between the lobbying relationship and the contract award is documented in the timeline table.

**Analytical patterns flagged:**
- Revolving Door Hub (62.9% rate; Lankler Defense Subcommittee → defense contractors; Finley Senate Aging → healthcare associations)
- Bipartisan Access Machine (engineered through founding structure, Lamond-R + Rosenberg-D)
- Regulatory Shuttle (Lankler carrying Defense Subcommittee knowledge into contractor lobbying work)

**Note on "Democratic-leaning" prior classification:**
The Lobbying Firms Index previously described TRP as "Democratic-leaning, labor/healthcare." This is inaccurate. TRP is explicitly bipartisan by design and has significant Republican-side relationships (Lamond/Thompson, Jewett/Williams-Culberson). The healthcare core does not make it Democratic-leaning. The defense build-out (Lankler/Wright) specifically targets Republican-controlled appropriations relationships. Classification updated in profile accordingly.

**Sources:** 10 total — all 10 VALID, 0 BROKEN. All Chrome-verified via `document.title` check.

---

- Used Format 2 (Donor Node table format) adapted for lobbying firm per Framework doc: `### Lobbying-to-Policy Timeline` with `| Date | Recipient/Target | Amount | Policy Return | Time Gap |` columns
- 8 timeline rows covering 2022–2024 client relationships and documented policy outcomes
- Anduril $20B Army contract (March 2026) explicitly flagged as a documented outcome correlated with TRP's ongoing lobbying relationship
- `content-readiness: draft` — not promoted to `developed` because secondary revolving door hires (Finley's specific Senate committee details, Chapman's full background) documented from press releases/Tier 3 sources only; future session should verify via LegiStorm or LDA LD-1 filings

### Automated Media Profile Builder — March 26, 2026 (media-profile-builder Run 3)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** media-profile-builder

#### What was built

Built `Bill Maher.md` — the 12th media pipeline profile, the 3rd in the Centrist category. Profile status: `draft`.

**Key research findings:**
- **Primary income:** $10M/year from Warner Bros. Discovery (HBO *Real Time*, contract through 2026). Cumulative HBO career income likely exceeds $200M. WBD is the structural funder — no advertiser veto but a corporate parent veto operates through renewal decisions.
- **Net worth: $140M** — derived from HBO salary, stand-up tours (9 HBO specials), New York Mets minority stake (~4%, purchased ~$20M in 2012, tripled to ~$60M when Steve Cohen purchased franchise for $2.4B in 2020), and Beverly Hills real estate.
- **Steve Cohen connection:** Mets investment made Maher a co-minority-owner alongside Steve Cohen (SAC Capital, $1.8B insider trading settlement). Class alignment via sports franchise ownership — personal wealth now tied to finance-sector billionaire ecosystem.
- **Club Random Studios (2024–2025):** Launched March 2024 as "uncancellable" heterodox podcast network; joined Studio71 distribution (ProSiebenSat.1 subsidiary, July 2024); **shut down May 2025** per Semafor reporting. Network housed Sage Steele, Billy Corgan, Shane Smith (Vice) — unified by anti-"woke" positioning. Independence Theater pattern confirmed: the "uncancellable" network was corporate-distributed and shuttered commercially in under 2 years.
- **Political donations:** $1M to Obama Priorities USA Action SuperPAC (2012), $1M to Democratic Senate PAC (2018). Total: $2M — less than 1.5% of net worth. "Liberal donor" credentialing at minimal personal cost.
- **FEC entity:** Bill Maher Productions (OpenSecrets ID: D000071834)
- **Analytical core — Centrist Laundering:** Maher serves the donor class not through direct funding or explicit advocacy, but by anchoring the leftward limit of acceptable liberal thought at a point that excludes anti-corporate class analysis. His structural position ($140M net worth, WBD employment, co-ownership with finance billionaires) generates the content the donor class needs without conspiracy.

**Key patterns documented:**
- **Centrist Laundering** — "I haven't changed, the left has" while earning $10M/year from a media conglomerate
- **Independence Theater** — "Club Random" positioned as uncancellable free speech platform; was a Studio71/ProSieben-distributed corporate product
- **Both-Sides Illusion (Media)** — performs opposition to GOP while systematically attacking the left's mobilizing issues; Fox News regularly amplifies his clips
- **Platform Dependency** — no advertiser veto (HBO subscription model) but corporate parent veto structures the implicit constraints on content

**17 Chrome-verified sources** — all VALID, 0 BROKEN. All logged in Source URL Audit Log.

### Automated Vault Audit — March 26, 2026

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** vault-audit

#### What was built

Generated `topics/Vault Maintenance/2026-03-26 Vault Audit.md` — comprehensive vault audit covering 1,258 files across YAML integrity, content-readiness accuracy, formatting compliance, wikilink integrity, and source verification status.

#### Key findings

- **YAML integrity: 100%.** All 1,258 files have complete, valid YAML frontmatter with all required fields. Zero missing, malformed, or invalid entries. 1 YAML/footer mismatch found (Vault Standards and Agent Instructions.md).
- **57 over-promoted `ready` files** contain `(UNVERIFIED)` or `(URL NEEDED)` tags, violating the Quality Standards rule that no file with these tags can be `ready`. This is the highest-priority integrity issue. Democracy Alliance has 4 tags; CA Farm Bureau, Reed Hastings, COVID No-Bid Contracts, and Josh Gottheimer have 2 each.
- **172 total unverified source tags** across all auditable files — the vault's outstanding verification debt.
- **36 files** in developed/ready status use bold text as section headers instead of `###`. AFL-CIO (7), Defense Contractors Bloc (6), Koch Industries (6), Blue Cross Blue Shield (6) are worst offenders.
- **Wikilink integrity ~98%.** 1 broken wikilink found in sample of 50: `[[UnitedHealth Group - Optum]]` in Connection Map Report.md. Extrapolated: ~265 potentially broken links vault-wide.
- **17 under-promoted files** may deserve status upgrades — notably ALEC Comprehensive Donor Profile Research (627 lines, 26 sources, still `draft`).

| Status | Count | % |
|--------|-------|---|
| ready | 1,157 | 92.0% |
| developed | 65 | 5.2% |
| raw | 21 | 1.7% |
| draft | 15 | 1.2% |

#### Key findings

1. **Daily Wire $0 FEC pattern:** All three Daily Wire personalities in this vault (Shapiro, Owens, Walsh) have confirmed $0 in federal political contributions. The people who produce the most influential conservative political content in America don't donate to the politicians they promote. This is analytically significant — it separates the media product (political advocacy) from personal financial commitment (zero).

2. **The Israel Veto:** The Owens firing is the clearest demonstration of editorial red lines in conservative media. Every other provocation (Hitler comments, conspiracy theories, culture war extremism) is tolerated because it drives engagement. Israel criticism ends careers immediately because it threatens the Wilks Brothers' Christian Zionist theological commitment. This is the class analysis of conservative media editorial limits.

3. **Three models of Daily Wire departure:**
   - Crowder: rejected contract → moved to Rumble (traded Wilks money for Thiel money)
   - Owens: fired → went fully independent (only figure to self-fund successfully)
   - Walsh: stayed loyal → got expanded budgets and theatrical distribution

4. **Bongino FBI discovery:** The Media Pipeline Index placeholder described Bongino as "Fox News host, Rumble investor." Research revealed he served as FBI Deputy Director (Mar 2025 - Jan 2026) — transforming the analytical angle to media-to-government revolving door. The Epstein files clash with AG Bondi adds a connection to the Epstein vault.

5. **Owens self-funding as control case:** Owens is the only right-wing media figure in the vault operating without any billionaire, platform, or institutional financial relationship. Her independent operation ($15-25M annually) shows what conservative media looks like without donor class guardrails: more conspiracy, more antisemitism, more engagement-optimized extremism.

   - **MOST SIGNIFICANT FEC FINDING IN ENTIRE MEDIA PIPELINE**: Kulinski is the only media figure in the entire vault (left or right) whose personal FEC contributions align with positions promoted on air. Every right-wing figure: $0. Every centrist: $0. Kulinski puts money where mouth is.
   - Key patterns: The Funding Asymmetry (left vs. right media), FEC as Integrity Metric, The Institution-Building Gap (co-founded JD then left)
   - Estimated income $500K-$1M — less than Ingraham earns in a single month

2. **David Pakman** (`topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Left/David Pakman.md`) — NEW, draft, 203 lines
   - 6 Chrome-verified sources (FEC, Wikipedia ×3, Yahoo News/NY Post, Wired via Google)
   - Career arc: Buenos Aires immigrant → naturalized citizen age 16 → UMass Amherst (BS) → Bentley (MBA) → WXOJ-LP community radio (Aug 2005) → Pacifica Radio (2006) → YouTube (2009) → Free Speech TV (2010) → TYT Network (2012, later departed) → 3.42M YouTube subscribers / 3.29B views → *The Echo Machine* NYT Bestseller (2025)
   - **FEC: $0** — the 8 results for "david pakman" belong to David B. Pakman, VC at CoinFund/Venrock (different person, confirmed by Wikipedia disambiguation)
   - **CRITICAL FINDING: Chorus Creator Incubator Program** — Sixteen Thirty Fund ($389M dark money 501(c)(4) administered by Arabella Advisors) paying Pakman up to $8K/month to promote Democratic messaging. Revealed by Taylor Lorenz/Wired (Aug 27, 2025), confirmed by Yahoo News/NY Post reporting. 90+ influencers in program.
   - Key patterns: The Dark Money Mirror (left's structural equivalent of Wilks/Daily Wire), Dark Money Contradiction (Pakman criticizes right-wing dark money while receiving left-wing dark money), FEC Gap (Pakman $0 vs Kulinski $5,500)
   - Comparison to Kulinski is the sharpest in the left section: Kulinski unfunded + $5,500 FEC → pushes class politics. Pakman Sixteen Thirty Fund-adjacent + $0 FEC → pushes Democratic Party politics.

**Index updates:**
- `_Media Pipeline Index.md` — Left Phase 2 updated to 5/5 ✅ COMPLETE. Kyle Kulinski and David Pakman entries added with full key angle summaries. Stats updated: **30/30 profiles built, ALL PHASES COMPLETE**. Profile counts: 0 ready / 8 developed / 22 draft / 0 raw / 0 planned.

#### Key findings

1. **Kulinski FEC as control case:** The finding that Kulinski is the ONLY media figure in the entire vault whose personal FEC contributions match on-air positions is the most significant cross-vault analytical discovery in the media pipeline section. It functions as the integrity metric: are you willing to personally finance the politics you promote? Right-wing: universally no ($0 across Shapiro, Owens, Walsh, Ingraham, Bongino — though Bongino gives $106K to Trump/GOP, not to the culture war positions he promotes on air). Centrist: universally no. Left: Kulinski yes ($5,500, proportional to income), Pakman no ($0).

2. **Pakman Chorus/Sixteen Thirty Fund connection:** This is the most analytically significant dark money finding on the left side of the media pipeline. The Sixteen Thirty Fund is the Democratic Party's largest dark money operation ($389M, Arabella Advisors network). Pakman receiving payments through the Chorus Creator Incubator Program creates the structural mirror to right-wing media funding: just as the Wilks Brothers fund Daily Wire to promote conservative messaging, the Sixteen Thirty Fund funds Pakman (and 90+ other influencers) to promote Democratic messaging. The class analysis must be applied symmetrically.

3. **The Kulinski-Pakman contrast maps the left media split:** Unfunded class politics (Kulinski) vs. institutionally backed party politics (Pakman). This mirrors the Democratic Party's own internal tension: Bernie vs. DNC, Justice Democrats vs. DCCC, class analysis vs. culture war liberalism. The funding structures of left media PREDICT the political positions of left media.

4. **Scheduler productivity:** 5 of the 7 remaining profiles had already been built by scheduled tasks running concurrently with manual sessions. This reduced the manual build queue from 7 profiles to 2 (Kulinski, Pakman). The automation pipeline is now a significant force multiplier.

5. **ALL 30 MEDIA PIPELINE PROFILES COMPLETE.** Phase 1 (15/15) and Phase 2 (15/15) are done. The next milestone is promotion passes: 22 drafts need source verification, formatting standardization, and gap-filling to reach developed, then ready.

- Budget: FY2024 revenue $22.7M, expenses $26.4M, net assets $102.9M, total assets $121M (running $3.7M deficit)

- Walter Russell Mead $406K (WSJ "Global View" columnist)

- Healthcare-dominant: 125 health + 111 Medicare/Medicaid reports — the highest healthcare concentration in the cohort

- Consortium model: PMJ core + Ickes & Enright Group (Democratic) + Bay Bridge Strategies (Republican) + TiberCom + Health Strategies
- Founded 1987 as Griffin, Johnson & Associates (Byrd aide + Muskie/Mitchell aide), Bloomberg Govt Top-Performing 2020/2022/2024

## Session — Story Discovery Run 5 (Automated) — 2026-03-26

**Task:** `story-discovery` scheduled task
**Type:** Automated news scan for unreported donor-to-policy connections

### What was built
- Created `topics/Stories/Internal/Daily Updates/2026-03-26 Story Discovery Run 5.md`
- 13 source URLs Chrome-verified and logged in Source URL Audit Log

### Key findings (classified by significance)

**Diamond:**
- **Fed rolls back bank capital rules (March 19)** — Fed/FDIC/OCC propose easing capital requirements for biggest banks by ~5%, after years of Wall Street lobbying against Basel III endgame. Billions freed for buybacks/dividends. Textbook Donor-Class Override.

**Gold:**
- **AIPAC's $22M Illinois primary blitz** — AIPAC/UDP spent ~$22M in IL House Democratic primaries (March 17). AIPAC's super PAC seeded two anonymous shell groups to funnel $14M+ — dark money laundering within Democratic primaries. Mixed results. Dark Money Symmetry pattern.
- **Crypto industry doubles 2024 spending** — $288M into 2026 midterms (vs $130M in 2024). Fairshake ($193M war chest) forced Rep. Al Green (D-TX) into runoff with $1.5M spend. Donor-Class Override case study.
- **Elon Musk's $71M GOP investment** — Top individual donor. $10M to KY Senate candidate (McConnell replacement), $5M each to CLF/SLF, $5M to MAGA Inc. Conflict with DOGE role.

**Silver:**
- **PBM reform passes but pharma profit model untouched** — Genuine Win + Structural Limit pattern. Reform targets middlemen, not manufacturers.
- **DISCLOSE Act reintroduction** — 180+ Dems reintroduce dark money disclosure bill with zero chance of passing. Two-Audience Problem.

**Bronze:**
- 2026 on track for most expensive midterm ever ($10.8B projected). 50 billionaire families: $433M spent. 80% supports GOP.

### Story leads for follow-up
1. Fed capital rollback — map bank PAC donors to congressional supporters
2. AIPAC anonymous shell groups in Illinois — deeper investigation needed
3. Fairshake vs. Al Green runoff tracking
4. Musk DOGE + campaign spending overlap
5. PBM reform 217-214 vote — who voted no and what pharma money did they take?

### Vault maintenance
- 13 URLs Chrome-verified, all logged in Source URL Audit Log
- 3 additional URLs marked UNVERIFIED in story discovery file (Chrome safety filter or time constraints)

#### Key editorial decisions

1. **Downgraded both files from `ready` to `developed`:** Both were incorrectly marked `ready` as thin stubs. Per Quality Standards, a 32-line file with 2-3 sources cannot be `ready`. Honest grading applied — both now have substantive content (107-108 lines, 9 sources each) that meets `developed` criteria but not yet `ready` (would need full citation pass on every claim, all wikilinks verified to resolve, etc.)

2. **Prioritized non-redirect nodes:** All 12 `raw` donor nodes turned out to be redirect stubs pointing to existing canonical profiles. Pivoted to thin `ready` nodes under 60 lines — these represent a larger quality gap (nodes marked as publishable that are actually stubs).

3. **Selected by vault connectivity:** Majority Forward (17 references) and Centene Corporation PAC (14 references) chosen as highest-connectivity non-redirect thin nodes to maximize vault impact.

### Automated Think Tank Builder — March 26, 2026 (think-tank-builder scheduled task)

**Type:** Scheduled task — automated run
**Task:** think-tank-builder (completing Phase 2 — final 3 TBD profiles)

#### What was built

Built **3 new think tank profiles** completing all 25 target profiles across Phase 1 and Phase 2. Selected three strategically significant institutions to fill remaining TBD slots:

1. **Brennan Center for Justice** (Liberal) — `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Liberal/Brennan Center for Justice.md`
   - NYU Law hybrid think tank/litigation shop/advocacy org, founded 1995
   - Soros/Open Society $7.47M (2000-2010), Carnegie $3.65M+, MacArthur $2.71M, Tides $2.75M
   - $57.9M budget (FY2024), $266.6M net assets (8x growth 2019-2024 during Trump era)
   - AVR pioneer — now adopted in 23 states + DC, single most impactful voter registration reform
   - Revolving door: Myrna Pérez → Second Circuit (2021), Vanita Gupta → DOJ #3, Waldman (Clinton speechwriter since 2005)
   - Pattern: "Genuine Win + Structural Limit" — expands who votes, never challenges what they're voting within
   - 12 Chrome-verified sources (ProPublica 990, NBC News, MacArthur, Brennan Center, Wikipedia, Capital Research Center)

2. **Mercatus Center** (Conservative) — `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Conservative/Mercatus Center.md`
   - Koch-founded/funded/governed at George Mason University, founded 1980 by Rich Fink (Koch's chief strategist)
   - Charles Koch himself on the board, plus Richard Fink, Brian Hooks (Koch Stand Together CEO), Edwin Meese (Reagan AG)
   - $39.3M budget (FY2024), $71.9M spike in FY2020 during peak deregulatory implementation
   - RegData → Executive Order 13771 (one-in-two-out) pipeline — Mercatus research became Trump deregulatory policy
   - 2018 FOIA: Koch donors had seats on George Mason faculty selection committees (academic capture documented)
   - Revolving door: Hester Peirce → SEC Commissioner, William Beach → BLS Commissioner, Brian Blasé → White House, John Graham → HHS
   - Pattern: Academic Capture — the clearest example in the vault
   - 12 Chrome-verified sources (ProPublica 990, ProPublica Trump Town, Inside Higher Ed, AAUP, DeSmog, Senate.gov, BLS.gov)

3. **Center for a New American Security** (Centrist) — `topics/Think Tanks & Policy Infrastructure/Centrist/Center for a New American Security.md`
   - Democratic national security personnel holding company, founded 2007 by Flournoy/Campbell
   - #1 recipient of defense contractor money among 50 major think tanks (CIP 2020 study)
   - $6.67M defense contractors (2019-2023), $3.99M U.S. government, $2.81M foreign governments
   - $14.1M budget (FY2024), $37.8M total assets
   - Most aggressive revolving door in the vault: 14+ Obama placements, 16+ Biden placements including:
     - Avril Haines (board → DNI), Kurt Campbell (co-founder → Deputy Sec State), Victoria Nuland (CEO → Under Sec State), Ely Ratner (EVP → Asst Sec Defense), Elizabeth Rosenberg (Program Dir → Asst Sec Treasury)
   - B-21 bomber scandal: recommended 50-75 additional jets ($33-49B) for Northrop Grumman (top donor) without disclosing conflict — calculated ROI 1,390,000%
   - Pattern: Military-Industrial-Think Tank Complex
   - 10 Chrome-verified sources (ProPublica 990, Revolving Door Project, Responsible Statecraft, The Lever, In These Times, Wikipedia, CNAS)

**Phase 3 — Vault Maintenance Updates (API-first integration):**

**Phase 4 — Scheduled Task Updates:**

- `finance-research` — converted to primarily API-driven daily data pulls

#### Files created/updated

New files:
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Centrist/Matt Taibbi.md`
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Left/Jimmy Dore.md`
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/Centrist/Ana Kasparian.md`
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/API Pipeline.md`
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/api-toolkit.js`

Updated files:
- `topics/Media & Influence Pipeline/_Media Pipeline Index.md`
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Quality Standards.md`
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Research Methodology and Data Sources.md`
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Sources Master Node.md`
- `topics/Vault Maintenance/Vault Standards and Agent Instructions.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`

#### Key findings

- **VM proxy blocks direct HTTP** — all API calls must route through Chrome JavaScript context (`fetch()` via `javascript_tool`). Curl returns 403 from `blocked-by-allowlist` proxy.
- **FEC API returns more complete data than web interface** — Taibbi: 7 results via API vs 2 via Chrome with date filter. Missing $4,300+ in Obama 2008 donations. API without date filter is always more complete.
- **CORS domain requirements mapped** — FEC API has open CORS (any page). USASpending and Senate LDA require being on their respective domains.
- **Senate LDA returns Django REST Framework HTML by default** — must use `Accept: application/json` header to get JSON.
- **API data is approximately 10x more token-efficient** than manual Chrome browsing for the same information.

#### Editorial decisions

- **API-first is now the primary research protocol** — all vault maintenance documents updated to reflect this. Manual Chrome browsing is secondary for data available via API.
- **API data is automatically Tier 1** — comes directly from government databases. No verification needed beyond the API response.
- **Media profiles categorized Ana Kasparian as centrist** (not left) — reflects her "unaligned" declaration and dual TYT/Valuetainment employment.
- **Jimmy Dore categorized as left** — despite audience swap, original positioning and content framework remain left-populist.
